Unique identifier for the engineering change notice (ECN) or design change notice. Primary key for the change_notice product.
Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Ensures each change notice is tied to the governing agreement for impact analysis and contractual approval workflow.

Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_target. Business justification: Change notices may modify project carbon reduction targets; linking ensures target updates are tracked in the change management process.
Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Change notices may require amendment of a permit; linking ensures the impacted permit is identified for regulatory follow‑up.

Foreign key linking to subcontractor.contract_agreement. Business justification: Change notices often affect subcontractor contracts; FK enables impact analysis, cost tracking, and schedule adjustments per subcontractor.

Foreign key linking to workforce.craft_worker. Business justification: Change notice originator is typically a site foreman (craft worker) who identifies the need for a design change (Change Management Process).
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Change Notice Cost Impact Report requires linking each notice to the specific cost code impacted for budgeting and variance analysis.
Foreign key linking to material.material_master. Business justification: Change notices often affect material selections; linking to material_master records the impacted material.
Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Change notices are originated by a client contact; linking enables change log reporting and responsibility tracking.
Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Change Notice originator must be linked to an employee for responsibility and impact analysis.

Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Procurement contracts assign each purchased or leased asset to an agreement; required for contract cost reporting and asset ownership tracking.

Foreign key linking to equipment.asset_category. Business justification: Asset belongs to an asset category; replace free‑text category with FK to asset_category for proper hierarchy and eliminate redundancy.

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_calendar. Business justification: Compliance calendars schedule inspections/maintenance for each asset; linking asset to calendar entry provides traceability.

Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Permit issuance for each piece of equipment is required for site operation; linking asset to permit enables permit tracking per equipment.
Identifier of the construction project to which the asset is currently assigned. Null when asset is idle or in yard storage.
Identifier of the construction site or yard where the asset is currently located. Updated during mobilization and demobilization events.
Foreign key linking to compliance.env_impact_assessment. Business justification: Environmental impact assessments often reference the equipment used; asset must point to its assessment record.

Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Equipment Ownership Tracking for Subcontractor‑Provided assets, required for billing, insurance, and compliance reporting on project equipment usage.

business_glossary_term: Fuel Material Material Master Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to material.material_master. Business justification: Associates each asset with its primary fuel material, enabling accurate fuel consumption tracking and environmental compliance.

Foreign key linking to workforce.craft_worker. Business justification: Required for Daily Equipment Operator Assignment report, linking each asset to the assigned craft worker for safety compliance and productivity tracking.

Foreign key linking to compliance.permit_condition. Business justification: Specific permit conditions (e.g., emissions limits) apply to individual equipment; linking enables condition monitoring per asset.

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_change. Business justification: Regulatory changes (e.g., new emission standards) affect specific equipment; asset must reference the change that impacts it.

Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Asset accountability report requires a designated responsible employee for safety and compliance; construction managers assign responsibility per asset.
Total cost to acquire the asset including purchase price, delivery, installation, and initial setup. Basis for depreciation calculations and capital asset reporting.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the acquisition cost (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency fleet management.
Date the asset was acquired by the company through purchase, lease commencement, or rental agreement start. Used for depreciation start date and asset age calculations.

Current operational status of the asset. Determines availability for project assignment and utilization reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|idle|under_maintenance|in_transit|disposed|retired|reserved — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Maximum rated capacity of the equipment in appropriate units (tons for cranes, cubic yards for excavators, kilowatts for generators). Critical for safe operation and project planning.
Unit of measure for the capacity rating. Varies by equipment type and regional standards.

Primary equipment type classification. Determines maintenance requirements, operator certification needs, and utilization tracking methods. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: crane|excavator|concrete_pump|generator|fleet_vehicle|small_tool|bulldozer|loader|grader|compactor|paver|drill_rig — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Timestamp when the asset master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.
Net book value of the asset after accumulated depreciation. Updated periodically based on depreciation schedule and impairment assessments.
Name of the manager or executive who authorized the asset disposal. Required for governance and audit compliance.
Name of the party who purchased or received the disposed asset. Required for audit trail and warranty transfer documentation.
Date the asset was disposed of or removed from active fleet. Triggers final depreciation calculation and asset retirement accounting entries.
Method used to dispose of the asset at end of life. Populated only for disposed assets. Impacts accounting treatment and tax reporting.
Amount received from disposal of the asset through sale, auction, or trade-in. Used to calculate gain or loss on disposal for financial reporting.
Business justification for disposing of the asset (e.g., end of useful life, excessive maintenance costs, fleet optimization, project completion, regulatory non-compliance).
EPA emissions tier classification (e.g., Tier 4 Final, Tier 3). Determines regulatory compliance for site operations and environmental permitting.
Asset identifier in HCSS HeavyJob field operations system. Used for time tracking, production tracking, and equipment hour logging.
Primary equipment yard or depot where the asset is based when not deployed to a project site. Used for logistics planning and idle asset management.
Expiration date of current insurance coverage. Equipment cannot operate without valid insurance.
Policy number for equipment insurance coverage. Required for risk management and claims processing.
Date of the most recent regulatory or safety inspection. Required for OSHA compliance and equipment certification tracking.
Date of the most recent operating hours meter reading. Used to validate meter reading frequency and detect anomalies.
Timestamp of the most recent update to any field in the asset master record. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.
Current stage in the asset lifecycle from acquisition through disposal. Used for lifecycle cost analysis and replacement planning.
Manufacturer or brand name of the equipment (e.g., Caterpillar, Komatsu, Liebherr, Volvo, John Deere).
Manufacturer model designation for the equipment. Used for parts compatibility, operator training, and maintenance procedure lookup.
Date by which the next regulatory inspection must be completed. Equipment cannot operate on site after this date without recertification.
Date of the next planned preventive maintenance service. Calculated based on operating hours, calendar time, or manufacturer recommendations.
Total operating weight of the equipment in kilograms. Used for transportation planning, site access evaluation, and ground bearing pressure calculations.
Classification of how the company controls the asset. Owned assets are capitalized, rented assets are expensed, leased assets follow lease accounting standards.
Regulatory classification determining inspection frequency, operator certification requirements, and safety standards (e.g., OSHA crane certification class, EPA emissions tier).
Equipment master record number in SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance module. System of record identifier for maintenance planning and execution.
Manufacturer-assigned serial number for the equipment unit. Critical for warranty claims, parts ordering, and regulatory compliance.
Externally visible unique identifier physically affixed to the equipment. Used for field identification and tracking across sites.
Cumulative operating hours recorded on the equipment meter. Used for maintenance scheduling, utilization analysis, and residual value estimation.
Calendar year the equipment was manufactured. Used for depreciation calculations, regulatory compliance age limits, and resale value estimation.
Primary key for the AssetActivityAssignment association
Foreign key linking to the schedule activity
Foreign key linking to the equipment asset
Quantity of the asset actually used in the activity
Planned finish date of the asset assignment for the activity
Quantity of the asset planned for the activity
Role of the asset in the activity (e.g., primary, support, auxiliary)
Planned start date of the asset assignment for the activity
Unique identifier for the asset category. Primary key for the asset category reference hierarchy.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Asset category default cost center ensures new assets inherit correct cost center for accounting.
Reference to the parent category in the asset classification hierarchy. Enables multi-level taxonomy (e.g., Heavy Equipment > Earthmoving > Excavators). Null for top-level categories.

Current lifecycle status of the asset category in the master taxonomy: active (in use for classification), inactive (no longer used but retained for historical data), deprecated (being phased out), pending (awaiting approval for activation).
SAP S/4HANA Asset Accounting asset class code for assets in this category. Determines account determination, depreciation keys, and asset master data screen layout in SAP FI-AA module.

Target utilization rate percentage for assets in this category, used for fleet optimization and equipment productivity benchmarking. Represents industry-standard or company target for equipment hours versus available hours.
reference: Equipment Management Best Practices, ISO 55000 Asset Management

description: Minimum acquisition cost threshold for capitalizing assets in this category as fixed assets versus expensing as consumables. Aligns with company capitalization policy and GAAP/IFRS materiality thresholds.
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment, GAAP ASC 360

description: Unique alphanumeric code identifying the asset category. Aligned with HCSS HeavyJob cost coding structure and SAP PM functional location classification. Used for cost allocation, reporting, and system integration.
reference: SAP PM Functional Location Structure, HCSS HeavyJob Cost Coding

description: Detailed description of the asset category scope, typical equipment types included, and business purpose. Provides context for classification decisions and user guidance.

Numeric level in the asset category hierarchy. Level 1 represents top-level categories (e.g., Heavy Equipment), Level 2 represents sub-categories (e.g., Earthmoving), Level 3 represents equipment classes (e.g., Excavators).
Full business name of the asset category (e.g., Heavy Earthmoving Equipment, Lifting and Hoisting, Concrete Placement, MEP Tools, Fleet Vehicles, Small Tools, Generators and Power).
Classification of the category node type within the taxonomy: major (top-level grouping), sub (intermediate grouping), class (equipment class), specialty (niche/specialized equipment).
Timestamp when this asset category record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.

Standard depreciation method applied to owned assets in this category for financial reporting: straight_line (equal annual depreciation), declining_balance (accelerated depreciation), units_of_production (usage-based depreciation for heavy equipment), none (non-depreciable or rental assets).
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment, GAAP ASC 360

description: Date when this asset category was retired or deprecated. Null for currently active categories. Supports historical reporting and taxonomy evolution tracking.
Date when this asset category became active and available for asset classification. Supports temporal validity and historical taxonomy tracking.

Indicates whether assets in this category are subject to environmental compliance tracking (emissions monitoring, fuel consumption reporting, EPA regulations). True for generators, diesel equipment, and vehicles; False for non-powered tools.
reference: EPA Clean Air Act, ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: Default General Ledger account code for asset capitalization and depreciation expense posting in SAP S/4HANA Finance. Aligns with chart of accounts structure for fixed asset accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance GL, IFRS IAS 16, GAAP ASC 360

description: HCSS HeavyJob equipment type classification for field operations time tracking and production reporting. Maps asset category to HeavyJob cost coding and equipment hour tracking structure.

Standard inspection interval in days for assets in this category, aligned with manufacturer recommendations and regulatory requirements. Used to generate preventive maintenance schedules in SAP PM. Null for categories not requiring periodic inspection.
reference: SAP PM Maintenance Planning, ISO 55000 Asset Management, OSHA 29 CFR 1926

description: Indicates whether assets in this category require dedicated insurance coverage beyond general liability. True for high-value equipment (cranes, excavators, specialized machinery); False for low-value consumable tools.
reference: IFRS 4 Insurance Contracts, Risk Management Framework

description: Timestamp when this asset category record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit trail for taxonomy governance.

Default maintenance strategy code assigned to assets in this category, aligned with SAP PM maintenance strategy master data. Defines preventive maintenance frequency, inspection intervals, and service requirements (e.g., HVYEQ for heavy equipment, LTTOOL for light tools).
reference: SAP PM Maintenance Strategy, ISO 55000 Asset Management

description: Indicates whether assets in this category require formal mobilization and demobilization tracking when deployed to project sites. True for heavy equipment requiring transport logistics; False for small portable tools.
User ID or system account that last modified this asset category record. Supports accountability and audit trail for master data governance.

Indicates whether operation of assets in this category requires certified/licensed operators per OSHA regulations and company HSE policy. True for cranes, forklifts, and heavy machinery; False for hand tools and general equipment.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart CC (Cranes), ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety

description: Default ownership model for assets in this category: owned (company-owned assets), rented (short-term rental), leased (long-term lease), mixed (category contains both owned and rented assets). Drives depreciation and cost allocation rules.
Numeric sort order for displaying asset categories in user interfaces and reports. Enables custom sequencing independent of alphabetical or hierarchical order.
Standard useful life in years for assets in this category, used for depreciation calculation and lifecycle planning. Null for rental/leased assets or categories with variable lifespans.
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment, GAAP ASC 360

description: Indicates whether assets in this category require detailed utilization tracking (equipment hours, production tracking) via HCSS HeavyJob or SAP PM. True for high-value equipment requiring cost recovery and productivity analysis; False for low-value consumable tools.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Field Operations, SAP PM Equipment Management

description: Unique identifier for the asset valuation record.
Reference to the equipment asset being valued. Links to the equipment master record.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for posting asset valuation depreciation to the correct cost center in financial statements.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Needed to allocate asset valuation entries to the appropriate GL account for depreciation expense tracking.
Total depreciation expense recognized from acquisition date through the valuation date. Represents the cumulative reduction in asset value due to wear, tear, and obsolescence.
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: Total operating hours logged by the equipment from acquisition through the valuation date. Used for units-of-production depreciation and lifecycle analysis.
Original purchase price or capitalized cost of the equipment asset at the time of acquisition, including delivery, installation, and commissioning costs.
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: Reference number of the external appraisal report document. Used to trace valuation back to supporting documentation.
Name of the professional appraiser or valuation firm that conducted the most recent independent valuation. Null if no external appraisal has been performed.

Financial accounting classification of the asset for reporting and tax purposes (e.g., heavy equipment, vehicles, tools, generators). Aligns with chart of accounts and fixed asset subledger structure.
System timestamp when this valuation record was first created in the system.
Net book value of the asset as of the valuation date, calculated as acquisition cost minus accumulated depreciation. This is the carrying amount on the balance sheet.
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: The accounting method used to calculate periodic depreciation expense. Straight-line allocates cost evenly over useful life, reducing balance applies a fixed percentage to declining book value, units of production bases depreciation on actual usage hours.
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: Annual depreciation rate expressed as a percentage, applicable for reducing balance and other rate-based depreciation methods. Null for straight-line method.
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: Date when the asset was sold, scrapped, or otherwise disposed of. Null if the asset is still in service.
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: The method by which the asset was disposed. Sale indicates sold to third party, trade-in exchanged for new equipment, scrap sold for parts/metal value, donation given to charity, write-off removed with no proceeds.
Actual amount received from the sale or disposal of the asset. Used to calculate gain or loss on disposal. Null if asset has not been disposed.
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: Current estimated market value of the asset if sold in an arms-length transaction. Used for insurance, disposal decisions, and rent-vs-buy analysis. May differ from book value.
Flag indicating whether the asset shows signs of impairment (carrying amount exceeds recoverable amount). True triggers impairment testing per accounting standards.
Amount by which the carrying amount of the asset exceeds its recoverable amount. Recognized as an expense when impairment is identified. Null if no impairment.
The cost to replace the asset with a new equivalent unit at current market prices. Used for insurance coverage determination and renewal valuations.
User ID or name of the person who last modified this valuation record.
System timestamp when this valuation record was last modified.
Scheduled date for the next periodic revaluation or impairment review. Used for fleet management planning and compliance with revaluation policies.
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: Classification of asset ownership. Owned assets are capitalized and depreciated, leased assets may be capitalized under IFRS 16, rented assets are expensed and not valued here.
Estimated remaining economic life of the asset in years from the valuation date. Used for fleet replacement planning and rent-vs-buy decisions.
Estimated salvage or scrap value of the asset at the end of its useful life. The amount expected to be recovered through sale or disposal after full depreciation.
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: The adjustment amount applied during the most recent revaluation. Positive values indicate upward revaluation, negative values indicate impairment. Null if no revaluation has occurred.
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: Date of the most recent revaluation event, if the asset has been revalued to fair market value. Null if no revaluation has occurred since acquisition.
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: Name of the operational system from which this valuation record originated (e.g., SAP FI-AA, Viewpoint Vista, HCSS HeavyJob).
Unique identifier of this valuation record in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.

Depreciation method used for tax reporting purposes, which may differ from book depreciation. MACRS (Modified Accelerated Cost Recovery System) is common in the US, Section 179 allows immediate expensing.
Useful life in years for tax depreciation purposes, as defined by tax regulations. May differ from book useful life.
Expected economic life of the asset in years, used to calculate depreciation. Determined based on manufacturer specifications, industry standards, and organizational experience.
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this valuation record (acquisition cost, book value, depreciation, etc.).
The effective date of this valuation snapshot. Represents the point in time when this valuation is applicable.
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: Free-text field for additional context, assumptions, or special considerations affecting this valuation (e.g., major overhaul capitalized, damage affecting value, market conditions).
Business identifier for the valuation record, typically system-generated or following organizational numbering convention.

The source or method used to determine the current valuation. Acquisition uses original cost, appraisal uses third-party professional valuation, market index uses industry pricing data, insurance quote from carrier, internal estimate by equipment managers.

Current lifecycle status of the valuation record. Draft indicates preliminary valuation, active is current book value, superseded indicates replaced by newer valuation, archived for historical records.
User ID or name of the person who created this valuation record.

Unique identifier for the insurance policy record. Primary key for the insurance policy entity.
Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Equipment insurance policies are procured under contracts; linking allows compliance monitoring and claim tracking per agreement.

Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Insurance policy is issued for a specific asset; linking policy to asset captures ownership and removes need for policy number fields on asset.
The name of the insurance broker or intermediary who arranged the policy on behalf of the construction company. Nullable if policy was purchased directly.
The date when the Certificate of Insurance was issued. Nullable if certificate has not been issued. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Boolean flag indicating whether a Certificate of Insurance has been issued for this policy. COIs are required for equipment mobilization and regulatory compliance.
The email address for submitting claims documentation and correspondence with the claims adjuster.
The name of the claims adjuster or contact person at the insurer for reporting and processing claims under this policy.
The phone number to reach the claims contact for reporting incidents and tracking claim status.
Free-text notes documenting specific regulatory requirements met by this policy, any compliance gaps, or special conditions required by clients or governing bodies.
The maximum monetary amount the insurer will pay for covered losses under this policy. This is the policy limit or sum insured.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the coverage amount and premium (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Detailed description of what assets, risks, and perils are covered under this policy. Includes covered equipment types, geographic scope, and specific inclusions or exclusions.

The category of construction equipment and assets covered by this policy. Heavy equipment includes cranes and excavators, light equipment includes smaller machinery, fleet vehicles includes trucks and cars, tools includes hand and power tools, generators includes power generation equipment, and all equipment covers comprehensive asset types.
The timestamp when this insurance policy record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
The amount the policyholder must pay out-of-pocket before the insurer pays a claim. This is the self-insured retention or excess.
The geographic regions or territories where the insurance coverage is valid. May specify countries, states, or project sites where equipment is covered.
The email address of the insurer contact for policy correspondence, claims submission, and documentation exchange.
The name of the primary contact person or account manager at the insurance company for policy administration and claims.
The primary phone number to reach the insurer contact for policy inquiries, claims reporting, and emergency notifications.
The legal name of the insurance company or underwriter providing the coverage. This is the carrier responsible for claims payment.
The username or identifier of the user who last modified this insurance policy record.
The timestamp when this insurance policy record was last modified in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
Specifies whether this policy covers only company-owned equipment, only rented equipment, or both owned and rented assets.
Reference identifier or file path to the full policy document, terms and conditions, and endorsements stored in the document management system.
The date when the insurance policy coverage expires or terminates. Nullable for open-ended policies. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
General free-text notes about the policy including special terms, exclusions, endorsements, or internal administrative information.
Externally-known unique policy number issued by the insurer. This is the business identifier used in all correspondence and claims.
The date when the insurance policy coverage becomes effective and binding. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.

Current lifecycle status of the insurance policy. Active policies provide coverage, pending policies are awaiting approval, expired policies have passed their end date, cancelled policies were terminated early, suspended policies are temporarily inactive, and renewed policies have been extended.

Classification of the insurance policy coverage type. All-risk plant covers comprehensive equipment damage, third-party liability covers legal claims, fleet motor covers vehicle insurance, marine transit covers equipment in transport, builders risk covers construction projects, and equipment breakdown covers mechanical failure.
The total premium cost paid to the insurer for this policy coverage period. This is the price of the insurance.
The frequency at which premium payments are made to the insurer (annual, semi-annual, quarterly, monthly, or one-time lump sum).
Boolean flag indicating whether this policy meets all regulatory and contractual insurance requirements for equipment operation and mobilization.
The date by which the policyholder must notify the insurer of intent to renew or cancel. Critical for mobilization planning and compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Indicates whether the policy will automatically renew at expiration, requires manual renewal action, is non-renewable, or is currently under renewal review.
The name of the operational system from which this insurance policy record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Viewpoint Vista, HCSS HeavyJob).
The unique identifier of this insurance policy record in the source operational system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.
The username or identifier of the user who created this insurance policy record in the system.
Unique identifier for the equipment mobilization or demobilization event.
Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Mobilization costs are billed to equipment mobilization contracts; the FK ties each mobilization event to its contract agreement.
Reference to the equipment asset being mobilized or demobilized.
Reference to the construction project associated with this mobilization event.
Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Logistics dispatch is performed by a specific employee; tracking supports operational reporting and liability management.
Reference to the subcontractor or vendor responsible for transporting the equipment.
Actual date when the equipment arrived at the destination location.
Actual date when the equipment departed from the origin location.
Actual duration in hours that the equipment was in transit from origin to destination.
Cost center or Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) code to which the mobilization cost is allocated.
Date and time when the mobilization record was first created in the system.
Name or identifier of the location to which the equipment is being mobilized (project site, yard, storage facility).
Standardized code identifying the destination site or facility.
Assessed physical and operational condition of the equipment at the time of dispatch from the origin location.
Detailed notes or observations regarding the equipment condition at dispatch, including any pre-existing damage or defects.
Date and time when the dispatch sign-off was completed.
Total distance in kilometers between the origin and destination locations.
Estimated duration in hours for the equipment to be transported from origin to destination.
Type of equipment movement event: mobilization (to site), demobilization (from site), inter-site transfer, or relocation.
Indicates whether the equipment is covered by insurance during the mobilization transport.
Policy number of the insurance coverage applicable to this mobilization event.
Date and time when the mobilization record was last updated in the system.
Externally-known business identifier for the mobilization event, typically formatted as MOB-YYYYNNNN.
Current lifecycle status of the mobilization event in the workflow.
Name or identifier of the location from which the equipment is being mobilized (yard, warehouse, previous site).
Standardized code identifying the origin site or facility.
Official permit number(s) issued by the governing authority for this transport.
Indicates whether special permits (oversize load, road permits, environmental permits) are required for this mobilization.
Type of permit(s) required for the transport, such as oversize load permit, road use permit, or environmental permit. Multiple permits may be listed.
Scheduled date when the equipment is expected to arrive at the destination location.
Scheduled date when the equipment is planned to depart from the origin location.
Business justification or reason for the mobilization event, such as project start, equipment replacement, or maintenance requirement.
Assessed physical and operational condition of the equipment upon receipt at the destination location.
Detailed notes or observations regarding the equipment condition at receipt, including any damage incurred during transport.
Date and time when the receipt sign-off was completed.
Name of the site manager or authorized personnel who signed off on the equipment receipt at the destination.
Additional comments, notes, or special instructions related to the mobilization event.
Total cost incurred for transporting the equipment, including freight, permits, and handling charges.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transport cost.
Method or mode of transportation used to move the equipment.
Unique identifier for the fleet assignment record. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Equipment lease/assignment to a project is governed by a contract; linking records the contract that authorizes each assignment.
Reference to the specific equipment or fleet vehicle being assigned. Links to the equipment master data.
Reference to the construction project to which the equipment is assigned.
Reference to the operator or driver responsible for operating the equipment during this assignment.
Reference to the rental company or vendor supplying the equipment, if applicable. Null for owned equipment.
Reference to the specific site or work location where the equipment is deployed.
Reference to the WBS element for cost allocation and project accounting. Used for internal charge-back to projects.
Reference to the specific work front or activity area within the site where the equipment is operating.
Actual cumulative equipment operating hours recorded during this assignment. Updated from daily logs and equipment hour meters.
The date when the equipment assignment ends or is planned to end. Nullable for open-ended assignments. Marks the end of cost allocation for this assignment.
Free-text notes and comments related to this equipment assignment. Captures special instructions, constraints, or operational considerations.
Business identifier for the fleet assignment. Externally visible reference number used in mobilization documents and site logistics planning.
Business priority level of this equipment assignment. Used for resource allocation decisions and conflict resolution when equipment is in high demand.
Business description of the purpose or scope of work for this equipment assignment. Describes the specific activities or tasks the equipment will perform.
The date when the equipment assignment begins. Marks the start of cost allocation and utilization tracking for this assignment.

Current lifecycle status of the equipment assignment. Tracks progression from planning through mobilization, active use, and demobilization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|mobilizing|active|standby|demobilizing|completed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Classification of the equipment ownership or procurement method for this assignment. Determines applicable cost rates and billing rules.
Cost center or cost allocation code for internal charge-back and financial reporting. Used to route equipment costs to the appropriate project budget line.
Timestamp when this assignment record was first created in the source system. Audit trail for record creation.
Internal daily cost rate for equipment assignment. Alternative to hourly rates for equipment charged on a daily basis.
Actual or estimated cost of demobilizing the equipment from the site, including transportation, cleaning, and inspection. One-time cost allocated to the project.
The date when the equipment was demobilized from the site. Actual date of equipment removal and return to fleet pool or rental company.
Cumulative hours the equipment was on site but not actively operating during this assignment. Used for standby cost allocation and utilization analysis.
Insurance policy number covering the equipment during this assignment. Required for high-value equipment and rental assets.
Actual or estimated cost of mobilizing the equipment to the site, including transportation, permits, and setup. One-time cost allocated to the project.
The date when the equipment was physically mobilized to the site. Actual date of equipment arrival and readiness for work.
Current physical mobilization status of the equipment. Tracks whether equipment is in transit, on site, or has been demobilized.
Username or identifier of the user who last modified this assignment record in the source system.
Timestamp when this assignment record was last modified in the source system. Audit trail for record updates.
Internal hourly operating cost rate including fuel, maintenance, and operator costs. Used for project cost allocation when equipment is actively operating.
Internal hourly ownership cost rate for owned equipment. Used for project cost allocation and internal charge-back calculations.
Regulatory permit or authorization number required for operating this equipment on the site. Applicable for cranes, heavy lifts, and specialized machinery.
Planned or estimated total equipment operating hours for this assignment. Used for capacity planning and utilization forecasting.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all rate amounts in this assignment record.
External rental contract or purchase order number for rented equipment. Used for vendor billing reconciliation.
Identifier of the operational system of record from which this assignment record was sourced. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.
Unique identifier of this assignment record in the source system. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and incremental updates.
Internal hourly standby cost rate when equipment is on site but not actively operating. Lower rate than operating rate, used for idle time cost allocation.
Internal weekly cost rate for equipment assignment. Alternative to hourly or daily rates for equipment charged on a weekly basis.
Username or identifier of the user who created this assignment record in the source system.

Internal rating of fuel quality at this point.

Indicates whether this is the primary fuel point for its site or fleet.

Type of vendor evaluation being conducted. Pre-qualification for new vendors entering AVL (Approved Vendor List), periodic review for ongoing performance assessment, project-specific for bid eligibility, re-qualification for expired certifications, or incident-triggered for corrective action follow-up.

Numerical score (0-100) assessing the vendor's financial stability based on credit rating, liquidity ratios, debt-to-equity, and financial statement analysis. Critical for pre-qualification and bonding capacity assessment.

business_glossary_term: Health Safety and Environment (HSE) Incident Count
description: Total number of HSE (Health Safety and Environment) incidents (including LTI, near-misses, and safety violations) involving the vendor during the evaluation period.

business_glossary_term: Health Safety and Environment (HSE) Rating Score
description: Numerical score (0-100) assessing the vendor's HSE (Health Safety and Environment) performance including TRIR (Total Recordable Incident Rate), LTI (Lost Time Injury) frequency, safety certifications (ISO 45001), and HSE management system maturity.
Boolean indicator (True/False) confirming that the vendor's general liability, professional indemnity, and workers' compensation insurance policies have been verified and meet project requirements.
Timestamp when the vendor evaluation record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.
Total number of NCR (Non-Conformance Report) incidents raised against the vendor during the evaluation period. Lower counts indicate better quality performance.
Percentage (0-100) of deliveries completed on or before the scheduled delivery date during the evaluation period. Key KPI for schedule performance and reliability.

Composite numerical score (0-100) representing the weighted average of all evaluation dimensions (financial, technical, HSE, quality, delivery, responsiveness, commercial). Determines overall vendor performance grade.

Letter grade (A through F) assigned based on overall KPI rating. A=Excellent (90-100), B=Good (80-89), C=Satisfactory (70-79), D=Marginal (60-69), F=Unsatisfactory (<60). Drives vendor tier classification and bid invitation priority.
Date when the vendor's qualification status expires and requires renewal through re-evaluation. Typically set 12-24 months from evaluation date for qualified vendors.
Resulting qualification status of the vendor based on evaluation outcome. Determines vendor eligibility for bid invitations and PO (Purchase Order) issuance.
Percentage (0-100) of delivered materials or services that passed quality inspection on first submission without NCR (Non-Conformance Report) during the evaluation period.

Numerical score (0-100) evaluating the vendor's quality management certifications (ISO 9001), QA/QC (Quality Assurance/Quality Control) processes, inspection capabilities, and adherence to ITP (Inspection and Test Plan) requirements.

Numerical score (0-100) assessing the vendor's responsiveness to RFI (Request for Information), RFQ (Request for Quotation), change orders, and issue resolution. Measures communication effectiveness and turnaround time.

Operational system from which the evaluation data was sourced. Procore for project-specific evaluations, SAP MM for vendor master and procurement data, Intelex for HSE performance data, or manual for offline assessments.
Numerical score (0-100) evaluating the vendor's technical competence, equipment availability, workforce skills, past project experience, and ability to meet technical specifications.
Unique identifier for the vendor invoice record. Primary key.
Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Ensures invoice verification against contract terms, supporting audit of payments versus agreed contract values.
Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Invoices for equipment purchases/rentals must link to the asset to close the financial‑asset loop for accounting and warranty tracking.
Foreign key linking to design.change_notice. Business justification: Invoice may be for work resulting from a change notice; linking tracks cost impact of design changes.
Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: Vendor invoices are billed to a specific client account; FK supports payment processing and client‑level invoice reconciliation.
Reference to the construction project to which this invoice is allocated. Used for project cost tracking and job costing.
Reference to the cost center to which this invoice is charged for financial accounting and cost control purposes.
Reference to the employee or user who approved this invoice for payment.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Vendor invoices need a cost‑code reference to map spend to project cost structures and support earned‑value analysis.
Reference to the general ledger account to which this invoice is posted in the financial accounting system.
Reference to the purchase order against which this invoice was received. Used for three-way match validation (PO-GR-Invoice).
Reference to the vendor who issued this invoice. Links to vendor master data.
The date on which the invoice was approved for payment by the authorized approver.
Reason why the invoice is blocked from payment, such as pending approval, missing documentation, price variance, or quality hold.
Timestamp when this invoice record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which the invoice is denominated.
Total discount amount applied to the invoice, including early payment discounts, volume discounts, or negotiated rebates.
Boolean indicator of whether this invoice is currently under dispute due to discrepancies in quantity, quality, pricing, or terms.
Detailed explanation of the reason for the invoice dispute, such as quantity mismatch, pricing error, quality issues, or missing documentation.
The SAP FI document number generated when the invoice is posted to the financial accounting system. This is the system-generated accounting document reference.
The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year in which this invoice is recorded.
The fiscal year in which this invoice is recorded for financial reporting purposes.
The goods receipt document number confirming that materials or services were received. Used in three-way match validation.
The date the vendor issued the invoice. This is the principal business event timestamp for the invoice transaction.
Textual description of the goods or services covered by this invoice, providing context for the transaction.
Total invoice amount before taxes and adjustments. Represents the base value of goods or services invoiced.
Total invoice amount payable after applying taxes, discounts, and adjustments. This is the final amount due to the vendor.
The externally-issued invoice number provided by the vendor. This is the vendor's unique identifier for the invoice document.
The date the invoice was received by the procurement or accounts payable department.

Current lifecycle status of the vendor invoice in the verification and payment workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|received|under_review|blocked|approved|posted|paid|disputed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Classification of the invoice document type indicating the nature of the transaction (standard invoice, credit memo for returns, debit memo for additional charges, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|credit_memo|debit_memo|prepayment|final|progress|retention_release — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Timestamp when this invoice record was last modified or updated.
Additional free-text notes or comments related to this invoice, such as special instructions, clarifications, or internal remarks.
The actual date on which payment was made to the vendor for this invoice.
The date by which payment must be made to the vendor to comply with payment terms and avoid late payment penalties.
The method by which payment will be made to the vendor for this invoice.
Reference number of the payment transaction when the invoice is paid, linking the invoice to the payment record.
The agreed payment terms for this invoice, such as Net 30, Net 60, or 2/10 Net 30, defining the payment schedule and any early payment discount conditions.
Amount withheld from the invoice payment as retention per contract terms, typically released upon project completion or after the Defects Liability Period (DLP).
Percentage of the invoice amount withheld as retention, typically ranging from 5% to 10% per construction contract terms.
Total tax amount applied to the invoice, including sales tax, VAT, GST, or other applicable taxes.
The tax code applied to this invoice, defining the tax rate and tax type (VAT, GST, sales tax) per jurisdiction.
Result of the three-way match validation comparing purchase order, goods receipt (GR), and invoice. Indicates whether quantities, prices, and terms align across all three documents.
reference: SAP MM Invoice Verification Three-Way Match

description: Status of the invoice verification process indicating whether the invoice has been validated against the purchase order and goods receipt.
Amount of withholding tax deducted from the invoice payment per tax regulations, to be remitted to tax authorities.
Unique identifier for the vendor qualification record. Primary key for the vendor qualification entity.
Reference to the procurement or QA/QC (Quality Assurance/Quality Control) employee who conducted the qualification assessment.
Reference to the vendor master record being qualified. Links to the vendor entity in the procurement domain.
Date when the vendor qualification was formally approved and the vendor was added to the AVL (Approved Vendor List).

Comma-separated list of material categories the vendor is qualified to supply. Examples include concrete, steel, MEP (Mechanical Electrical and Plumbing) equipment, formwork, aggregates. Aligns with MTO (Material Take-Off) classification.

Comma-separated list of service types the vendor is qualified to provide. Examples include earthworks, structural steel erection, electrical installation, HVAC installation, concrete placement, surveying.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for bonding capacity limit. Typically USD for US-based projects.

Maximum bonding capacity available from the vendor's surety company. Determines the maximum contract value the vendor can undertake. Critical for GC (General Contractor) and major subcontractor qualifications.
Timestamp when the vendor qualification record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for compliance and governance.

Composite financial health assessment score ranging from 0.00 to 100.00. Based on credit rating, liquidity ratios, debt-to-equity, and financial statement analysis. Minimum threshold typically 60.00 for approval.
Geographic regions or countries where the vendor is qualified to operate. Comma-separated list of ISO 3166 country codes or region names. Important for multi-site and international projects.

business_glossary_term: Health Safety and Environment (HSE) Performance Rating
description: HSE (Health Safety and Environment) performance rating based on TRIR (Total Recordable Incident Rate), LTI (Lost Time Injury) frequency, safety audit results, and compliance with OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration) standards. Critical factor for vendor approval.
Expiry date of the vendor's insurance certificate. Procurement tracks this to ensure continuous coverage throughout contract execution.
General liability insurance coverage limit. Minimum requirements typically range from $1M to $10M depending on project size and risk profile.
Indicates whether valid workers compensation insurance has been verified. Mandatory for all vendors providing on-site labor per OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration) requirements.
Certificate number for ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification.

Indicates whether the vendor holds valid ISO 14001 Environmental Management System certification. Increasingly required for LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) projects and environmentally sensitive sites.
Expiry date of ISO 14001 certification. Critical for projects with EPA (Environmental Protection Agency) compliance requirements.
Certificate number for ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System certification.

business_glossary_term: ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Certified
description: Indicates whether the vendor holds valid ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management System certification. Demonstrates commitment to worker safety and HSE (Health Safety and Environment) excellence.
Expiry date of ISO 45001 certification. Monitored to ensure ongoing HSE (Health Safety and Environment) compliance.
Certificate number for ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification. Used for verification with certification body.
Indicates whether the vendor holds valid ISO 9001 Quality Management System certification. Often mandatory for critical material suppliers and major subcontractors.
Expiry date of ISO 9001 certification. Procurement monitors this to ensure vendors maintain valid certification throughout contract execution.
Timestamp when the vendor qualification record was last updated. Tracks changes to qualification status, scores, certifications, or other attributes.
LTI (Lost Time Injury) frequency rate calculated as (number of LTIs × 1,000,000) / total hours worked. Measures severity of safety incidents. Target is typically below 1.0 for qualified vendors.
Free-text notes capturing additional qualification details, special conditions, limitations, or observations from the assessment process. Used by procurement team for decision support.
Percentage of past deliveries completed on or before scheduled date. Critical metric for CPM (Critical Path Method) schedule-sensitive projects. Target is typically above 90.00%.

Composite past performance score ranging from 0.00 to 100.00. Based on historical project delivery, quality, schedule adherence, cost control, and client satisfaction. Derived from completed project evaluations and NCR (Non-Conformance Report) history.
Date when the qualification assessment was conducted. Includes site visits, document reviews, financial analysis, and technical capability evaluation.
Primary category of goods or services the vendor is qualified to supply. MEP (Mechanical Electrical and Plumbing) is a specialized category for building systems contractors.
Date on which the vendor qualification expires and requires renewal. Typically set 1-3 years from start date depending on vendor category and risk profile.
Business identifier for the vendor qualification record. Externally visible unique reference number used in procurement communications and AVL (Approved Vendor List) documentation.
Effective date from which the vendor qualification becomes valid. Vendors can only receive PO (Purchase Order) assignments on or after this date.

Current lifecycle status of the vendor qualification. Approved vendors are eligible for PO (Purchase Order) issuance. Suspended vendors require re-qualification. Expired qualifications require renewal.

Type of qualification process being conducted. Initial for new vendors, renewal for periodic re-assessment, re-qualification for vendors returning after suspension, conditional for limited-scope approval, emergency for urgent project needs.

Historical quality defect rate expressed as percentage of deliverables requiring rework or NCR (Non-Conformance Report) issuance. Lower values indicate better quality performance. Target is typically below 5.00%.

Detailed explanation if the vendor qualification was rejected. Includes specific deficiencies in financial health, technical capability, HSE (Health Safety and Environment) performance, or documentation.
Date when the vendor qualification was suspended. Suspended vendors cannot receive new PO (Purchase Order) assignments until re-qualified.
Reason for suspending an approved vendor qualification. Common causes include safety incidents, quality failures, financial distress, contract breaches, or regulatory violations.

Assessment score of vendor technical capabilities ranging from 0.00 to 100.00. Evaluates equipment, workforce skills, past project complexity, and technical certifications. Minimum threshold typically 70.00 for complex EPC (Engineering Procurement and Construction) projects.

TRIR (Total Recordable Incident Rate) calculated as (number of recordable incidents × 200,000) / total hours worked. Industry benchmark for construction is typically below 3.0. Lower values indicate better safety performance.
Unique identifier for the vendor quotation record. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the procurement or engineering professional who evaluated this quotation. Supports audit trail and accountability.

Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Vendor quotations submitted by subcontractors require association with subcontractor firm for evaluation, audit, and bonding verification.
Foreign key linking to procurement.material_catalog. Business justification: Link vendor quotation material to master catalog for normalization and remove redundant material_id column.
Reference to the RFQ document that this quotation responds to. Links the vendor response back to the procurement request.
Reference to the vendor master record submitting this quotation. Identifies the supplier providing the quote.
Number of supporting documents attached to the quotation (technical datasheets, certifications, test reports, etc.).
Procurement team recommendation on whether to award the PO to this vendor based on evaluation results.
Vendor-declared exceptions or deviations from RFQ commercial terms (payment, delivery, penalties, etc.). Must be evaluated during bid tabulation.
Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the material is manufactured or sourced. Relevant for customs, duties, and local content requirements.
System timestamp when the quotation record was first created in the procurement system. Audit trail for record creation.
Three-letter ISO currency code for the quoted prices (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, AED). Critical for multi-currency bid comparison.
Number of calendar days from PO issuance to material delivery at site. Critical for project scheduling and CPM integration.
Incoterms delivery terms defining responsibility transfer point (e.g., EXW, FOB, CIF, DDP). Impacts freight cost allocation and risk.
Vendor-declared exceptions, deviations, or clarifications to the RFQ technical specifications. Critical for commercial evaluation and risk assessment.
Percentage discount offered by vendor from list price or previous quotation. Used for price negotiation and historical benchmarking.
Date when the quotation evaluation was completed and recommendation finalized.
Free-text notes and comments from the evaluation team regarding technical compliance, commercial terms, or vendor performance considerations.
Composite evaluation score assigned during bid tabulation based on price, technical compliance, delivery, and vendor performance. Used for award recommendation.
Quoted freight or transportation cost if not included in unit price. Depends on delivery terms (Incoterms).
System timestamp when the quotation record was last updated. Tracks changes during evaluation and negotiation cycles.
Vendor-provided description of the material or service being quoted. May include brand, model, or specification details.
Vendor-offered payment terms (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 50% advance + 50% on delivery, LC at sight). Impacts cash flow and working capital.
Vendor-assigned unique reference number for this quotation. External business identifier used for correspondence and tracking.
Current lifecycle status of the vendor quotation in the evaluation and award process.
Quantity of material or service the vendor is quoting for. Must align with RFQ requested quantity.
Date and time when the vendor submitted the quotation response. Critical for evaluating timeliness and compliance with RFQ deadlines.
Applicable tax amount (VAT, GST, sales tax) on the quoted price. May vary by jurisdiction and project location.
Assessment of whether the quoted material meets the technical specifications defined in the RFQ. Evaluated by engineering team.
Total quoted price for the full quantity (unit price × quantity). May include or exclude taxes and freight depending on quotation terms.
Unit of measure for the quoted quantity (e.g., EA, M, KG, TON, M3, HR). Must match material master UOM or include conversion factor.
Vendor-quoted price per unit of measure. Base price before taxes, freight, or other charges.
Date until which the vendor commits to honor the quoted price and terms. Critical for award decision timing.
Date from which the quoted price and terms become valid. Typically the quotation submission date.
Duration of warranty coverage offered by the vendor in months. Aligns with DLP (Defects Liability Period) requirements.
Detailed warranty terms and conditions including coverage scope, exclusions, and claim procedures.
Unique surrogate identifier for the project cost account (control account) record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Project Systems; Viewpoint Vista Job Costing

description: Reference to the parent construction project to which this cost account belongs. Links cost account to the project master record for project-level cost roll-up and reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Project Systems (PS Module); Oracle Primavera P6

description: Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Cost account manager employee is required for financial accountability and reporting.

Reference to the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element at which this cost account is positioned. The cost account represents the intersection of a WBS node and a cost code, enabling granular budget control per WBS deliverable.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS WBS Element; Oracle Primavera P6 WBS; PMI PMBOK WBS Standard

description: Human-readable description of the cost account scope, such as 'Structural Concrete — Foundation Works' or 'MEP Subcontract — Electrical Rough-In'. Used in cost reports, BOQ reconciliation, and EVM reporting.
reference: Viewpoint Vista Job Costing; SAP S/4HANA PS; AACE International

description: Current lifecycle status of the cost account. Active accounts accept cost postings; on_hold accounts are temporarily frozen pending review; closed accounts are complete and locked; cancelled accounts are voided; pending_approval accounts await PMO authorization before activation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS Account Status; PMO Governance Framework; IFRS 15

description: Total actual cost incurred and posted to this cost account to date, including paid invoices, posted labor timesheets, and equipment charges. Equivalent to Actual Cost of Work Performed (ACWP) in EVM. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO and Viewpoint Vista job cost ledger.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-CO Actual Costs; Viewpoint Vista Job Costing; PMI PMBOK EVM; IFRS 15

description: Current approved budget for this cost account inclusive of all approved Change Orders (COs). Equivalent to Budget at Completion (BAC) in EVM terminology. Updated each time a CO is approved and posted to the cost account. The primary budget control figure used by project cost controllers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS Approved Budget; AACE International; PMI PMBOK EVM; IFRS 15

description: Planned completion date for the scope of work associated with this cost account as established in the approved project baseline schedule. Used for schedule performance tracking and Earned Value Management (EVM) analysis.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Schedule; PMI PMBOK; SAP S/4HANA PS

description: Planned start date for the scope of work associated with this cost account as established in the approved project baseline schedule. Used for Planned Value (PV) calculation and schedule variance analysis in EVM reporting.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Schedule; PMI PMBOK; SAP S/4HANA PS

description: Budgeted cost per unit of measure for this cost account (Approved Budget divided by Budgeted Quantity). Used for unit rate benchmarking, bid analysis, and productivity monitoring. Expressed in the cost account currency per unit of measure.
reference: AACE International; Viewpoint Vista Job Costing; HCSS HeavyJob

description: Cumulative value of all approved Change Orders (COs) posted to this cost account. Represents the delta between the original budget and the current approved budget. Tracked separately to support contract administration and CO impact analysis.
reference: Procore Change Orders; SAP S/4HANA PS; FIDIC Contract Conditions; Contract Administration

description: Total value of contractual commitments against this cost account, including issued Purchase Orders (POs) and subcontract awards not yet invoiced. Represents financial obligations that will become actual costs. Critical for cash flow forecasting and cost-to-complete analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchase Orders; Viewpoint Vista Committed Cost; AACE International

description: Contingency budget allocated to this cost account to cover identified risks and uncertainties within the defined scope. Managed separately from the base budget and released by the project manager upon risk materialization. Supports risk-adjusted cost forecasting.
reference: AACE International Risk Analysis; PMI PMBOK Risk Management; SAP S/4HANA PS

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the cost account within the project. Derived from the job cost coding structure in Viewpoint Vista and SAP PS. Used on timesheets, purchase orders, and subcontract invoices for cost allocation.
reference: Viewpoint Vista Job Costing; SAP S/4HANA Cost Element; AACE International Cost Engineering

description: SAP Cost Center code associated with this cost account, representing the organizational unit responsible for the costs. Used for overhead allocation, departmental P&L reporting, and internal charge-back processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center Accounting; IFRS/GAAP

description: Standardized cost code from the company's master cost code library, representing the work activity or resource category (e.g., '03300' for cast-in-place concrete, '16000' for electrical). Used for cross-project benchmarking and historical cost analysis. Aligns with CSI MasterFormat or company-specific coding structure.
reference: CSI MasterFormat; HCSS HeavyJob Cost Coding; Viewpoint Vista Cost Code

description: Estimated remaining cost required to complete the scope of work for this cost account. Calculated as Forecast Cost at Completion minus Actual Cost to Date. Used by cost controllers to project future cash requirements and update project financial forecasts.
reference: PMI PMBOK EVM; AACE International; SAP S/4HANA PS

description: Primary classification of the cost account by resource type: labor (direct workforce), material (permanent and consumable materials), equipment (plant and machinery), subcontract (third-party subcontractor scope), or overhead (indirect project costs). Drives cost segregation in EVM and P&L reporting.
reference: AACE International Recommended Practice 10S-90; SAP S/4HANA Cost Element Category; Viewpoint Vista Cost Type

description: Variance between the approved budget and the forecast cost at completion for this cost account (Approved Budget minus Forecast Cost at Completion). A negative value indicates a cost overrun; positive indicates an underspend. Key metric for project cost control reporting and PMO governance.
reference: PMI PMBOK EVM Cost Variance; AACE International; SAP S/4HANA PS Variance

description: Earned Value Management (EVM) Cost Performance Index for this cost account, calculated as Earned Value divided by Actual Cost (EV/AC). A CPI below 1.0 indicates cost overrun; above 1.0 indicates cost efficiency. Stored at the cost account level to support drill-down analysis from project-level CPI reporting.
reference: PMI PMBOK EVM; AACE International; SAP S/4HANA PS EVM

description: Timestamp when this cost account record was first created in the source system or ingested into the Silver Layer. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; Data Governance Framework; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this cost account (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, AUD). Supports multi-currency project reporting for international EPC and JV projects.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: Budgeted cost of work performed (BCWP) for this cost account, representing the value of completed work expressed in budget terms. Core EVM metric used to calculate Cost Performance Index (CPI) and Schedule Performance Index (SPI) at the cost account level.
reference: PMI PMBOK EVM; AACE International; SAP S/4HANA PS EVM

description: Project cost controller's current best estimate of the total cost to complete this cost account, including actual costs to date plus estimated cost to complete (ETC). Equivalent to Estimate at Completion (EAC) in EVM. Updated periodically during project cost reviews.
reference: AACE International Recommended Practice; PMI PMBOK EVM EAC; SAP S/4HANA PS Forecast

description: Current forecast completion date for the scope of work associated with this cost account. Updated during project schedule reviews. Used to assess Extension of Time (EOT) impacts and project completion forecasting.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6; SAP S/4HANA PS; FIDIC EOT Provisions

description: Current forecast start date for the scope of work associated with this cost account, updated during project schedule reviews. Compared against the baseline start date to identify schedule slippage and its cost impact.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6; SAP S/4HANA PS; PMI PMBOK

description: SAP General Ledger (GL) account code to which costs posted against this cost account are mapped for financial reporting. Ensures alignment between project cost control and the corporate chart of accounts. Required for IFRS/GAAP financial statement preparation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI General Ledger; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: Indicates whether this cost account is priced on a lump sum basis (True) rather than a unit rate or reimbursable basis (False). Lump sum accounts are managed differently in terms of progress measurement and payment certification.
reference: FIDIC Contract Conditions; Contract Administration; Viewpoint Vista

description: Indicates whether the scope of work for this cost account is executed by a subcontractor (True) or by the General Contractor's (GC) own forces (False). Drives subcontract management workflows, retention tracking, and payment certification processes.
reference: Procore Subcontract Management; SAP S/4HANA MM; FIDIC Subcontract Conditions

description: The baseline budget amount for this cost account as established at project sanction or Notice to Proceed (NTP). Represents the original authorized cost plan before any Change Orders (COs). Immutable once the baseline is locked. Used as the denominator for variance analysis and EVM performance measurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS Budget; AACE International; PMI PMBOK Earned Value Management; IFRS 15

description: Physical percent complete of the scope of work associated with this cost account, as assessed by the project team or quantity surveyor. Expressed as a value between 0.00 and 100.00. Used to calculate Earned Value and assess progress against the baseline schedule.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Activity Percent Complete; SAP S/4HANA PS; PMI PMBOK

description: Project phase or stage code associated with this cost account (e.g., 'DESIGN', 'PROCURE', 'CONSTRUCT', 'COMMISSION'). Enables cost tracking and reporting by EPC phase. Sourced from Viewpoint Vista and HCSS HeavyJob phase coding.
reference: Viewpoint Vista Phase Code; HCSS HeavyJob; EPC Project Delivery Framework

description: Budgeted cost of work scheduled (BCWS) for this cost account as of the reporting data date. Represents the cumulative planned spend per the project baseline schedule. Used with Earned Value to compute Schedule Variance (SV) and SPI.
reference: PMI PMBOK EVM; AACE International; Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline

description: Actual quantity of work completed and posted against this cost account to date. Compared against budgeted quantity to assess production efficiency and unit cost performance. Sourced from HCSS HeavyJob production entries and field daily logs.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Production Tracking; Viewpoint Vista; Procore Daily Logs

description: Original budgeted quantity of work for this cost account as derived from the Bill of Quantities (BOQ) or Material Take-Off (MTO). Expressed in the unit of measure applicable to the cost type (e.g., m3 for concrete, tonnes for steel, hours for labor). Enables unit rate analysis and productivity benchmarking.
reference: BOQ (Bill of Quantities); MTO (Material Take-Off); HCSS HeavyJob Production Tracking; Viewpoint Vista

description: The data date or cut-off date as of which the cost account financial figures (actual cost, earned value, planned value, forecast) are reported. Aligns with the project monthly cost report cycle and EVM data date in Oracle Primavera P6.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Data Date; SAP S/4HANA PS Reporting Period; PMI PMBOK EVM

description: Contractual retention percentage applied to payments against this cost account, expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 for 5%). Retention is withheld from subcontractor and supplier payments until practical completion or Defects Liability Period (DLP) expiry.
reference: FIDIC Contract Conditions; Contract Administration; Viewpoint Vista AP

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this cost account record was sourced: SAP S/4HANA Project Systems, Viewpoint Vista Job Costing, HCSS HeavyJob, Oracle Primavera P6, or manually entered. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Data Governance Framework; Databricks Lakehouse Architecture

description: Earned Value Management (EVM) Schedule Performance Index for this cost account, calculated as Earned Value divided by Planned Value (EV/PV). An SPI below 1.0 indicates schedule slippage on the associated scope; above 1.0 indicates ahead of schedule. Supports integrated cost-schedule performance analysis.
reference: PMI PMBOK EVM; AACE International; Oracle Primavera P6

description: Unit of measure for the budgeted and actual quantities on this cost account (e.g., HR for hours, M3 for cubic metres, TON for tonnes, LM for linear metres, EA for each, LS for lump sum). Aligns with the BOQ and MTO unit conventions.
reference: ISO 80000 Quantities and Units; BOQ Standard; HCSS HeavyJob

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this cost account record, whether from a source system refresh, cost controller update, or Change Order posting. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; Data Governance Framework; GDPR Article 5

business_glossary_term: Earned Value Management (EVM) Period Record ID
description: Unique surrogate identifier for each EVM period record. Primary key for the evm_period_record data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: PMO Governance Standards; ANSI/EIA-748 Earned Value Management

description: Reference to the parent construction project for which this EVM period record is captured. Links to the project master entity.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Project Systems; SAP S/4HANA Project Systems (PS)

description: Reference to the fiscal or project reporting period (e.g., monthly cut) for which this EVM record applies. Enables period-over-period trend analysis.
reference: PMO Governance Standards; SAP S/4HANA Fiscal Period

description: Reference to the specific WBS element at which this EVM measurement is recorded. Supports both project-level and WBS-level EVM reporting.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 WBS; SAP S/4HANA PS WBS Element; ANSI/EIA-748

business_glossary_term: Actual Cost of Work Performed (ACWP) / Actual Cost (AC)
description: The cumulative actual cost incurred for work performed as of the data date. Also known as Actual Cost (AC). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Project Systems actual postings and Viewpoint Vista job costing.
reference: ANSI/EIA-748; SAP S/4HANA PS Actual Costs; Viewpoint Vista Job Costing

description: The name or user identifier of the PMO or project controls manager who approved this EVM period record for official reporting. Required for audit trail and progress billing certification.
reference: PMO Governance Standards; ISO 9001 QA/QC; Aconex Workflow Approvals

description: The date and time at which this EVM period record was formally approved by the PMO approver. Establishes the official record for audit and compliance purposes.
reference: PMO Governance Standards; Aconex Workflow Approvals; ISO 9001

description: The approved baseline scheduled completion date for this WBS element or project as established at NTP (Notice to Proceed) or last approved baseline revision. Used to calculate schedule variance in time terms.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Finish; FIDIC Contract Administration; NTP

description: Integer ordering of the phase within the project lifecycle. Determines the execution sequence for scheduling, phase-gate progression, and earned value rollup. Lower numbers execute first.

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this phase record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.

description: Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) code assigned to this phase within the project hierarchy. Aligns the phase to the SAP PS WBS element and Oracle Primavera P6 WBS node for cost and schedule integration.

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each approved project baseline record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the baseline data product.

description: Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Baseline changes must be approved by a specific employee for audit trails and compliance.

description: Reference to the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element at which this baseline budget and schedule snapshot is captured. Enables cost-account-level baseline tracking per EVM methodology.

description: Calendar date on which the baseline was formally approved by the designated approving authority. Marks the effective start of the approved baseline for EVM measurement and PMO reporting.

description: Governance tier at which this baseline was approved, reflecting the authorization hierarchy. Higher-value or re-baseline revisions typically require elevated approval levels per PMO governance policy.

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the baseline record, used in PMO reporting, EVM analysis, and audit documentation. Format: BL-{ProjectCode}-{SequenceNumber}.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Management; PMO Governance Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the baseline record within the PMO approval workflow. Draft baselines are under preparation; pending_approval awaits sign-off; approved is the active authorized baseline; superseded indicates a newer revision has replaced it; cancelled denotes a withdrawn baseline.
reference: PMO Governance Standards; Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Management

description: Classification of the baseline record indicating the nature of the snapshot: original (initial approved baseline), revised (minor scope/cost adjustment), re_baseline (full project re-baselining), co_incorporation (Change Order incorporated), re_forecast (updated forecast adopted as new baseline), management_reserve_release (management reserve funds released into budget).
