description: Total approved budget amount immediately following this baseline revision. Together with budget_before_revision, provides the complete budget delta audit trail for PMO governance and financial audit requirements.
reference: PMO Governance Standards; SAP S/4HANA Project Systems; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting

description: Total approved budget for the project or WBS element as captured in this baseline snapshot, representing the Budget at Completion (BAC) in the EVM framework. This is the total planned cost against which CPI (Cost Performance Index) and cost variance are measured.

description: Total approved budget amount immediately prior to this baseline revision. Enables audit trail tracking of budget evolution and quantification of the budget delta introduced by each revision.
reference: PMO Governance Standards; SAP S/4HANA Project Systems; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting

description: Total monetary value of Change Orders (COs) incorporated into this baseline revision. Quantifies the cumulative scope change impact on the approved budget for audit trail and contract administration purposes.

description: Approved contingency reserve budget included within this baseline, allocated to cover identified project risks and uncertainties. Distinct from management reserve which is held outside the project baseline.
reference: PMI PMBOK Risk Management; SAP S/4HANA Project Systems; PMO Governance Standards

description: Total authorized contract value associated with this baseline, as agreed with the client or owner. Used to reconcile the project budget against the contracted revenue and to track budget-to-contract alignment.
reference: FIDIC Contract Administration; SAP S/4HANA Project Systems; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting

description: Cost account or cost code identifier at which this baseline budget is allocated, enabling WBS-level budget breakdown and cost-account-level EVM variance analysis. Aligns with the project cost coding structure in SAP S/4HANA and Viewpoint Vista.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Project Systems; Viewpoint Vista Job Costing; EVM (Earned Value Management) Guidelines

description: Timestamp when this baseline record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary amounts in this baseline record are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency project financial reporting for international EPC projects.

description: Construction project delivery model under which this baseline was established. Determines the contractual framework and approval requirements: EPC (Engineering Procurement Construction), DB (Design-Build), DBB (Design-Bid-Build), PPP (Public-Private Partnership), BOT (Build-Operate-Transfer), GMP (Guaranteed Maximum Price).
reference: FIDIC (International Federation of Consulting Engineers); PMO Governance Standards

description: Approved total duration of the project or WBS element in calendar days as captured in this baseline snapshot. Derived from baseline start and finish dates; stored explicitly for EVM schedule variance analysis and reporting.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Management; EVM (Earned Value Management) Guidelines

description: Date on which this baseline was superseded by a subsequent revision or closed. Null if this is the currently active approved baseline. Supports audit trail and historical EVM analysis.
reference: EVM (Earned Value Management) Guidelines; PMO Governance Standards

description: Date from which this approved baseline becomes the active reference for EVM variance analysis and project performance measurement. Typically aligns with approval_date or the NTP (Notice to Proceed) date for original baselines.
reference: EVM (Earned Value Management) Guidelines; PMI PMBOK; Oracle Primavera P6

description: Number of calendar days of Extension of Time (EOT) granted and incorporated into this baseline revision. Tracks the cumulative schedule relief authorized through contract administration, impacting the baseline finish date.
reference: FIDIC Contract Administration; PMO Governance Standards; Procore Change Orders

description: Approved planned finish date for the project or WBS element as captured in this baseline snapshot. Used as the schedule reference for SPI (Schedule Performance Index) calculation and milestone tracking.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Management; CPM (Critical Path Method); EVM Guidelines

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the client or owner has formally approved this baseline revision. Relevant for contract-driven baselines under FIDIC, GMP (Guaranteed Maximum Price), or EPC delivery models where client sign-off is contractually required.
reference: FIDIC Contract Administration; PMO Governance Standards; Procore Change Orders

description: Boolean flag indicating whether this record represents the currently active approved baseline for the project or WBS element. Only one baseline per project/WBS should have this flag set to True at any point in time.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Management; EVM (Earned Value Management) Guidelines

description: Management reserve budget held outside the project baseline for unknown unknowns, as authorized by senior management. Tracked separately from contingency reserve per EVM methodology. Populated when baseline_type is management_reserve_release.
reference: EVM (Earned Value Management) Guidelines; PMI PMBOK; PMO Governance Standards

description: Free-text field for additional PMO or project controls notes, assumptions, constraints, or caveats associated with this baseline record. Supplements the formal revision justification narrative.
reference: PMO Governance Standards; Oracle Primavera P6

description: Planned Value (PV) — the authorized budget assigned to scheduled work in this baseline snapshot, also known as Budgeted Cost of Work Scheduled (BCWS) in EVM methodology. Serves as the EVM performance measurement baseline reference.
reference: EVM (Earned Value Management) Guidelines; PMI PMBOK; Oracle Primavera P6

description: Source system identifier for this baseline record as assigned by Oracle Primavera P6. Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and the operational scheduling system.
Calendar date on which the baseline revision was formally prepared and submitted for approval. Represents the point-in-time snapshot date for scope, schedule, and cost parameters.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Management; PMO Governance Standards

description: Narrative explanation of the business reason for this baseline revision, including the drivers of scope, schedule, or cost changes. Required for PMO governance approval and financial audit documentation.
reference: PMO Governance Standards; FIDIC Contract Administration; SAP S/4HANA Project Systems

description: Sequential integer indicating the revision iteration of the baseline for a given project or WBS element. Revision 0 denotes the original approved baseline; subsequent revisions increment by 1.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Management; PMO Change Control Process

description: SAP S/4HANA Project Systems version identifier corresponding to this baseline snapshot. Used for reconciliation between the ERP project budget version and the Primavera schedule baseline.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Project Systems (PS); PMO Governance Standards

description: The data date (status date) of the Oracle Primavera P6 schedule from which this baseline snapshot was extracted. Defines the point-in-time reference for all schedule parameters captured in this record.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Management; CPM (Critical Path Method)

description: Narrative description of the approved project scope captured in this baseline snapshot. Documents the authorized scope boundaries, key deliverables, and inclusions/exclusions at the time of baseline approval.
reference: PMI PMBOK Scope Management; Oracle Primavera P6; PMO Governance Standards

description: Approved planned start date for the project or WBS element as captured in this baseline snapshot. Used as the schedule reference for SPI (Schedule Performance Index) calculation and CPM (Critical Path Method) analysis.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Management; CPM (Critical Path Method); EVM Guidelines

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this baseline record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, audit trail, and data lineage requirements.
reference: GDPR/Privacy Regulations; PMO Governance Standards; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Variance at Completion (VAC) — the difference between the Budget at Completion (BAC) and the Estimate at Completion (EAC) at the time this baseline was established. Provides a forward-looking cost performance indicator for PMO reporting.
reference: EVM (Earned Value Management) Guidelines; PMI PMBOK

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each project milestone record in the Construction lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for the project_milestone data product.
Reference to the parent construction project to which this milestone belongs. Links the milestone to the core project master record.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Project Scheduling; SAP S/4HANA Project Systems

description: Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Milestone owner contact needed for client notifications and acceptance sign‑off per Milestone Management process.
Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Milestone owner employee is needed for performance tracking and escalation procedures.
Reference to the immediately preceding milestone in the project schedule logic chain. Supports Critical Path Method (CPM) analysis and schedule dependency tracking within the milestone network.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Activity Planning; CPM (Critical Path Method) Scheduling

description: The Activity ID of the corresponding milestone activity in Oracle Primavera P6, the system of record for project scheduling. Enables direct traceability between the lakehouse Silver layer record and the source scheduling system activity.

description: Reference to the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element under which this milestone is classified. Enables milestone tracking at the WBS decomposition level within the project schedule.

description: Formal criteria that must be satisfied for the milestone to be considered achieved and accepted by the client or engineer. May reference ITP (Inspection and Test Plan) requirements, regulatory sign-offs, or contractual deliverable submissions.

description: The date on which the milestone was formally achieved and accepted. Populated only upon milestone completion and sign-off. Used for schedule variance calculation, contract administration, and LD (Liquidated Damages) trigger assessment.

description: Approved contract baseline date for the milestone, which may differ from the original planned date if a formal baseline revision or approved EOT (Extension of Time) has been incorporated. Used as the contractual reference for LD (Liquidated Damages) exposure calculation.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Management; FIDIC Clause 8; PMO Baseline Control

description: Percentage of work completed toward achieving this milestone, expressed as a value between 0.00 and 100.00. Updated during progress reporting cycles. Used for EVM (Earned Value Management) percent-complete calculations and schedule performance tracking.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Progress Tracking; EVM ANSI/EIA-748; Procore Construction Management

description: Timestamp when this milestone record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for data governance and lineage tracking in the Databricks lakehouse Silver layer.
reference: GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; Data Governance Standards

description: The project delivery model under which this milestone is defined and tracked. Determines the contractual framework and milestone obligation structure. EPC = Engineering Procurement Construction; DB = Design-Build; DBB = Design-Bid-Build; PPP = Public-Private Partnership; BOT = Build-Operate-Transfer; GMP = Guaranteed Maximum Price.
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract; PMO Governance Framework

description: Total number of calendar days of approved Extension of Time (EOT) granted for this milestone under the contract. Reflects cumulative approved EOT claims that have adjusted the baseline date. Zero if no EOT has been granted.

description: Reference number of the approved EOT (Extension of Time) claim or contract amendment that adjusted the baseline date for this milestone. Used for contract administration audit trail and dispute resolution.
reference: FIDIC Clause 8.4 Extension of Time; Aconex Document Management; Contract Administration

description: Current best estimate of the date on which the milestone will be achieved, updated during schedule reviews and progress reporting cycles. Reflects schedule recovery plans, approved EOT (Extension of Time) claims, and current site conditions.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Progress Tracking; EVM (Earned Value Management) Schedule Forecasting

business_glossary_term: Health Safety Environment (HSE) Clearance Required Flag
description: Indicates whether HSE (Health, Safety and Environment) regulatory clearance or permit is a prerequisite for achieving this milestone (e.g., commissioning milestones requiring OSHA or EPA sign-off). True = HSE clearance must be obtained before milestone can be marked complete.
reference: OSHA Occupational Safety and Health Administration; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; EPA Environmental Protection Agency

description: Indicates whether this milestone is a contractually obligated key date as defined in the project contract (FIDIC, GMP, or bespoke agreement). True = contractual obligation with potential LD (Liquidated Damages) or payment consequences; False = internal PMO or programme milestone.
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract; Contract Administration Standards

description: Indicates whether this milestone lies on the Critical Path Method (CPM) critical path of the project schedule. True = any delay to this milestone directly delays the project completion date; False = milestone has float and is not on the critical path.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 CPM Scheduling; PMI PMBOK Schedule Management

description: Indicates whether missing this milestone triggers Liquidated Damages (LD) as specified in the contract. True = LD clause applies upon delay; False = no LD exposure for this milestone. Critical for contract risk management and financial exposure reporting.
reference: FIDIC Clause 8.7 Delay Damages; Contract Administration; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting

description: Indicates whether achievement of this milestone triggers a contractual payment event or progress payment certificate under the contract payment schedule. True = milestone completion initiates a payment claim or invoice; False = no direct payment linkage.
reference: FIDIC Clause 14 Contract Price and Payment; SAP S/4HANA Accounts Receivable

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the Liquidated Damages (LD) daily rate (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, AED). Ensures correct financial exposure reporting in multi-currency international EPC projects.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: The contractually agreed daily monetary rate of Liquidated Damages (LD) applicable if this milestone is not achieved by the baseline date. Expressed in the project contract currency. Populated only when is_ld_trigger = True. Sensitive commercial data.
reference: FIDIC Clause 8.7 Delay Damages; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: Indicates whether this milestone is associated with LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) certification requirements or green building compliance deliverables. True = milestone contributes to LEED certification pathway.

description: Functional category grouping the milestone within the EPC (Engineering, Procurement, and Construction) project lifecycle phase. Supports schedule performance analysis by phase and PMO reporting. reference: EPC Project Lifecycle Framework; Oracle Primavera P6 Project Scheduling

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the milestone within the project schedule, as assigned in Oracle Primavera P6 (e.g., MS-NTP001, MS-MC001). Used for cross-system referencing and contract administration.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Project Scheduling; FIDIC Contract Administration

description: Human-readable name of the milestone describing the key project event or deliverable (e.g., 'Notice to Proceed (NTP)', 'Mechanical Completion', 'Substantial Completion', 'Final Handover'). Used in schedule reports and contract documents.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Project Scheduling; FIDIC Conditions of Contract

description: Current lifecycle state of the milestone tracking its progress toward completion. 'not_started' = work not yet begun; 'in_progress' = activities underway; 'completed' = milestone achieved; 'overdue' = past planned date without completion; 'waived' = formally waived by contract amendment; 'deferred' = postponed with approved EOT (Extension of Time).
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Progress Tracking; FIDIC Clause 8 Extension of Time

description: Classification of the milestone by its origin and obligation level. 'contractual' = bound by contract terms (FIDIC/GMP); 'internal' = PMO governance milestone; 'regulatory' = required by OSHA/EPA/IBC; 'client' = client-driven key date; 'financial' = triggers payment or LD exposure.
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract; PMO Governance Framework; OSHA Regulatory Requirements

description: Number of calendar days in advance of the milestone date that formal notification must be issued to the client or engineer, as required by the contract. Applicable when notification_required = True. Supports contract compliance monitoring.
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract; Contract Administration Standards

description: Indicates whether formal written notification to the client or engineer is contractually required prior to or upon achievement of this milestone. True = notification obligation exists per contract; False = no formal notification required.
reference: FIDIC Clause 8 Commencement, Delays and Suspension; Contract Administration

description: Contractual payment amount due upon achievement of this milestone, expressed in the project contract currency. Applicable only when is_payment_trigger = True. Used for cash flow forecasting and contract financial management.
reference: FIDIC Clause 14 Contract Price and Payment; IFRS/GAAP Revenue Recognition; SAP S/4HANA Finance

description: Original baseline date on which the milestone was scheduled to be achieved, as established at project kick-off or NTP (Notice to Proceed). Serves as the schedule baseline reference for variance analysis and SPI (Schedule Performance Index) calculation.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Baseline Management; FIDIC Conditions of Contract

description: The corresponding milestone or schedule item identifier in Procore Construction Management platform. Supports cross-system reconciliation between the scheduling system of record (Primavera P6) and the construction management platform (Procore).
reference: Procore Construction Management; Data Integration Standards

description: Detailed narrative description of the milestone, including the scope of work, acceptance criteria, and deliverables required to formally achieve the milestone. Sourced from the contract schedule or project execution plan.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Project Scheduling; FIDIC Conditions of Contract

description: Free-text field for project team notes, status commentary, risk flags, or contextual information related to the milestone. Used during progress review meetings and PMO reporting to capture qualitative schedule narrative.
reference: PMO Governance Framework; Oracle Primavera P6 Project Scheduling

description: The contracting party primarily responsible for achieving this milestone. Distinguishes between the General Contractor (GC), client, subcontractor, engineer/designer, Joint Venture (JV) partner, or regulatory authority. Critical for contract administration and risk allocation.
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract; Contract Administration Standards

description: Number of calendar days by which the milestone is ahead of (negative) or behind (positive) the baseline date. Calculated as forecast_date minus baseline_date for open milestones, or actual_date minus baseline_date for completed milestones. Key input for SPI (Schedule Performance Index) reporting.
reference: EVM (Earned Value Management) ANSI/EIA-748; Oracle Primavera P6 Schedule Analysis

description: Reference number of the formal sign-off document, completion certificate, or taking-over certificate issued upon milestone achievement (e.g., Aconex document number, Procore submittal reference). Provides audit trail for contract administration and handover documentation.
reference: FIDIC Clause 10 Taking Over Certificate; Aconex Document Management; Procore Construction Management

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this milestone record was ingested into the Databricks lakehouse Silver layer. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and master data management across the construction technology stack.
reference: Data Governance Standards; Databricks Lakehouse Architecture

description: Total schedule float in calendar days available for this milestone before it impacts the project completion date, as calculated by the CPM (Critical Path Method) schedule engine in Oracle Primavera P6. Zero or negative float indicates critical or super-critical status.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 CPM Scheduling; ANSI/EIA-748 EVM Standard

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this milestone record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change detection, incremental data loading, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks lakehouse Silver layer.
reference: GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity; ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; Data Governance Standards

description: Expiry date of the team member's medical fitness certificate required for site deployment. Monitored to ensure ongoing compliance with OSHA and ISO 45001 occupational health requirements.
reference: OSHA – Medical Fitness; ISO 45001 – Worker Health; Intelex – HSE Compliance

description: Actual date the team member physically mobilized to the project site or commenced active project duties. May differ from assignment start date if there is a pre-mobilization preparation period.
reference: FIDIC – Mobilization; Procore – Daily Log; HCSS HeavyJob – Mobilization Tracking

description: Current mobilization status of the team member, tracking the physical deployment lifecycle from pre-mobilization through on-site presence to demobilization. Distinct from assignment_status which tracks the contractual assignment lifecycle.
reference: FIDIC – Mobilization; Procore – Site Management; PMO Mobilization Tracking

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the team member's nationality. Required for visa and work permit management, local content compliance reporting, and workforce diversity tracking.
reference: ISO 3166-1 – Country Codes; GDPR Article 9 – Special Category Data; Local Content Regulations

description: Date of the NTP (Notice to Proceed) relevant to this team member's assignment commencement. Used to validate mobilization compliance with contractual NTP obligations.
reference: FIDIC – Notice to Proceed; EPC Contract Administration; PMO Governance

description: Total planned man-days budgeted for this team member's assignment on the project. Used for resource planning, schedule development, and baseline establishment in Oracle Primavera P6.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – Budgeted Units; PMI PMBOK – Resource Planning; EVM – ANSI/EIA-748

description: Primary professional certification or licence held by the team member relevant to their project role (e.g., PE – Professional Engineer, PMP – Project Management Professional, NEBOSH, LEED AP, CQE). Used for competency verification and regulatory compliance.
reference: ASCE – Professional Engineer Licensure; PMI – PMP Certification; OSHA – Competency Requirements; ISO 9001 – Competence

description: Internal professional grade or seniority level of the team member as defined in the company's grading structure (e.g., G1–G10, Senior, Principal, Director). Confidential HR data used for cost rate assignment and succession planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product per HR grading framework]
reference: SAP SuccessFactors – Job Grade; Internal HR Grading Policy

description: Free-text field for PMO or project administration notes regarding this team member assignment, such as reasons for early demobilization, scope of role changes, or special contractual conditions.
reference: PMO Governance Standards; Procore – Project Notes

description: Functional category grouping the project role for workforce planning, reporting, and analytics. Enables aggregation of team composition by discipline across the project portfolio. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: management|engineering|hse|commercial|planning|quality|commissioning|support — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – Resource Category; SAP SuccessFactors – Job Classification

description: Formal project role title assigned to the team member on this project (e.g., Project Director, Project Manager, Construction Manager, Site Manager, Resident Engineer, HSE Manager, QA/QC Manager, Cost Engineer, Planner, Commissioning Manager, Interface Manager). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Project Director|Project Manager|Construction Manager|Site Manager|Resident Engineer|HSE Manager|QA/QC Manager|Cost Engineer|Planner|Commissioning Manager|Interface Manager|MEP Manager|Procurement Manager|Design Manager — promote to reference product]
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – Resource Role; FIDIC – Project Organization; PMO Role Framework

description: Work rotation schedule applicable to this team member's assignment (e.g., 28 days on / 28 days off for remote sites, 5/2 for office-based roles). Drives accommodation planning, travel logistics, and per diem calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 28/28|21/21|14/14|6/2|5/2|residential|non_rotational — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FIDIC – Personnel Conditions; SAP SuccessFactors – Work Schedule; HSE Fatigue Management Guidelines

description: Code identifying the specific site, zone, or work front within the project where the team member is primarily deployed (e.g., SITE-A, ZONE-3, CAMP-NORTH). Supports site operations reporting and HSE zone management.
reference: Procore – Site Management; HCSS HeavyJob – Cost Coding; ISO 45001 – Work Location

description: Operational system of record from which this team member assignment record was sourced (e.g., Oracle Primavera P6, SAP SuccessFactors, Procore). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Data Governance – Data Lineage; Databricks Lakehouse – Silver Layer Standards

description: Native identifier of this team member assignment record in the originating operational system (e.g., Primavera P6 resource assignment ID, SAP SuccessFactors assignment ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse and source systems.
reference: Data Governance – Data Lineage; Oracle Primavera P6 – Resource Assignment; SAP SuccessFactors

description: Timestamp when this team member assignment record was last modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and data quality monitoring in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 9001 – Record Control; GDPR Article 5 – Data Integrity; Databricks Silver Layer Audit Standards

description: Classification of the team member's primary work location type for this assignment. Drives per diem entitlements, accommodation planning, and HSE induction requirements.
reference: FIDIC – Site Presence Requirements; ISO 45001 – Workplace Classification; SAP SuccessFactors – Work Location

description: Expiry date of the team member's work permit or visa for the project host country. Monitored to prevent illegal working and ensure continuous regulatory compliance.
reference: Local Immigration Regulations; ISO 45001 – Legal Compliance; OSHA – Worker Authorization

description: Government-issued work permit or visa reference number authorizing the team member to work in the project's host country. Required for regulatory compliance and immigration audits.
reference: GDPR Article 4 – Personal Data; Local Immigration Regulations; ISO 45001 – Legal Compliance

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the WBS element record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.
reference: PMI PMBOK Guide – WBS Standard; Oracle Primavera P6 WBS Object

description: Foreign key linking to material.warehouse. Business justification: Supports Site Staging Plan linking each WBS element to its dedicated staging warehouse.

Foreign key linking to design.bim_model. Business justification: BIM coordination process requires each WBS element to reference its BIM model for clash detection and model federation; experts expect this link.
Reference to the parent construction project to which this WBS element belongs. Links the WBS node to its project context for EVM rollup and PMO governance.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – Project Object; SAP PS Project Definition

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Required for cost‑coding WBS elements in EVM and budget reports; experts assign a cost code to each WBS element for financial tracking.
Foreign key linking to material.material_master. Business justification: Needed for WBS Cost Estimation to identify the primary material type for each work package.

business_glossary_term: Parent Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) Element ID
description: Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediate parent WBS node, enabling hierarchical decomposition of project scope. Null for root-level WBS elements.
reference: PMI Practice Standard for Work Breakdown Structures; Oracle Primavera P6 WBS Hierarchy

description: Foreign key linking to hr.org_unit. Business justification: Required for cost allocation reports linking WBS responsibility to org unit managing the work.

Foreign key linking to schedule.wbs_node. Business justification: Required for integrated Earned Value reporting aligning cost WBS with schedule hierarchy; project managers need to map WBS elements to schedule nodes.
Total actual costs incurred to date for this WBS element, sourced from SAP S/4HANA job costing and Viewpoint Vista. Used as the ACWP input in EVM calculations.
reference: PMI Practice Standard for Earned Value Management; SAP PS – Actual Costs; IFRS 15

description: Date on which work on this WBS element was physically completed, as recorded in Oracle Primavera P6. Null if work is still in progress. Used for schedule performance reporting and DLP (Defects Liability Period) trigger.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – Actual Finish; PMI PMBOK – Schedule Control; FIDIC Clause 11

description: Actual quantity of work completed and installed to date for this WBS element, as recorded in HCSS HeavyJob field production tracking. Compared against planned_quantity for production performance analysis.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob – Production Tracking; AACE RP 10S-90

description: Date on which work on this WBS element actually commenced on site, as recorded in Oracle Primavera P6 or Procore daily logs. Used to calculate schedule variance and Extension of Time (EOT) claims.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – Actual Start; Procore Daily Log; FIDIC Clause 8

description: Cumulative value of approved Change Orders (CO) that have modified the original budget for this WBS element. Reconciles original_budget_cost to budgeted_cost.
reference: FIDIC Clause 13 – Variations; Procore Change Order Management; AACE RP 10S-90

description: Reference number or code linking this WBS element to the corresponding Bill of Quantities (BOQ) line item in the contract. Enables traceability between scope decomposition and contract pricing.
reference: FIDIC – Bill of Quantities; NRM2 (RICS New Rules of Measurement); ASCE Quantity Surveying

description: Total approved budget allocated to this WBS element, representing the Budget at Completion (BAC) for EVM purposes. Expressed in the project currency. Forms the baseline for CPI and SPI calculations.
reference: AACE International – Earned Value Management; PMI Practice Standard for EVM; IFRS 15 – Revenue from Contracts

description: Classification of costs associated with this WBS element: direct (billable to project scope), indirect (overhead allocation), overhead, contingency (risk reserve), or provisional (BOQ provisional sum). Affects cost reporting and contract billing.
reference: AACE International RP 10S-90; FIDIC – Provisional Sums; SAP PS Cost Classification

description: Cost account or cost code assigned to this WBS element for job costing and financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA and Viewpoint Vista. Maps WBS scope to the chart of accounts for P&L reporting.
reference: SAP PS – Cost Element; Viewpoint Vista – Job Cost Code; AACE RP 10S-90

description: Timestamp when this WBS element record was first created in the source system or lakehouse. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
reference: ISO 8601 – Date and Time Formats; GDPR Article 5 – Data Integrity and Accountability

description: CSI MasterFormat division code classifying the type of construction work for this WBS element (e.g., 03 = Concrete, 05 = Metals, 26 = Electrical). Enables industry-standard scope classification and benchmarking.
reference: CSI MasterFormat – Construction Specifications Institute; IBC – International Building Code

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values on this WBS element (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, AED). Supports multi-currency EPC projects and financial consolidation.
reference: ISO 4217 – Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: Contract delivery model under which this WBS element is executed: EPC (Engineering Procurement Construction), DB (Design-Build), DBB (Design-Bid-Build), PPP (Public-Private Partnership), BOT (Build-Operate-Transfer), or GMP (Guaranteed Maximum Price). Affects cost control and contract administration rules.
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract; PMI PMBOK – Project Procurement Management

business_glossary_term: Earned Value (EV) – Budgeted Cost of Work Performed (BCWP)
description: Budgeted cost of work actually performed to date for this WBS element (BCWP). Calculated as percent_complete multiplied by budgeted_cost. Core EVM metric used to derive CPI and SPI at the WBS level.
reference: PMI Practice Standard for Earned Value Management; AACE International RP 11R-88; Oracle Primavera P6 – Earned Value

description: Flag indicating whether this WBS element is included in the project's EVM (Earned Value Management) performance measurement baseline. Summary and planning package nodes may be excluded from direct EVM measurement.
reference: PMI Practice Standard for Earned Value Management; AACE RP 11R-88; Oracle Primavera P6 – EVM Settings

description: Current forecast completion date for this WBS element based on progress to date and remaining work, as updated in Oracle Primavera P6. Used for schedule recovery planning and LD exposure monitoring.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – Early Finish (Forecast); PMI PMBOK – Schedule Forecasting; FIDIC Clause 8

description: Flag indicating whether this WBS element is priced as a lump sum (fixed price) rather than a re-measurable unit-rate item. Affects billing, change order valuation, and BOQ reconciliation.
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract – Lump Sum vs. Re-measurable; AACE RP 10S-90

description: Flag indicating whether this WBS element represents a contractual or project milestone (e.g., NTP, substantial completion, handover). Milestone WBS elements trigger payment certificates and schedule reporting.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – Milestone Flag; FIDIC Clause 14 – Payment Milestones

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this WBS element record, either in the source system or during lakehouse processing. Supports incremental data loading and change detection.
reference: ISO 8601 – Date and Time Formats; GDPR Article 5 – Data Integrity and Accountability

description: The initial approved budget for this WBS element at project baseline, before any approved change orders or budget transfers. Enables variance analysis between original and current budget.
reference: AACE International Recommended Practice No. 10S-90 – Cost Engineering Terminology; PMI PMBOK – Cost Baseline

description: Current physical percent complete of the WBS element, representing the proportion of scope delivered to date. Used as the primary EVM progress measurement input. Range: 0.00 to 100.00.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – Physical % Complete; PMI Practice Standard for EVM; AACE RP 11R-88

description: Method used to calculate percent complete for EVM purposes: physical (field measurement), units_complete (quantity-based), duration (time-proportional), weighted_milestone, level_of_effort, or fixed_formula (e.g., 0/100, 50/50). Configured in Oracle Primavera P6.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – % Complete Type; PMI Practice Standard for EVM; AACE RP 11R-88

description: Baseline planned finish date for this WBS element as established in the approved project schedule. Used for schedule variance analysis, milestone tracking, and Liquidated Damages (LD) exposure assessment.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – Planned Finish; PMI PMBOK – Schedule Baseline; FIDIC Clause 8

description: Total planned quantity of work for this WBS element as defined in the Bill of Quantities (BOQ) or Material Take-Off (MTO). Used for production tracking and unit-rate cost control in HCSS HeavyJob.
reference: ASCE – Quantity Surveying Standards; BOQ per FIDIC; HCSS HeavyJob – Planned Quantity

description: Baseline planned start date for this WBS element as established in the approved project schedule in Oracle Primavera P6. Used for schedule variance analysis and CPM scheduling.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – Planned Start; PMI PMBOK – Schedule Baseline; FIDIC Clause 8

business_glossary_term: Planned Value (PV) – Budgeted Cost of Work Scheduled (BCWS)
description: Budgeted cost of work scheduled to be completed by the data date for this WBS element (BCWS). Derived from the time-phased budget baseline in Primavera P6. Used to compute Schedule Variance (SV) in EVM.
reference: PMI Practice Standard for Earned Value Management; Oracle Primavera P6 – Planned Value; AACE RP 11R-88

description: Reference to the approved project schedule baseline against which this WBS element's planned dates and costs are measured. Supports multi-baseline comparison in Oracle Primavera P6.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – Baseline Management; PMI PMBOK – Performance Measurement Baseline

description: Engineering or construction discipline accountable for executing this WBS element (e.g., Civil, Structural, MEP, Electrical, Mechanical, Instrumentation). Used for resource planning and discipline-level cost reporting.
reference: ASCE Standards – Engineering Disciplines; EPC Project Execution Framework

description: Detailed narrative description of the work scope, deliverables, and boundaries associated with this WBS element. Aligns with the project's Statement of Work (SOW) and Bill of Quantities (BOQ) scope definitions.
reference: PMI PMBOK Guide – Scope Baseline; FIDIC Conditions of Contract – Scope of Works

description: Numeric sequence defining the display order of this WBS element among its siblings within the same parent node. Used for consistent rendering in schedules, reports, and BOQ documents.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – WBS Sequence Number

description: Operational system of record from which this WBS element record was ingested: primavera_p6 (Oracle Primavera P6), sap_ps (SAP S/4HANA Project Systems), procore, or manual entry. Supports data lineage and audit.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6; SAP S/4HANA PS; Procore

description: Native identifier of this WBS element in the originating operational system (e.g., Primavera P6 WBS Object ID, SAP PS WBS Element internal number). Supports data lineage and reconciliation between the lakehouse and source systems.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – WBS Object ID; SAP PS – WBS Element (PRPS-PSPNR)

description: Unit of measure for planned and actual quantities on this WBS element (e.g., m3, m2, LF, EA, TON, LS, HR). Aligns with BOQ and MTO unit conventions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: m3|m2|LF|EA|TON|LS|HR|KG|ML|CY — promote to reference product]
reference: FIDIC BOQ Standards; ASCE Quantity Surveying; ISO 80000 – Quantities and Units

description: Structured alphanumeric code representing the WBS node position in the hierarchy (e.g., 1.2.3.4). Used as the externally-known identifier in Primavera P6, SAP PS, and project documentation. Conforms to project WBS coding convention.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – WBS Code Field; PMI PMBOK WBS Standard; SAP PS WBS Element Code

description: Integer depth of the WBS node within the project hierarchy (Level 1 = project root, Level 2 = major deliverable, Level 3+ = sub-deliverable or work package). Used for hierarchical filtering, rollup aggregation, and reporting drill-down.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – WBS Level; PMI PMBOK Guide

description: Human-readable name or title of the WBS element describing the scope deliverable or work package (e.g., 'Substructure Works', 'Electrical Installation – Level 3'). Used in reports, dashboards, and EVM analysis.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – WBS Name Field; PMI Practice Standard for WBS

description: Current lifecycle status of the WBS element indicating whether work is active, not yet started, on hold, completed, or cancelled. Drives EVM eligibility and cost control reporting.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 – WBS Status; PMI PMBOK Guide – Project Scope Management

description: Classification of the WBS node by its role in the hierarchy: 'summary' (rollup node), 'work_package' (lowest deliverable-level node), 'control_account' (EVM measurement point), or 'planning_package' (future scope not yet detailed). Drives EVM rollup logic and cost control granularity.
reference: PMI Practice Standard for Earned Value Management; AACE International Recommended Practice No. 11R-88

description: Unique system-generated identifier for the handover certificate record. Primary key for the handover_certificate data product within the project domain.
Reference to the contract under which the handover is being executed. Ties the certificate to the governing contractual instrument (e.g., FIDIC, EPC, DB, DBB).
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract; Procore Contract Administration

description: Foreign key linking to client.contact. Business justification: Handover certificate requires linking to the client signatory contact for legal sign‑off tracking and audit compliance.
Reference to the parent project for which this handover certificate is issued. Links the certificate to the project master record.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6; SAP S/4HANA Project Systems

description: Reference to the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element representing the scope of works being handed over. Enables traceability from certificate to project scope decomposition.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6; PMI PMBOK WBS Standard

description: The document identifier assigned to the handover certificate in the Aconex document management system. Enables traceability to the official correspondence and transmittal register for audit and contractual purposes.
Document transmittal or register reference for the as-built drawings submitted as part of the handover package. Confirms that record drawings have been delivered to the client.

Reference identifier or document number of the as-built Building Information Modeling (BIM) model associated with the handed-over works. Links the certificate to the digital asset record in Autodesk BIM 360 for O&M (Operations and Maintenance) purposes.

Externally-known, human-readable unique reference number for the handover certificate as issued and registered in the document management system (e.g., Aconex). Used in correspondence, transmittals, and contractual communications.
reference: Aconex Document Management; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: Current lifecycle status of the handover certificate. Draft: under preparation; Issued: formally submitted to client; Accepted: client has signed acceptance; Rejected: client has declined acceptance; Superseded: replaced by a revised certificate.
reference: Procore Submittals Workflow; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: The date on which the client formally accepted and signed the handover certificate, confirming transfer of care, custody, and control of the works. Triggers the start of the Defects Liability Period (DLP).
reference: FIDIC Clause 10.1; ASCE Project Delivery Standards

description: Job title or designation of the client representative who signed the handover certificate (e.g., Client Project Manager, Engineer's Representative, Owner's Representative). Validates the authority level of the acceptance.

Indicates whether commissioning activities have been fully completed for the works included in the handover scope. Commissioning completion is a mandatory prerequisite for final handover in EPC and infrastructure projects.
Full name of the authorized contractor representative who signed the handover certificate on behalf of the contractor organization. Required for contractual validity of the certificate.

Job title or designation of the contractor representative who signed the handover certificate (e.g., Project Director, Site Manager, Construction Manager). Validates the authority level of the signatory.
The timestamp when the handover certificate record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting data lineage, compliance, and record management requirements.

The contractual delivery model under which the project is executed and the handover is being performed. Determines the applicable handover procedures and obligations. EPC: Engineering Procurement Construction; DB: Design-Build; DBB: Design-Bid-Build; PPP: Public-Private Partnership; BOT: Build-Operate-Transfer; GMP: Guaranteed Maximum Price.
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract; PMO Governance Framework

business_glossary_term: Defects Liability Period (DLP) Duration in Days
description: The contractually agreed duration of the Defects Liability Period (DLP) expressed in calendar days. Typically 365 days (12 months) for standard construction contracts but may vary by contract type and jurisdiction.

The date on which the Defects Liability Period (DLP) expires, after which the contractor's obligation to remedy defects under the contract ceases. Calculated from DLP start date plus the contractually agreed DLP duration.

The date on which the Defects Liability Period (DLP) commences, typically aligned with the client acceptance date. Marks the beginning of the contractor's obligation to remedy defects at no additional cost.

Full name of the Engineer (or Engineer's Representative) under FIDIC contracts who countersigns the handover certificate. Applicable under FIDIC Red/Yellow/Silver Book delivery models where an independent Engineer administers the contract.

Total number of Extension of Time (EOT) days granted and applied to the contractual completion date for the scope covered by this handover certificate. Affects LD liability calculation and schedule performance reporting.

Indicates whether Factory Acceptance Testing (FAT) has been completed for all equipment and systems included in the handover scope. FAT is a prerequisite for handover in EPC and MEP-intensive projects.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 8.6; IEC Factory Acceptance Standards

description: Classification of the handover certificate indicating the nature and extent of the transfer of works. Partial: a defined portion of works; Sectional: a contractually defined section; Substantial Completion: works are practically complete with minor outstanding items; Final: all works including punch list resolved; Provisional: conditional acceptance pending outstanding items.
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract Clause 10; IBC Completion Standards

business_glossary_term: Health Safety and Environment (HSE) Clearance Obtained
description: Indicates whether all required Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) clearances and regulatory approvals have been obtained prior to handover. Includes OSHA compliance, environmental permits, and occupancy certificates.
reference: OSHA Standards; ISO 45001; ISO 14001; EPA Regulations

description: The date on which the handover certificate was formally issued by the contractor to the client. This is the principal business event date (BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category) representing the real-world transfer initiation event.

Indicates whether all Inspection and Test Plan (ITP) hold points and witness points for the works scope have been completed and signed off prior to handover. ITP completion is a mandatory QA/QC prerequisite for handover.

The date on which the contractor's exposure to Liquidated Damages (LD) for delay ceases, typically aligned with the handover acceptance date. Critical for financial exposure tracking and contract administration.
reference: FIDIC Clause 8.7; SAP S/4HANA Contract Management

business_glossary_term: Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design (LEED) Certification Level
description: LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) certification level achieved for the works being handed over, if applicable. Relevant for commercial and public sector projects with sustainability requirements.
reference: LEED U.S. Green Building Council; ISO 14001

description: Total number of open Non-Conformance Reports (NCRs) against the works scope at the time of handover certificate issuance. Open NCRs may be a condition precedent to client acceptance under QA/QC requirements.

Document reference number for the Operations and Maintenance (O&M) manual submitted as part of the handover package. Confirms that O&M documentation has been provided to the client as a condition of handover.

The submittal identifier assigned to the handover certificate package in Procore Construction Management. Enables cross-system traceability between the data lakehouse and the operational construction management platform.
The date by which all outstanding punch list items are contractually required to be resolved and closed. Agreed between contractor and client at the time of handover.

Number of punch list items classified as critical or Category A at the time of handover, representing items that must be resolved before or immediately after handover. Distinct from total punch list count.
reference: Procore Punch List; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Total number of outstanding punch list items (minor defects, incomplete works, or non-conformances) recorded at the time of handover certificate issuance. A key indicator of completion quality at handover.

Reference number or identifier of the regulatory authority approval (e.g., building permit, occupancy certificate, environmental clearance) obtained as a condition of handover. Required for compliance with IBC, OSHA, and EPA regulations.

Narrative description of the reason(s) for client rejection of the handover certificate, if applicable. Populated when certificate_status is 'rejected'. Supports root cause analysis and resubmission planning.
reference: Procore RFI and Submittal Workflow; ISO 9001 Non-Conformance

description: Indicates whether the issuance of this handover certificate triggers eligibility for release of contractual retention monies held by the client. Typically, substantial completion or final handover certificates trigger partial or full retention release.
reference: FIDIC Clause 14.9; SAP S/4HANA Accounts Payable

description: Sequential revision number of the handover certificate, incremented each time the certificate is revised and reissued following client rejection or scope amendment. Revision 0 represents the original issue.
reference: Aconex Document Management; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: Indicates whether Site Acceptance Testing (SAT) has been completed for all systems and equipment included in the handover scope. SAT confirms that systems function correctly in their installed environment prior to handover.
reference: ISO 9001 Clause 8.6; IEC Site Acceptance Standards

description: Narrative description of the works, systems, or areas being handed over under this certificate. Defines the physical and functional boundary of the handover including building sections, infrastructure systems, or MEP (Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing) systems.

Indicates whether all contractually required spare parts and consumables have been delivered to the client as part of the handover package. Spare parts delivery is a standard handover condition in EPC and infrastructure contracts.
reference: FIDIC Clause 10; SAP S/4HANA Materials Management

description: Indicates whether the contractor has completed all contractually required training for the client's operations and maintenance personnel on the handed-over systems and equipment.
The timestamp when the handover certificate record was last modified in the system. Supports audit trail, change tracking, and data quality monitoring in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Physical location or site area description of the works being handed over (e.g., Building A - Floors 1-5, Highway Section KM 12-18, Substation Block C). Provides geographic and spatial context for the handover scope.
Primary key for the Regulatory_Oversight association
Links to the construction project being overseen
Links to the regulatory authority providing oversight
Current compliance status of the project with respect to the authority
Date when the authority began overseeing the project
How often the project must report to the authority
Unique surrogate identifier for each risk register entry in the project risk register. Primary key for the risk_register data product.
reference: PMO Governance Framework; ISO 31000 Risk Management

description: Reference to the parent construction project to which this risk register entry belongs. Links the risk to the project master record for PMO governance and EPC execution tracking.
reference: PMO Governance Framework; Oracle Primavera P6

description: Reference to the workforce member who originally identified and raised this risk entry in the register. Supports accountability tracking and risk identification trend analysis.
reference: ISO 31000 Risk Management; PMO Governance Framework

description: Reference to the specific Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element at which this risk is identified. Enables risk attribution at sub-project or work-package level for granular PMO reporting.
reference: PMO Governance Framework; Oracle Primavera P6 WBS; PMBOK WBS Standard

description: Construction or engineering discipline most affected by this risk (e.g., Civil, Structural, MEP, Geotechnical, Electrical, Procurement). Supports discipline-level risk reporting and resource allocation for mitigation.
reference: PMO Governance Framework; ASCE Standards; AISC Standards

description: Calendar date on which the risk entry was formally closed, either because the risk window has passed, the risk was fully mitigated, or the risk was realized and the contingency plan executed.
reference: ISO 31000 Risk Management; PMO Governance Framework

description: Monetary contingency reserve allocated to this risk entry, expressed in the project currency. Feeds into the project contingency budget managed through SAP S/4HANA Project Systems and PMO cost control.
reference: IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting; PMO Governance Framework; SAP S/4HANA Project Systems

description: Description of the fallback actions to be executed if the risk event is realized despite mitigation efforts. Defines trigger conditions, response steps, and responsible parties for contingency activation.
reference: ISO 31000 Risk Management; PMO Governance Framework; PMBOK Risk Management

description: Reference to the specific contract clause (e.g., FIDIC Clause 17.3, GMP provision, LD clause) that governs the allocation or treatment of this risk. Supports contract administration and legal risk management.
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract; PMO Governance Framework

description: Estimated monetary value of the cost impact if the risk event is realized, expressed in the project currency. Used for contingency reserve sizing, Earned Value Management (EVM) risk exposure, and project P&L forecasting.
reference: IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting; PMO Governance Framework; ISO 31000

description: Qualitative rating of the potential cost impact if the risk event is realized, using the standard five-point PMO scale. Used in risk matrix scoring and contingency reserve determination.
reference: ISO 31000 Risk Management; PMO Governance Framework; FIDIC Clause 17

description: Timestamp when this risk register record was first created in the data platform. RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED for this entity. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: GDPR Article 5 Data Integrity; PMO Governance Framework

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the cost impact amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency international EPC projects.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting

description: Indicates whether this risk has been escalated to senior management or the PMO for executive attention due to its high risk score, proximity, or strategic significance to the project.

Number of audit findings that remain open (not yet resolved and verified) at the time of the last record update. Key metric for audit close-out tracking and corrective action management.

Indicates whether findings from this audit require formal notification to a regulatory authority (e.g., OSHA, EPA). Drives compliance reporting obligations and legal review workflows.

description: Primary regulatory or management system standard against which the audit was conducted (e.g., OSHA 29 CFR 1926, ISO 45001:2018, ISO 14001:2015, site-specific HSE plan). Multiple standards may be listed separated by semicolons.

Indicates whether a stop-work order was issued as a result of critical findings identified during the audit. Triggers mandatory escalation to project management and HSE leadership.

Comma-separated list of names or employee IDs of additional audit team members who participated in the audit. Stored as a denormalised string for reporting convenience; detailed team composition is managed in Intelex.
Descriptive title of the audit, summarising its scope and focus area (e.g., 'Q3 Site Safety Compliance Audit – Highway Project Alpha'). Used for identification in reports and dashboards.
Total number of audit criteria or checklist items evaluated during the audit. Used as the denominator for compliance score calculation and for comparing audit rigour across audits.
Total number of audit findings raised across all severity levels (non-conformances, observations, and opportunities for improvement). Provides a headline count for executive reporting.
Timestamp of the most recent update to the audit record in the data platform. Used for incremental load detection, change data capture, and data quality monitoring.
reference: ISO 45001:2018 Clause 7.5; Data Governance — Change Tracking

Current lifecycle status of the hazard record. open = identified but controls not yet fully implemented; controlled = controls in place and residual risk is tolerable; eliminated = hazard has been fully removed; closed = record closed after verification. Short, human-readable title describing the hazard (e.g., 'Unguarded floor opening — Level 3', 'Overhead power line proximity — Zone B'). Used in pre-start briefings and toolbox meeting topic selection.

Classification of the hazard by primary injury mechanism aligned with OSHA Fatal Four and construction industry taxonomy. Highest level of the hierarchy of controls applied to manage this hazard, per ISO 45001 and OSHA guidance. Values: elimination (remove hazard), substitution (replace with lesser hazard), engineering (physical barriers/guards), administrative (procedures/training), ppe (Personal Protective Equipment). Drives control effectiveness assessment.

The originating activity or process through which the hazard was identified. Supports root-cause analysis and evaluation of hazard identification program effectiveness. Values: risk_assessment, inspection, incident, audit, near_miss, worker_report.

Numeric consequence/severity score assigned during initial risk assessment before controls are applied, typically on a 1–5 scale (1=Insignificant, 5=Catastrophic). Component of the initial risk rating matrix (likelihood × consequence).

Numeric likelihood score assigned during initial risk assessment before controls are applied, typically on a 1–5 scale (1=Rare, 5=Almost Certain). Component of the initial risk rating matrix (likelihood × consequence).
Qualitative risk band derived from the initial risk score (e.g., low, medium, high, critical). Used for risk prioritisation, escalation thresholds, and executive HSE dashboards.

Pre-control risk score calculated as the product of initial_likelihood_rating × initial_consequence_rating. Represents the inherent risk level before any control measures are applied. Used to prioritise hazard treatment actions.

Source system record identifier from the Intelex HSE Management platform. Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and bi-directional synchronisation between the Databricks Silver layer and the operational Intelex system.

Date on which the hazard record was most recently reviewed and updated by the responsible HSE officer or site supervisor. Supports periodic review compliance requirements under ISO 45001 and regulatory obligations.
System timestamp recording when the hazard record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver layer.

Free-text description of the precise physical location of the hazard within the site zone (e.g., 'North face of pier 3, 2m from edge', 'Adjacent to transformer substation'). Complements site_zone for field navigation.
Scheduled date for the next mandatory review of this hazard record. Drives automated review reminders and overdue review reporting in the Intelex HSE system.

Indicates whether a Permit to Work (PTW) is mandatory before any work activity associated with this hazard can commence. True = PTW required (e.g., confined space entry, hot work, electrical isolation). Integrates with Intelex Permit to Work module.

Specific PPE items mandated for workers operating in proximity to this hazard (e.g., 'Hard hat, safety harness, hi-vis vest, safety glasses'). Supports PPE compliance checks during pre-start briefings and site inspections.

description: Indicates whether this hazard requires formal notification to a regulatory authority (e.g., OSHA, EPA) due to its nature or severity. True = notification required. Supports regulatory compliance workflow management.

description: Numeric consequence/severity score after all control measures have been applied, on the same 1–5 scale as initial_consequence_rating. Reflects the effectiveness of controls in reducing severity of potential harm.
Numeric likelihood score after all control measures have been applied, on the same 1–5 scale as initial_likelihood_rating. Reflects the effectiveness of controls in reducing probability of harm.

Qualitative risk band derived from the residual risk score after controls are applied. Determines whether the hazard is tolerable (low/medium) or requires escalation and further treatment (high/critical).

Post-control risk score calculated as residual_likelihood_rating × residual_consequence_rating. Represents the remaining risk after controls are applied. Used to determine if the risk is tolerable or requires further treatment.

Name or role of the individual or team accountable for implementing and maintaining the control measures for this hazard (e.g., 'Site Supervisor — Civil Works', 'HSE Manager'). Supports accountability tracking and corrective action follow-up.
