reference: EPA - Environmental Protection Agency; ISO 14001 Clause 8.1 - Operational Control

description: Number of welfare facility units (site offices, canteens, toilets, first aid rooms, drying rooms) included in this logistics plan. Welfare provision must meet regulatory minimums per workforce headcount.
Unique surrogate identifier for each material delivery receipt event recorded at the construction site. Primary key for this entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.

Foreign key linking to schedule.activity. Business justification: Material deliveries are scheduled against specific construction activities; the FK enables material‑to‑activity traceability for cost and progress.
Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Material deliveries are executed by specific vehicles; tracking the asset supports logistics, cost, and compliance audits.
Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Delivery of hazardous or regulated materials requires a specific permit; linking validates compliance before receipt.
Reference to the construction project to which this material delivery belongs. Links the physical receipt event to the project context for cost coding and earned value computation.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6; SAP S/4HANA Project Systems

description: Foreign key linking to design.design_submittal. Business justification: Deliveries are matched to approved design submittals to verify material compliance before acceptance.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Material delivery records must map to finance cost codes for material cost allocation and inventory valuation.

Foreign key linking to subcontractor.firm_profile. Business justification: Materials are frequently delivered by subcontractors; linking delivery to the subcontractor enables accountability, invoicing and quality traceability.
Reference to the supplier or vendor who delivered the materials. Links to the procurement domain supplier master for vendor performance tracking.
Reference to the procurement domain Purchase Order (PO) against which this delivery was made. Enables reconciliation between the physical receipt event and the contractual procurement record.
Reference to the employee record of the site personnel who received and signed off on the delivery. Links to the workforce domain for accountability and audit trail purposes.
Reference to the construction site where the material delivery was physically received. Supports site-level logistics and laydown area management.

The manufacturer's batch or lot number for the delivered material. Critical for quality traceability, enabling recall or investigation of specific production batches if non-conformance is identified post-installation.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; ACI; AISC

description: The date and time when this material delivery record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.
reference: ISO 9001; GDPR Article 5 (data integrity and accountability)

description: The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the delivery value and unit rate are expressed (e.g., USD, AUD, GBP). Required for multi-currency project financial management and IFRS/GAAP reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: The calendar date on which the material physically arrived at the site gate or designated laydown area. This is the principal real-world event date for the delivery transaction, distinct from the PO expected delivery date.

Current lifecycle status of the material delivery receipt event. Indicates whether the delivery was fully accepted, rejected at the gate, partially accepted, pending quality inspection, or placed on hold pending resolution of a discrepancy.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; Procore Construction Management

description: The precise date and time at which the delivery vehicle arrived at the site gate or laydown area. Supports site logistics scheduling, gate management, and time-stamped audit trails.
reference: Procore Daily Logs; HCSS HeavyJob Field Operations

description: The total monetary value of the accepted delivery, calculated as quantity_accepted multiplied by unit_rate. Used for goods receipt posting, cost accrual, and three-way matching against the supplier invoice. Expressed in the project currency.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance GL/AP; IFRS/GAAP; Viewpoint Vista

description: Free-text field capturing details of any discrepancy identified at the point of receipt, including quantity shortfalls, damage descriptions, specification mismatches, or incorrect materials. Used to support NCR (Non-Conformance Report) creation and supplier claims.
reference: ISO 9001; Procore RFI and Submittal Management

description: The externally-issued delivery docket or delivery note number provided by the supplier on the physical delivery documentation. Used as the primary business identifier for the delivery event and for three-way matching against the PO and invoice.
The name of the delivery driver as recorded on the delivery docket or site gate register. Captured for site security, access control, and delivery audit trail purposes.
reference: GDPR Article 4; OSHA Site Access Requirements

description: The date on which the delivery was originally scheduled or expected to arrive at site as per the Purchase Order (PO) or procurement schedule. Used to calculate delivery schedule variance and supplier on-time delivery performance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Materials Management; Oracle Primavera P6 Scheduling

description: The SAP S/4HANA Goods Receipt (GR) document number generated upon posting of the material receipt in the ERP system. Provides the formal financial and inventory accounting reference for the delivery event.

Indicates whether the delivered material is classified as hazardous under applicable regulations (e.g., chemicals, solvents, fuels, asbestos-containing materials). When True, triggers HSE compliance protocols including MSDS verification and segregated storage.
reference: OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (HazCom); EPA; ISO 14001; NFPA

description: Reference to the Inspection and Test Plan (ITP) hold or witness point associated with this material delivery. Links the receipt event to the project quality plan to confirm that required inspections were completed before material acceptance.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; Procore Quality Management

description: The designated receiving location or laydown area on the site logistics plan where the delivered material was stored upon receipt (e.g., 'Zone A - Steel Yard', 'Zone C - Concrete Batching Area'). Supports site logistics management and material traceability.
reference: Procore Field Management; HCSS HeavyJob Site Operations

description: High-level classification of the material type delivered. Supports site logistics planning, laydown zone assignment, and HSE compliance for hazardous materials. MEP refers to Mechanical, Electrical, and Plumbing materials. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concrete|steel|timber|aggregates|MEP|civil|finishing|hazardous|other — promote to reference product]
reference: ISO 9001; OSHA Hazard Communication Standard; NFPA

description: The internal material catalogue code or SAP material number identifying the type of material delivered. Used for stock updates in the materials domain and reconciliation with the Bill of Quantities (BOQ).
reference: SAP S/4HANA Materials Management; BOQ standards

description: Free-text description of the material delivered as stated on the delivery docket. Captures the specific grade, specification, or product name (e.g., 'Ready-Mix Concrete 40MPa', 'Structural Steel Grade 350', 'HDPE Pipe DN200').
reference: ISO 9001; ACI (American Concrete Institute); AISC (American Institute of Steel Construction)

business_glossary_term: Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) Verified Flag
description: Indicates whether the Material Safety Data Sheet (MSDS) or Safety Data Sheet (SDS) was verified and on file at the time of receipt for hazardous materials. Required for OSHA HazCom compliance and site HSE management.

The reference number of the Non-Conformance Report (NCR) raised as a result of a rejected or non-conforming delivery. Links the physical receipt event to the quality management process for corrective action tracking.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; Procore Quality Management

description: The specific line item number on the Purchase Order (PO) to which this delivery relates. Enables precise three-way matching at the line level between the PO, goods receipt, and supplier invoice.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Materials Management; IFRS/GAAP procurement standards

description: The reference identifier of the associated Procore daily log entry in which this material delivery was recorded. Enables traceability back to the source system of record for field management and audit purposes.

The quantity of material formally accepted after inspection at the point of receipt. May be less than quantity_delivered in cases of partial acceptance due to damage, non-conformance, or specification mismatch. Triggers stock update in the materials domain.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; SAP S/4HANA Materials Management

description: The actual quantity of material physically received at the site as measured or counted at the point of receipt. This is the principal quantitative fact for the delivery event and is used to update material stock records and reconcile against the PO quantity.

The quantity of material that was ordered on the associated Purchase Order (PO) line for this delivery. Enables immediate over/under delivery variance calculation at the point of receipt without requiring a join to the procurement domain.

The quantity of material rejected at the point of receipt due to damage, non-conformance with specification, or incorrect material. Triggers a Non-Conformance Report (NCR) and return-to-supplier process.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; NCR process

description: The physical condition of the material as assessed at the point of receipt. Drives the acceptance/rejection decision and informs quality control processes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: good|damaged|wet|contaminated|incorrect_spec|short_delivered — promote to reference product]
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems; ACI; AISC

description: The full name of the site personnel who physically received and inspected the delivery at the site gate or laydown area. This individual is accountable for the acceptance/rejection decision recorded on the delivery docket.

Indicates whether the delivered material requires temperature-controlled storage conditions (e.g., epoxy resins, certain adhesives, bituminous products, or cold-chain materials). When True, triggers special handling and storage protocols on site.
reference: OSHA Hazard Communication Standard; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: The reference number of the material test certificate or mill certificate provided by the supplier with the delivery. Confirms that the material meets the specified engineering standards and is required for quality assurance sign-off.
reference: ISO 9001; ACI 301; AISC Code of Standard Practice

description: The unit of measure in which the delivered quantity is expressed (e.g., cubic metres for concrete, tonnes for steel, each for prefabricated elements). Must align with the unit specified on the Purchase Order (PO) for reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: m3|tonne|kg|m|m2|EA|L|bag|roll|set — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA Materials Management; ISO 80000 (Quantities and Units)

description: The agreed unit price for the material as per the Purchase Order (PO), expressed in the project currency. Used to calculate the value of the delivery for goods receipt posting and cost accrual in the project financial management system.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance; IFRS/GAAP; Viewpoint Vista Job Costing

description: The date and time when this material delivery record was most recently modified. Supports change tracking, audit compliance, and incremental data pipeline processing.
The registration plate number of the delivery vehicle. Recorded at the site gate for security, access control, and logistics tracking purposes.
reference: OSHA Site Access Requirements; Procore Daily Logs

description: The Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) code to which the cost of this material delivery is allocated. Enables project cost control, earned value management (EVM), and cost performance index (CPI) computation at the WBS element level.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6; SAP S/4HANA Project Systems; PMI PMBOK

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each daily crew deployment record. Primary key for the crew_deployment data product in the site domain Silver layer.
Reference to the scheduled activity (Work Breakdown Structure task) from Oracle Primavera P6 to which this crew is assigned. Enables schedule performance tracking and earned value computation.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6; WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) Standard

description: Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Crew deployment plans include equipment assigned to each crew; needed for resource planning and equipment utilisation tracking.
Reference to the construction project under which this crew deployment is recorded. Links deployment records to project-level earned value and cost control.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6; SAP S/4HANA Project Systems

description: Foreign key linking to site.daily_log. Business justification: crew_deployment records reference a daily log via the string field daily_log_reference; converting to a proper FK (daily_log_id) enforces referential integrity and removes the redundant string column.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Crew deployment cost tracking uses finance cost codes to aggregate labor costs per activity for payroll and project costing.

Reference to the subcontractor entity if this crew is supplied by a subcontractor rather than the general contractor's direct workforce. Null for self-performed work. Supports subcontractor cost tracking and performance management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Materials Management; Procore Subcontractor Management; FIDIC (International Federation of Consulting Engineers)

description: Employee identifier of the lead foreman from the workforce system (SAP SuccessFactors). Enables linkage to workforce domain records without duplicating personal data.
reference: SAP SuccessFactors Human Capital Management; GDPR Article 4

description: Mandatory reference to the work front (spatial zone or work face) to which this crew is deployed. Establishes the spatial allocation of the crew on site for the given date.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Field Operations; Procore Construction Management

description: Total man-hours actually worked by the crew on this deployment date, as recorded in HCSS HeavyJob time tracking. Compared against planned_hours to compute productivity variance and feed EVM calculations.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Time Tracking; EVM (Earned Value Management) Standard

description: Actual quantity of work output achieved by the crew on this deployment date, as recorded in HCSS HeavyJob production tracking. Compared against planned_production_qty for productivity analysis and earned value computation.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Production Tracking; EVM (Earned Value Management) Standard

description: Date and time when this crew deployment record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for data governance and lineage tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability); ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Alphanumeric business identifier for the crew or gang as defined in HCSS HeavyJob (e.g., 'CIV-A', 'CONC-03'). Used for cost coding and production tracking lookups.
Human-readable name or designation of the crew or gang being deployed (e.g., 'Civil Gang A', 'Concrete Pour Crew 3', 'Steel Erection Team North'). Sourced from HCSS HeavyJob crew master.
Total number of workers (headcount) in the deployed crew or gang for this date. Used for productivity analysis, labour cost coding, and HSE compliance checks.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Time Tracking; OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration)

description: Classification of the crew by trade discipline. Drives resource planning, productivity benchmarking, and cost code allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: civil|mep|concrete|steel_erection|earthworks|finishing|specialist|formwork|rebar|waterproofing — promote to reference product]
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Field Operations; AISC (American Institute of Steel Construction); ACI (American Concrete Institute)

description: Standardised code identifying the primary reason for any production delay or underperformance on this deployment (e.g., WEATHER, MATERIAL_SHORTAGE, EQUIPMENT_BREAKDOWN, DESIGN_CHANGE, PERMIT_DELAY). Supports delay analysis and EOT claims. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: WEATHER|MATERIAL_SHORTAGE|EQUIPMENT_BREAKDOWN|DESIGN_CHANGE|PERMIT_DELAY|LABOUR_SHORTAGE|REWORK|SAFETY_STOP — promote to reference product]
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Field Operations; FIDIC (International Federation of Consulting Engineers); Procore Change Orders

description: Calendar date on which the crew is deployed to the work front. The principal business event date for this daily deployment record. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Time Tracking; Procore Daily Logs

description: Externally-known business identifier for this crew deployment record, typically generated by HCSS HeavyJob (e.g., CD-2024-00123). Used for cross-system referencing and field reporting.
Current lifecycle state of the crew deployment record. Tracks whether the deployment is planned, actively in progress, completed, cancelled, or suspended due to site conditions.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Field Operations; Procore Construction Management

business_glossary_term: Health Safety and Environment (HSE) Toolbox Meeting (TBM) Held Flag
description: Indicates whether a Toolbox Meeting (TBM) was conducted with this crew prior to commencing work on this deployment date (True) or not (False). Mandatory HSE compliance check per OSHA and ISO 45001 requirements.
reference: OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration); ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; Intelex HSE Management

description: Indicates whether this crew deployment involved overtime work (True) or was a standard shift (False). Sourced from HCSS HeavyJob. Used for payroll flagging and cost escalation alerts.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Time Tracking; Viewpoint Vista Payroll

description: Indicates whether this crew is supplied by a subcontractor (True) or is part of the general contractor's direct workforce (False). Drives cost segregation between self-performed and subcontracted labour.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Project Systems; Viewpoint Vista Job Costing; FIDIC (International Federation of Consulting Engineers)

description: Indicates whether adverse weather conditions materially impacted crew productivity or caused a work stoppage on this deployment date (True) or not (False). Supports Extension of Time (EOT) and delay claim substantiation.
reference: Procore Daily Logs; FIDIC (International Federation of Consulting Engineers) Contract Conditions

description: Full name of the lead foreman or gang leader responsible for supervising this crew deployment. Captured from HCSS HeavyJob time tracking. Classified confidential as it identifies a named employee in a supervisory role.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Field Operations; SAP SuccessFactors Human Capital Management

description: Indicates the mobilization lifecycle stage of the crew relative to the project site. Tracks whether the crew is in the process of mobilizing, fully on site, demobilizing, or has fully demobilized. Supports site logistics planning.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Field Operations; Procore Construction Management

description: Number of overtime hours worked by the crew beyond the standard shift duration on this deployment date. Sourced from HCSS HeavyJob payroll records. Feeds Viewpoint Vista payroll and job costing.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Time Tracking; Viewpoint Vista Payroll; OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration)

description: Reference number of the Permit to Work (PTW) issued for this crew deployment, where the work activity requires a formal permit (e.g., hot work, confined space, excavation). Sourced from Intelex HSE management system.
reference: OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration); ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; Intelex Permit to Work

description: Total man-hours planned for this crew deployment on the given date, as scheduled in Oracle Primavera P6. Used as the baseline for earned value computation and productivity variance analysis.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 Resource Assignment; EVM (Earned Value Management) Standard

description: Target quantity of work output planned for this crew deployment (e.g., cubic metres of concrete, linear metres of pipe, tonnes of steel). Defined in HCSS HeavyJob production tracking against the cost code.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Production Tracking; BOQ (Bill of Quantities) Standard

description: Indicates whether all crew members were observed to be wearing required Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) at the start of this deployment (True) or a non-compliance was recorded (False). Sourced from Procore or Intelex field inspection.
reference: OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration); ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; Intelex HSE Management

description: Unit of measure for the planned and actual production quantities (e.g., m3, LM, tonne, m2, EA). Sourced from the HCSS HeavyJob cost code definition and aligned with the Bill of Quantities (BOQ).
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Production Tracking; BOQ (Bill of Quantities) Standard; ISO 80000 Quantities and Units

description: Free-text field capturing foreman or site supervisor observations about crew productivity, constraints, delays, or notable events during this deployment. Sourced from HCSS HeavyJob or Procore daily log narrative.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Field Operations; Procore Daily Logs

description: Actual production output per man-hour achieved by this crew on this deployment date (actual_production_qty / actual_hours). Sourced from HCSS HeavyJob production tracking. Used for benchmarking and Schedule Performance Index (SPI) analysis.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Production Tracking; SPI (Schedule Performance Index) Standard; EVM (Earned Value Management)

description: Actual date and time the crew shift ended on site. Sourced from HCSS HeavyJob time tracking. Used in conjunction with shift_start_time to compute actual hours worked. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Time Tracking; OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration)

description: Actual date and time the crew shift commenced on site. Sourced from HCSS HeavyJob time tracking. Used for hours calculation, overtime determination, and HSE compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Time Tracking; OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration)

description: Classification of the work shift for this deployment (day, night, swing, or weekend). Drives overtime calculations, HSE fatigue management, and payroll processing.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Time Tracking; OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration); ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety

description: The native record identifier from the originating source system (HCSS HeavyJob or Procore) for this crew deployment entry. Enables reverse traceability from the Silver layer back to the operational system of record.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Field Operations; Procore Construction Management

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this crew deployment record was sourced (HCSS HeavyJob, Procore, or Manual entry). Supports data lineage and audit traceability in the Silver layer.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob Field Operations; Procore Construction Management

description: Document reference number of the Safe Work Method Statement (SWMS) applicable to this crew deployment and work activity. Confirms that the required HSE documentation was in place before work commenced.
reference: OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration); ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; Intelex HSE Management

description: Date and time when this crew deployment record was last modified in the data platform. Supports incremental data processing, audit trails, and change detection in the Silver layer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability); ISO 9001 Quality Management Systems

description: Work Breakdown Structure code from Oracle Primavera P6 identifying the project deliverable or work package to which this crew deployment is charged. Supports earned value management (EVM) computation.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6; EVM (Earned Value Management) Standard; PMI Practice Standard for WBS

description: Prevailing weather condition recorded on site for this deployment date, as captured in the Procore daily log. Impacts productivity analysis, delay claims, and Extension of Time (EOT) documentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|cloudy|rain|heavy_rain|wind|fog|snow|extreme_heat — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Procore Daily Logs; FIDIC (International Federation of Consulting Engineers) Contract Conditions

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the daily site log record. Primary key for the daily_log data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.
Foreign key linking to schedule.activity. Business justification: Daily Log records activities performed each day; linking to schedule.activity enables daily progress reports and variance analysis.
Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Daily logs are billed to the governing contract; linking enables cost allocation and compliance reporting per agreement.
Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Daily logs must reference the active permit governing that day's work to satisfy HSE audit trails and permit compliance verification.
Reference to the project this daily log belongs to. Links the site log to the master project record for earned value and cost control reporting.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6; Procore Daily Logs Module

description: Foreign key linking to design.drawing. Business justification: Daily Log reports reference the specific design drawing used for the day's work; required for progress audit and compliance.
Foreign key linking to safety.incident. Business justification: REQUIRED: Daily logs capture any incident that occurred that day; the FK enables quick incident lookup from the log.
Reference to the site superintendent responsible for completing and signing off this daily log. The accountable party for the legal and operational record of site activity.
reference: Procore Daily Logs Module; OSHA 29 CFR 1926 (Construction Safety)

description: Reference to the physical construction site or work front location where this daily log was recorded. Supports multi-site project tracking.
reference: Procore Daily Logs Module; HCSS HeavyJob

description: Date and time when the daily log was formally approved by the project manager or designated reviewer. Marks the transition from submitted to approved status in the log lifecycle.
reference: Procore Daily Logs Module; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: Total volume of concrete poured on site for the log date in cubic metres. Critical production metric for structural progress tracking, QA/QC compliance (ITP), and earned value computation. Sourced from Procore and HCSS HeavyJob.
reference: ACI 301 (Specifications for Structural Concrete); ITP (Inspection and Test Plan); HCSS HeavyJob

description: HCSS HeavyJob cost code assigned to the primary work activity recorded in this daily log. Enables direct integration between field production data and job costing in Viewpoint Vista and SAP S/4HANA Project Systems.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob (Cost Coding); Viewpoint Vista (Job Costing); SAP S/4HANA Project Systems

description: Indicates whether any event recorded in this daily log has been flagged as having a potential cost impact requiring a Change Order (CO) or variation claim. True triggers cost impact assessment and contract administration workflow.
reference: FIDIC Clause 13 (Variations); Procore Change Orders; SAP S/4HANA Project Systems

description: Date and time when this daily log record was first created in the system. Audit trail timestamp for data lineage and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity); ISO 9001 Records Management

description: Number of direct (GC-employed) workers on site for the log date, excluding subcontractor personnel. Used for workforce planning, payroll reconciliation, and labour productivity benchmarking.

Total volume of earthworks (cut, fill, or excavation) completed on site for the log date in cubic metres. Key production quantity for earned value computation, BOQ progress measurement, and HCSS HeavyJob production tracking.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob (Production Tracking); EVM (Earned Value Management); BOQ (Bill of Quantities)

description: Indicates whether any event recorded in this daily log has been flagged as having a potential Extension of Time (EOT) impact on the project programme. True initiates EOT substantiation documentation under FIDIC Clause 8.4 or applicable contract conditions.
reference: FIDIC Clause 8.4 (Extension of Time); Procore Daily Logs Module

description: Total number of major equipment units (plant and machinery) present and operational on site for the log date. Used for equipment utilisation tracking, cost coding in HCSS HeavyJob, and mobilisation/demobilisation records.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob (Equipment Hours); Viewpoint Vista (Equipment Management)

description: Total number of embedded line-item events (delay events, weather stoppages, utility strikes, design holds, access restrictions, safety observations) recorded within this daily log header. Supports event density analytics and claim complexity assessment.
reference: Procore Daily Logs Module; FIDIC Clause 20 (Claims)

description: Indicates whether one or more delay events were recorded in the embedded line-item events for this daily log. True triggers downstream delay analysis workflows and EOT claim tracking. Key filter for delay analytics.
reference: FIDIC Clause 8.4 (Extension of Time); Procore Daily Logs Module

description: Indicates whether one or more HSE safety observations or incidents were recorded in the embedded line-item events for this daily log. True triggers integration with Intelex HSE management for incident reporting and corrective action workflows.

Reference identifier linking this daily log to the corresponding field operations record in HCSS HeavyJob. Supports production tracking reconciliation and cost code validation between Procore and HCSS.

The calendar date for which this daily site log records field activities. The principal business event date — one log per project site per calendar day. Drives schedule performance and delay analysis.
reference: Procore Daily Logs Module; FIDIC Clause 8 (Programme)

description: Externally-known sequential log number assigned by the construction management system (Procore) for this daily log. Used as the human-readable business identifier in correspondence, delay claims, and EOT substantiation. Format: DL-{ProjectCode}-{Year}-{Sequence}.
reference: Procore Daily Logs Module; FIDIC Clause 20 (Claims)

description: Current workflow state of the daily log record. Draft indicates in-progress field entry; submitted indicates superintendent sign-off pending review; approved indicates accepted by project management; rejected indicates returned for correction; void indicates cancelled/superseded entry.
reference: Procore Daily Logs Module; ISO 9001 Document Control

description: Indicates whether a Lost Time Injury (LTI) occurred on site on this log date. True triggers mandatory incident reporting to Intelex HSE system, regulatory notification under OSHA, and TRIR (Total Recordable Incident Rate) calculation update.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1904 (Injury and Illness Recordkeeping); ISO 45001; Intelex (Incident Reporting); LTI (Lost Time Injury)

description: Indicates whether a Non-Conformance Report (NCR) was raised on this log date. True triggers integration with the QA/QC management workflow and requires corrective action documentation per ISO 9001.
reference: ISO 9001 (Non-Conformance); NCR (Non-Conformance Report); Procore Daily Logs Module

description: High-level classification of site productivity for the log date. Productive indicates full operations; partially_productive indicates reduced output due to delays or restrictions; non_productive indicates no billable work performed; shutdown indicates planned or emergency site closure.
reference: Procore Daily Logs Module; EVM (Earned Value Management)

description: Number of active Permits to Work (PTW) issued and in force on site for the log date. Supports HSE compliance monitoring, Intelex PTW tracking, and regulatory audit evidence for high-risk work activities.
reference: Intelex (Permit to Work); ISO 45001 (Occupational Health and Safety); OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147

description: Free-text description of the construction activities that were planned for the log date per the project schedule. Compared against work_performed_narrative to identify schedule variances and support look-ahead planning.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6 (Activity Planning); Procore Daily Logs Module

description: Total rainfall or precipitation recorded at the site in millimetres for the log date. Key metric for weather delay claims, earthworks productivity assessment, and EOT substantiation under FIDIC Clause 8.4.
reference: FIDIC Clause 8.4 (Extension of Time); Procore Daily Logs Module

description: Source system identifier for this daily log record in the Procore Construction Management platform. Enables traceability and reconciliation between the Silver Layer data product and the operational system of record.
reference: Procore Daily Logs Module; Data Lineage and Audit

description: Additional free-text remarks or notes entered by the superintendent for items not captured in structured fields or the work performed narrative. Provides supplementary context for project management review and legal record completeness.
reference: Procore Daily Logs Module; FIDIC Clause 20 (Claims)

description: Type of work shift recorded in this daily log. Distinguishes day, night, double-shift, or split-shift operations. Relevant for overtime cost tracking, HSE fatigue management, and productivity benchmarking.
reference: HCSS HeavyJob (Time Tracking); ISO 45001 (Fatigue Management); Viewpoint Vista (Payroll)

description: Operational access status of the construction site for the log date. Closed or restricted access directly impacts productivity and supports delay claim documentation. Partial indicates some work fronts accessible.
reference: Procore Daily Logs Module; OSHA 29 CFR 1926 (Site Safety)

description: Number of subcontractor personnel on site for the log date. Supports subcontractor performance monitoring, site coordination, and contract administration.
reference: Procore Daily Logs Module; HCSS HeavyJob

description: Date and time when the site superintendent formally signed off and submitted the daily log. Establishes the legal record timestamp for the site activity log. Critical for delay claim and EOT substantiation timelines.
reference: Procore Daily Logs Module; FIDIC Clause 20.1 (Contractor's Claims); ISO 9001

description: Indicates whether a Toolbox Meeting (TBM) was conducted on site for the log date. Mandatory HSE compliance indicator. True confirms daily safety briefing was held per SWMS (Safe Work Method Statement) requirements.
reference: ISO 45001 (Occupational Health and Safety); OSHA 29 CFR 1926; TBM (Toolbox Meeting); SWMS (Safe Work Method Statement)

description: Highest recorded ambient temperature at the site in degrees Celsius for the log date. Used for concrete pour suitability assessment, HSE heat stress monitoring, and weather delay substantiation.
reference: ACI 305R (Hot Weather Concreting); ISO 45001 (Occupational Health and Safety); Procore Daily Logs Module

description: Lowest recorded ambient temperature at the site in degrees Celsius for the log date. Used for cold weather concreting assessment, frost protection requirements, and weather delay substantiation.

description: The current construction phase being executed at this work front (e.g., 'Earthworks', 'Foundation', 'Structural', 'MEP Rough-In', 'Finishing', 'Commissioning'). description: Current lifecycle status of the work front. description: Classification of the spatial unit represented by this work front. description: Baseline-scheduled start date for work activities at this work front as defined in the project schedule (Oracle Primavera P6). Used for schedule variance analysis and earned value computation.

Comma-separated list of project types or sectors for which this prequalification is valid (e.g., commercial, infrastructure, energy, residential). Used to filter RFQ distribution.
Date when the prequalification evaluation was completed by the evaluating officer.
Free-text notes and observations recorded by the evaluating officer during the assessment, including strengths, weaknesses, and areas of concern.
Date when the prequalification approval expires and requalification is required. Nullable for rejected or conditional statuses.
Evaluation score assessing the subcontractor's financial strength, liquidity, and ability to fund project work. Typically scored on a 0-100 scale.
Indicates whether the subcontractor's financial statements (balance sheet, P&L, cash flow) have been reviewed and verified by the evaluating officer or finance team.
Geographic regions or countries where the subcontractor is prequalified to operate, based on licensing, local presence, and regulatory compliance.

Evaluation score assessing whether the subcontractor's insurance coverage (general liability, workers compensation, professional indemnity) meets project requirements. Typically scored on a 0-100 scale.
Timestamp when this prequalification record was last modified, supporting audit trail and change tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the maximum contract value.
Maximum trade package contract value the subcontractor is prequalified to bid on, based on financial capacity and bonding limits.
Threshold score required for prequalification approval. Subcontractors scoring below this threshold are rejected or granted conditional approval.
Composite weighted score aggregating all evaluation criteria (financial, safety, performance, technical, bonding, insurance). Used to determine approval decision. Typically scored on a 0-100 scale.
Evaluation score reflecting the subcontractor's track record on previous projects, including quality of work, schedule adherence, and client satisfaction. Typically scored on a 0-100 scale.
Externally-known unique identifier for this prequalification assessment, used in correspondence and RFQ invitations.
Current lifecycle status of the prequalification record, determining whether the subcontractor is eligible to receive RFQs and bid invitations.
Indicates whether reference checks with previous clients or general contractors have been completed and verified as part of the evaluation process.
Detailed explanation of why the prequalification was rejected, including specific criteria failures or compliance gaps. Null for approved applications.

Business event or condition that requires the subcontractor to undergo requalification before expiry date (e.g., major safety incident, financial distress, change in ownership, significant performance failure).

Evaluation score based on the subcontractor's historical safety performance, including TRIR (Total Recordable Incident Rate), LTI (Lost Time Injury) frequency, and HSE compliance. Typically scored on a 0-100 scale.
reference: OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration), ISO 45001 (Occupational Health and Safety)

description: Date when the subcontractor submitted their prequalification application package.
Evaluation score assessing the subcontractor's technical expertise, workforce qualifications, equipment availability, and ability to execute complex trade packages. Typically scored on a 0-100 scale.
Primary trade or specialty category for which the subcontractor is being prequalified (e.g., MEP, civil works, structural steel, concrete, electrical, plumbing, HVAC).
Unique surrogate identifier for the client/owner account record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for the account master entity.

business_glossary_term: Customer Relationship Management (CRM) Account Owner ID
description: Identifier of the internal Salesforce CRM user (business development manager or account executive) responsible for managing this client relationship. Drives pipeline ownership, opportunity assignment, and performance reporting.

Reference to the parent account record for clients that are subsidiaries or divisions within a larger corporate hierarchy. Enables account hierarchy traversal for consolidated reporting and group-level relationship management.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy; IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements

description: Reference to the top-level account in the corporate hierarchy (ultimate holding entity). Used for group-level exposure analysis, consolidated pipeline reporting, and enterprise relationship management.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Hierarchy; IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements

description: Foreign key linking to client.segment. Business justification: An account belongs to a single segment classification; using a foreign key to segment normalizes the model and removes the redundant string column.
Current lifecycle status of the client account. Controls whether the account is eligible for new project bids, contract awards, and CRM pipeline activities.

Classification of the client account by ownership and sector type. Drives contract template selection, compliance requirements, and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: public_sector|private_sector|government_body|corporate_sponsor|jv_entity|ppp_authority — promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Object; FIDIC Contract Classification

description: Reported or estimated annual revenue of the client organisation in the account's billing currency. Used for client segmentation, credit assessment, and strategic account prioritisation.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Object; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: Address to which invoices and financial correspondence are directed for this client account. May differ from the registered address. Used in SAP S/4HANA accounts receivable and Viewpoint Vista job costing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA AR Module; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Level of Building Information Modeling (BIM) mandated by the client for construction projects. Drives design collaboration tooling requirements (e.g., Autodesk BIM 360), data exchange standards, and project delivery methodology.
reference: Autodesk BIM 360; ISO 19650 BIM Standard; BIM (Building Information Modeling) Level Definitions

description: Strategic segmentation tier assigned to the client based on revenue potential, relationship depth, and strategic importance. Tier 1 represents the highest-value clients. Drives account management resource allocation and executive engagement levels.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Object; PMO Client Segmentation Framework

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country of incorporation or primary operations for the client organisation. Used for jurisdictional compliance, tax treatment, and geographic segmentation.
Timestamp when the account record was first created in the source Salesforce CRM system. Represents the audit trail creation event for the master client record.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Object; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Confidentiality)

description: Maximum outstanding receivable balance approved for this client account. Used by the finance team to manage exposure and payment risk on construction contracts. Expressed in the account's billing currency.
reference: IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards; SAP S/4HANA AR Module

description: External credit rating assigned to the client organisation by a recognised credit rating agency (e.g., S&P, Moody's, Fitch) or internal credit assessment score. Used for financial risk evaluation, payment terms negotiation, and bonding/surety decisions.
reference: IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards; PCI DSS

description: Source system identifier for the account as recorded in Salesforce CRM. Used for cross-system reconciliation and lineage tracing back to the operational system of record.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the primary billing currency for this client account (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, AED). Used for contract valuation, invoicing, and financial reporting.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: Indicates whether outbound marketing and unsolicited commercial communications to this client account are restricted. Set to True when the client has opted out of communications or is subject to legal/regulatory restrictions. Supports GDPR compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 7 (Consent); GDPR Article 21 (Right to Object); Privacy Regulations

description: Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet Data Universal Numbering System identifier for the client organisation. Used for credit assessment, supplier/client qualification, and global entity resolution.
Approximate number of employees in the client organisation. Used as a proxy for organisational scale, procurement capacity, and client segmentation in CRM analytics.

Client's standard policy for granting Extension of Time (EOT) on construction contracts. Indicates whether the client follows standard FIDIC provisions, applies strict timelines, or allows flexible negotiation. Used in bid risk evaluation.
reference: FIDIC General Conditions of Contract; EOT (Extension of Time) Provisions

business_glossary_term: Health Safety and Environment (HSE) Compliance Required Flag
description: Indicates whether this client mandates specific Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) compliance standards (e.g., ISO 45001, OSHA requirements, client-specific HSE plans) as a condition of contract. Drives HSE planning and prequalification requirements.
reference: OSHA (Occupational Safety and Health Administration); ISO 45001; ISO 14001

description: Primary industry sector of the client organisation (e.g., Infrastructure, Energy, Real Estate, Transportation, Healthcare, Government). Used for market segmentation and opportunity pipeline analytics in Salesforce CRM.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Object; NAICS Industry Classification

description: Indicates whether this client account represents a Joint Venture (JV) entity formed specifically for a construction project or programme. JV entities require special contract administration, profit-sharing arrangements, and consolidated reporting.
reference: FIDIC Joint Venture Provisions; IFRS 11 Joint Arrangements

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the account record in the source Salesforce CRM system. Used for change data capture, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Object; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Confidentiality)

description: Date of the most recent contract or project award from this client. Used to identify dormant accounts, measure relationship recency, and prioritise re-engagement activities in the CRM pipeline.
reference: Salesforce CRM Opportunity Object; Oracle Primavera P6

description: Indicates whether this client typically enforces Liquidated Damages (LD) clauses in construction contracts, and whether the LD terms are standard or negotiable. Informs bid risk assessment and contract strategy.
reference: FIDIC General Conditions of Contract; LD (Liquidated Damages) Clause

business_glossary_term: Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design (LEED) Certification Required Flag
description: Indicates whether this client requires LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) certification for construction projects. Drives design, procurement, and construction methodology decisions to meet green building standards.
reference: LEED (U.S. Green Building Council); ISO 14001 Environmental Management Systems

description: Full registered legal name of the client/owner organisation as it appears on contracts, invoices, and regulatory filings. This is the authoritative identity label for the account.
reference: FIDIC General Conditions of Contract; IFRS/GAAP Entity Identification Standards

description: Describes the client's internal authority level or approval threshold required to issue a Notice to Proceed (NTP) on construction contracts. Used in contract administration to understand client governance and approval workflows.
reference: FIDIC General Conditions of Contract; NTP (Notice to Proceed) Standard

description: Standard payment terms agreed with the client for construction contracts (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, milestone-based, progress payment). Drives accounts receivable management and cash flow forecasting in SAP S/4HANA.
reference: FIDIC General Conditions of Contract; SAP S/4HANA AR Module; IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Client's preferred contract delivery model for construction engagements. Options include Engineering Procurement and Construction (EPC), Guaranteed Maximum Price (GMP), Design-Bid-Build (DBB), Design-Build (DB), Public-Private Partnership (PPP), and Build-Operate-Transfer (BOT). Informs bid strategy and contract template selection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EPC|GMP|DBB|DB|PPP|BOT|FIDIC — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: FIDIC General Conditions of Contract; ASCE Contract Standards

description: Date on which the client's prequalification approval expires and requires renewal. Used to trigger re-assessment workflows and ensure compliance before new contract awards.

description: Current prequalification status of the client as a commissioning entity. Indicates whether the client has been assessed and approved for contract award eligibility, is pending review, or has an expired/rejected qualification.

description: Email address of the primary contact at the client organisation. Used for RFI responses, RFP submissions, contract correspondence, and CRM communication tracking.

description: Full name of the primary point of contact at the client organisation for commercial and contractual matters. Typically the client's project director, procurement manager, or authorised representative.

description: Direct telephone number for the primary contact at the client organisation. Used for urgent project communications, bid clarifications, and relationship management activities.
reference: GDPR Article 4; Salesforce CRM Contact Object

description: Full registered office address of the client organisation as recorded with the relevant business registry. Used for legal correspondence, contract execution, and regulatory filings.
reference: GDPR Article 4; FIDIC General Conditions of Contract

description: Official company or entity registration number issued by the relevant national or state business registry authority. Used for legal entity verification and compliance due diligence.
reference: IBC (International Building Code Council); FIDIC; GDPR Article 4

description: Date on which the business relationship with this client was formally established (e.g., first contract award, first NTP issued, or CRM account creation date). Used for relationship tenure analysis and loyalty segmentation.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Object; FIDIC NTP (Notice to Proceed)

description: Government-issued tax identification number (e.g., EIN, VAT number, GST number) for the client entity. Required for invoicing, contract administration, and financial reporting under IFRS/GAAP.
reference: IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards; IRS EIN; VAT Directive

description: Operating or brand name used by the client organisation in day-to-day business, which may differ from the registered legal name. Used in CRM displays and correspondence.
Official website address of the client organisation. Used for due diligence, background research, and CRM account enrichment.
Unique surrogate identifier for the account credit profile record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.
Reference to the client account for which this credit profile applies. Links to the master client account entity in the client domain.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Customer Master; Salesforce CRM Account Object

business_glossary_term: Credit Approved By Employee Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Credit approval workflow tracks the approving HR employee, enabling audit and compliance reports.

Contractually approved payment terms for this client account, governing the number of days within which invoices must be settled or the basis on which payments are triggered. Milestone-based and retention-release terms are common in EPC and FIDIC contracts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_30|net_45|net_60|net_90|milestone_based|retention_release|advance_payment|progress_claim — promote to reference product]
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract; SAP S/4HANA Payment Terms (FI-AP/AR); IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition

description: Business Development (BD) team's qualitative assessment of the strategic importance and relationship health of this client account, used as input to the internal credit scoring process. Strategic key accounts may receive more favourable credit terms.
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Management; Internal Credit Policy

description: Business segment classification of the client account for credit risk segmentation and portfolio analysis. Supports differentiated credit policies for Public-Private Partnership (PPP), Build-Operate-Transfer (BOT), Joint Venture (JV), and private developer clients. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: public_sector|private_sector|ppp|bot|jv_partner|developer|utility|epc_owner|multilateral — promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce CRM Account Segmentation; Internal Credit Policy; FIDIC

description: Timestamp when this credit profile record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; GDPR Article 5 (Data Integrity and Accountability)

description: Date on which the current credit limit and payment terms were formally approved by the authorised finance approver.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Credit Management (FI-AR-CR); Internal Credit Policy; SOX Internal Controls

description: Date on which the credit hold was applied to this client account. Null if no credit hold is currently active.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Credit Management (FI-AR-CR); Internal Credit Policy

description: Indicates whether this client account is currently placed on credit hold, blocking the issuance of new contracts, purchase orders, or progress claims until the hold is resolved. True = credit hold active; False = normal trading.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Credit Management (FI-AR-CR); Internal Credit Policy

description: Reason code explaining why the credit hold was applied to this client account. Populated only when credit_hold_flag is True. Supports root-cause analysis and escalation workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Credit Management (FI-AR-CR); Internal Credit Policy

description: Indicates whether trade credit insurance has been obtained to cover the financial exposure to this client account. True = credit insurance in place; False = no credit insurance.
reference: Internal Credit Policy; IFRS 9; Euler Hermes / Coface Trade Credit Insurance

description: Maximum insured amount covered by the trade credit insurance policy for this client account. Populated only when credit_insurance_flag is True.
reference: Internal Credit Policy; IFRS 9; Euler Hermes / Coface Trade Credit Insurance

description: Name of the trade credit insurance provider covering the exposure to this client account (e.g., Euler Hermes, Coface, Atradius). Populated only when credit_insurance_flag is True.

Maximum approved credit exposure the business is willing to extend to this client account at any point in time, expressed in the profile currency. Used in contract terms negotiation and financial risk management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Credit Management (FI-AR-CR); IFRS 9; GAAP ASC 310

description: Free-text explanation of the reason for the most recent change to the credit limit, such as improved payment history, new contract award, rating upgrade, or financial distress. Supports audit trail and governance requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Credit Management (FI-AR-CR); Internal Audit; SOX Internal Controls

description: Frequency at which this client account's credit profile is formally reviewed. 'triggered' indicates review is event-driven (e.g., new contract award, overdue threshold breach) rather than calendar-based.
reference: Internal Credit Policy; SAP S/4HANA Credit Management (FI-AR-CR)

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the credit limit, overdue balance, and all monetary fields on this profile are denominated (e.g., USD, GBP, AED, AUD).
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: Total current financial exposure to this client account, comprising all open invoices (billed but unpaid), unbilled certified work, and retention receivables. Used to assess utilisation against the approved credit limit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Credit Management (FI-AR-CR); IFRS 9; GAAP ASC 310

description: Average number of days this client account takes to pay invoices, calculated over the trailing review period. DSO (Days Sales Outstanding) is a key accounts receivable metric used to assess payment behaviour and cash flow risk. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA AR aging analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Accounts Receivable (FI-AR); IFRS 9; GAAP ASC 310; Viewpoint Vista Job Costing

description: Date from which the current credit profile terms (credit limit, payment terms, rating) became effective. Supports temporal tracking of credit profile changes over time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Credit Management (FI-AR-CR); IFRS 9; Internal Credit Policy

description: Date until which the current credit profile terms are valid. Null for open-ended profiles. Triggers a review workflow when the current date approaches this date.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Credit Management (FI-AR-CR); IFRS 9; Internal Credit Policy

description: Credit rating assigned to the client by an external credit rating agency such as Dun & Bradstreet (D&B), Standard & Poor's (S&P), Moody's, or Fitch. Examples: AAA, AA+, BBB-, Ba1, 5A1 (D&B PAYDEX). Null if no external rating is available.
reference: Dun & Bradstreet (D&B) Credit Rating; S&P Global Ratings; Moody's; IFRS 9

description: Name of the external credit rating agency that issued the external_credit_rating for this client account. Enables multi-agency comparison and source traceability.
reference: Dun & Bradstreet; S&P Global; Moody's; Fitch Ratings; IFRS 9

description: Date on which the external credit rating was issued or last confirmed by the rating agency. Used to assess the currency and relevance of the external rating.
reference: Dun & Bradstreet; S&P Global Ratings; IFRS 9

description: Internally calculated credit score assigned to this client account by the finance team, typically on a scale of 0–100 or 0–1000, reflecting payment history, financial strength, project delivery track record, and relationship context provided by Business Development (BD). Higher scores indicate lower credit risk.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Credit Management (FI-AR-CR); Internal Credit Policy; IFRS 9

description: Date on which the most recent formal credit review was completed by the finance team. Triggers periodic reassessment workflows and ensures credit profiles remain current.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Credit Management (FI-AR-CR); IFRS 9; Internal Credit Policy

description: Maximum financial exposure to Liquidated Damages (LD) that the client could apply against the business under active contracts, based on contractual LD rates and project delay risk. Informs credit risk and P&L exposure assessments.
reference: FIDIC Conditions of Contract Clause 8.7; SAP S/4HANA Project Systems; IFRS 15

description: Scheduled date for the next periodic credit review of this client account. Supports proactive credit management and ensures timely reassessment before contract renewals or new bid submissions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Credit Management (FI-AR-CR); Internal Credit Policy

description: Total outstanding invoice balance that has exceeded the approved payment terms due date as of the last credit review or system refresh. A key indicator for credit hold decisions and risk escalation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Accounts Receivable (FI-AR); IFRS 9 Expected Credit Loss; GAAP ASC 310

description: Qualitative rating of the client's historical payment behaviour based on analysis of invoice settlement patterns, frequency of late payments, and dispute history. Derived from AR aging data in SAP S/4HANA and Viewpoint Vista.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Accounts Receivable (FI-AR); Viewpoint Vista Job Costing; IFRS 9

description: Numeric representation of the approved payment terms expressed as the number of calendar days from invoice date to due date (e.g., 30 for net-30, 60 for net-60). Complements the approved_payment_terms field for precise DSO calculation and aging analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Payment Terms Configuration; IFRS 15

description: Credit limit that was in effect prior to the most recent credit review. Enables trend analysis and audit trail of credit limit changes over time.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Credit Management (FI-AR-CR); IFRS 9; Internal Audit

description: Externally-known alphanumeric reference code assigned to this credit profile, used in correspondence with finance teams and credit agencies. Format: CP- followed by 6-12 alphanumeric characters.

description: Current lifecycle status of the credit profile. 'active' indicates normal trading terms apply; 'credit_hold' indicates all new work or invoicing is blocked pending resolution of overdue balances or risk concerns; 'under_review' indicates a periodic or triggered reassessment is in progress.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Credit Management (FI-AR-CR); IFRS 9 Expected Credit Loss

description: Percentage of each progress claim withheld by the client as retention money under the contract, typically released upon practical completion and expiry of the Defects Liability Period (DLP). Standard construction retention rates range from 5% to 10%.

description: Operational system of record from which this credit profile data was sourced or last updated. Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the sovereign government entity when sovereign_risk_flag is True. Used for country-level risk aggregation and geopolitical exposure reporting.

description: Timestamp when this credit profile record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each parent-child account hierarchy relationship record in the construction client domain.

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this hierarchy relationship, used for cross-system referencing in SAP S/4HANA and Salesforce CRM integrations.

description: Numeric depth of the child account within the corporate hierarchy tree. Level 1 represents the ultimate parent (root), with each subsequent level incrementing by 1. Enables consolidated reporting across multi-tier enterprise client groups.

description: Full materialized path string representing the ancestry chain from root to child account (e.g., '/1001/1045/1078'). Supports efficient tree traversal queries and consolidated reporting without recursive joins.

description: Qualitative assessment of the current health of the business relationship with this contact, as evaluated by the account manager or business development team. Used in CRM pipeline forecasting and client retention strategies.

description: Formal honorific or title prefix for the contact (e.g., Mr., Ms., Dr.) used in official correspondence, contract documents, and client communications.

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the Joint Venture (JV) structure record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.

description: Timestamp when the JV structure record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting.

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the primary currency in which the JV's financial obligations, equity contributions, and profit/loss distributions are denominated.

description: Specific legal jurisdiction or state/province within the governing law country that applies to the JV agreement (e.g., 'New South Wales', 'Dubai International Financial Centre'). Supplements governing_law_country for sub-national jurisdictions.

Boolean flag indicating whether the JV structure record is currently active and operational. False indicates the JV has been dissolved, superseded, or administratively deactivated. Used for data filtering in reporting and CRM pipeline views.

Boolean flag indicating whether the lead sponsor of the JV is an internal entity (i.e., Construction itself or one of its subsidiaries) as opposed to an external third-party organisation. Drives internal vs. external reporting segmentation.

description: Document reference number or identifier for the executed JV agreement, as registered in Aconex document management or the contract administration system. Used to trace the legal instrument governing the JV.

description: Abbreviated or commonly used name for the JV, used in internal reporting, dashboards, and project management systems such as Oracle Primavera P6 and Procore.

Current lifecycle status of the JV arrangement. 'formation' indicates the JV is being constituted; 'pending' indicates awaiting regulatory or client approval; 'active' indicates fully operational; 'suspended' indicates temporarily halted; 'dissolved' indicates the JV has been wound up.

description: Classification of the JV arrangement by delivery model or legal structure. business_glossary_term: Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design (LEED) Certification Target

description: Legal liability arrangement between JV partners as defined in the JV agreement. 'joint_and_several' means each partner is fully liable; 'several_only' means each partner is liable only for their share. Critical for risk management and contract administration.

description: Type of governance and management structure adopted by the JV. description: Free-text field for capturing additional context, special conditions, or commentary about the JV arrangement that is not captured in structured fields. Used by contract administrators and project managers.

description: Total number of participating entities (partners or members) in the JV arrangement. Used for governance reporting and to validate completeness of the JV participant roster.

description: Basis on which profits and losses are distributed among JV participants. 'equity_proportional' aligns P&L distribution with equity share percentages; 'negotiated' indicates a bespoke arrangement defined in the JV agreement.

description: Primary industry sector of the construction project(s) associated with this JV. Used for client segmentation, pipeline forecasting in Salesforce CRM, and portfolio analytics. description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country in which the JV entity is legally registered. May differ from the governing law country for cross-border projects.
Official company or entity registration number issued by the relevant government authority for the incorporated JV entity. Applicable for incorporated JV types; null for unincorporated consortia.

description: Name of the operational system of record from which this JV structure record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking and reconciliation across Salesforce CRM, Aconex, SAP S/4HANA, and Procore.

description: The client's indicative target date for issuing the Notice to Proceed (NTP) or contract award, as stated in the RFP. Used for resource planning, bid team scheduling, and pipeline forecasting in Salesforce CRM.
reference: FIDIC Procurement Guidelines; Oracle Primavera P6; Salesforce CRM

description: The client's required or indicative project completion date as stated in the RFP. Defines the overall programme duration and is a key input to CPM (Critical Path Method) scheduling and resource planning.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6; FIDIC Procurement Guidelines; ASCE

description: The client's indicative target date for project commencement (Notice to Proceed / NTP) as stated in the RFP. Used for resource mobilisation planning and schedule baseline development in Oracle Primavera P6.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6; FIDIC Procurement Guidelines

description: The bid bond (tender guarantee) value expressed as a percentage of the estimated contract value, as required by the client in the RFP. Typically ranges from 1% to 5% of contract value. Null if bid bond is not required.
Indicates whether the client requires a bid bond (tender guarantee) to be submitted with the proposal (True) or not (False). Bid bonds protect the client against bidder withdrawal after award.

The number of calendar days from the submission deadline during which the bidder's proposal must remain valid and binding, as specified in the RFP. Typically 60 to 180 days. Impacts financial exposure and resource commitment.
The BIM maturity level required by the client in the RFP: level_1 (CAD-based), level_2 (collaborative BIM with federated models), or level_3 (fully integrated open BIM). Null if BIM is not required.

Indicates whether the client mandates the use of BIM (Building Information Modeling) for design and construction delivery as specified in the RFP (True) or not (False). Impacts technology requirements and Autodesk BIM 360 platform usage.

Classification of the client/owner by sector: public (government/public authority), private (private developer/corporation), ppp (Public-Private Partnership arrangement), or jv (Joint Venture client entity). Drives compliance, reporting, and bid strategy.
reference: FIDIC Procurement Guidelines; IFRS/GAAP; Internal Client Domain

description: The percentage weighting assigned to the commercial/price component of the bid evaluation, as specified in the RFP (e.g., 40.00 for 40%). Combined with technical_score_weight to total 100%. Null for quality-only evaluations.

The pricing and risk-sharing mechanism for the contract as specified in the RFP: lump_sum (fixed price), unit_rate (BOQ-based), cost_plus (reimbursable), target_cost (incentivised), or framework (call-off arrangement). Directly influences bid strategy and cost estimation.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country where the project is located (e.g., USA, GBR, AUS). Used for regulatory compliance, currency, and jurisdictional reporting.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the contract value and financial terms stated in the RFP (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, AUD). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and IFRS/GAAP compliance.

The duration of the Defects Liability Period (DLP) in calendar days as specified in the RFP, commencing from the date of practical completion. Typically 365 or 730 days. Impacts warranty provisioning and post-completion resource planning.

The contractual delivery model specified in the RFP: DBB (Design-Bid-Build), DB (Design-Build), EPC (Engineering Procurement and Construction), GMP (Guaranteed Maximum Price), PPP (Public-Private Partnership), BOT (Build-Operate-Transfer), EPCM (Engineering Procurement Construction Management), or CM (Construction Management). Determines risk allocation and bid structure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DBB|DB|EPC|GMP|PPP|BOT|EPCM|CM — promote to reference product]

Client's published or internally estimated value of the contract in the nominated currency. Used for bid/no-bid decision-making, bonding requirements, and pipeline forecasting. May be client-disclosed or internally estimated.
reference: IFRS 15; FIDIC Procurement Guidelines; Salesforce CRM Pipeline Forecasting

description: The overall evaluation methodology specified in the RFP for assessing and ranking bids: price_only (lowest price wins), price_quality (weighted price and technical), quality_only (technical merit), best_value (whole-life cost), or pass_fail (compliance-based). Drives bid strategy.
The date on which the RFP/RFQ/ITT was formally issued and released to prospective bidders by the client. Represents the principal business event date for this solicitation record.
reference: FIDIC Procurement Guidelines; Aconex Document Management

description: The system timestamp when this RFP issuance record was first created in the data platform. Represents the audit creation time, distinct from the business issue_date. Used for data lineage and audit trail.
reference: GDPR Article 5; ISO 9001; Internal Data Governance

description: The system timestamp of the most recent modification to this RFP issuance record. Used for change tracking, data synchronisation, and audit trail compliance.
reference: GDPR Article 5; ISO 9001; Internal Data Governance

description: The date of the most recent addendum issued by the client against this RFP. Null if no addenda have been issued. Used to ensure bidders are working from the latest version of the solicitation document.
reference: Aconex Document Management; FIDIC Procurement Guidelines

description: The minimum LEED certification level required by the client as stated in the RFP: certified, silver, gold, or platinum. Null if LEED certification is not required.

Indicates whether the client has specified a LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design) certification requirement for the project in the RFP (True) or not (False). Impacts design, materials, and cost estimation.
Indicates whether the RFP specifies that Liquidated Damages (LD) will apply for delays or non-performance (True) or not (False). LD clauses are a key risk factor in bid pricing and schedule planning.

The daily Liquidated Damages (LD) rate specified in the RFP, expressed in the contract currency. Applied for each calendar day of delay beyond the contractual completion date. Null if LD is not applicable.

Minimum percentage of local workforce, materials, or subcontractors required by the client as specified in the RFP. Common in public-sector and government-funded projects. Impacts procurement and subcontractor strategy.
reference: FIDIC Procurement Guidelines; EPA; Internal Procurement Policy

description: The performance bond value expressed as a percentage of the contract value, as specified in the RFP. Typically ranges from 5% to 10% of contract value. Null if performance bond is not required.
Indicates whether the client requires a performance bond to be provided upon contract award (True) or not (False). Performance bonds guarantee the contractor will fulfil contractual obligations.
Scheduled date and time of the mandatory or optional pre-bid conference/site visit where the client clarifies scope and answers bidder questions. Attendance may be a prerequisite for bid eligibility.
reference: FIDIC Procurement Guidelines; Procore Construction Management

description: Indicates whether attendance at the pre-bid meeting is mandatory for bid eligibility (True) or optional (False). Mandatory attendance is a common client requirement for complex infrastructure projects.

Detailed narrative description of the project scope, works, and deliverables as outlined in the RFP. Includes key scope elements such as civil works, MEP systems, infrastructure type, and geographic extent.
Geographic location or address of the proposed project site as stated in the RFP. Used for site visit planning, logistics assessment, and regional market analysis.

The industry sector classification of the project as described in the RFP. Used for market segmentation, capability matching, and portfolio analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: infrastructure|energy|commercial|residential|industrial|transport|water|healthcare|education|defence — promote to reference product]

The official name or title of the project as stated in the RFP document. Provides the primary human-readable description of the work being solicited (e.g., 'Highway 45 Expansion Phase 2', 'Airport Terminal C Construction').
reference: FIDIC Procurement Guidelines; Procore Construction Management

description: The document control reference number or URL/path to the official RFP package in the document management system (Aconex or Autodesk BIM 360). Enables direct retrieval of the source solicitation document.

Externally-known reference number assigned by the client to uniquely identify this solicitation document (RFP, RFQ, or ITT). Used in all correspondence and bid submissions. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Bid Management or Aconex Document Management.

Current lifecycle status of the RFP issuance: draft (being prepared), issued (released to market), amended (addendum issued), closed (submission deadline passed), cancelled (withdrawn by client), or awarded (contract awarded). Drives workflow and reporting.
reference: FIDIC Procurement Guidelines; Procore Change Orders

description: Classification of the solicitation document type: RFP (Request for Proposal), RFQ (Request for Quotation), ITT (Invitation to Tender), EOI (Expression of Interest), or RFI (Request for Information). Determines the formality and evaluation process applied.

The exact date and time by which all bid/proposal submissions must be received by the client. Late submissions are typically disqualified. Critical for bid planning and scheduling in Oracle Primavera P6.
reference: FIDIC Procurement Guidelines; Oracle Primavera P6

description: The percentage weighting assigned to the technical/quality component of the bid evaluation, as specified in the RFP (e.g., 60.00 for 60%). Combined with commercial_score_weight to total 100%. Null for price-only evaluations.
Unique system-generated identifier for each client satisfaction survey record. Primary key for the client_survey data product.
Reference to the client account (project owner, developer, or sponsor) against whom this survey is administered. Links to the master client account record in the CRM.
Reference to the construction project associated with this survey. Enables project-level relationship health tracking and milestone-based survey correlation.
