description: Reference to the internal account manager or relationship owner responsible for administering this survey and managing the client relationship. Links to the workforce/employee master record.

description: Reference to the individual stakeholder contact at the client organisation who completed or responded to this survey.

description: The date on which the survey was formally administered or distributed to the client respondent. Principal business event date for this transaction.
The channel or method through which the survey was administered to the client respondent (e.g., email link, online portal, telephone interview, face-to-face meeting, or structured workshop).

description: Sector classification of the client at the time of survey, distinguishing between public-sector, private-sector, Public-Private Partnership (PPP), Build-Operate-Transfer (BOT), and Joint Venture (JV) arrangements. Enables sector-level satisfaction benchmarking.

description: Timestamp when the client survey record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and record provenance.

description: Indicates whether the survey results have triggered an escalation to senior management or executive leadership due to critically low scores or identified relationship risks.

Detailed narrative explanation of the circumstances and events that led to the back-charge. Includes specifics of the subcontractor default, defective work details, or damage caused.

Final outcome of the back-charge after any dispute resolution process. Reference to the client (project owner) to whom this invoice is issued. Links to the client master data product.
Reference to the construction contract under which this invoice is raised. Links to the contract master agreement product.
Reference to the construction project for which work is being billed. Links to the construction project master data product.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Accounts receivable invoices should be linked to a cost center for reporting and allocation purposes.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Accounts receivable invoice posts to GL; FK provides authoritative account data.
Reference to the specific WBS element being billed. Enables detailed cost tracking and revenue allocation at the work package level.
Classification of the invoice based on the number of days past the due date. Used for accounts receivable aging analysis and collection prioritization.
Cumulative amount received from the client against this invoice. Updated as partial or full payments are applied.
End date of the work period covered by this invoice. Defines the cutoff for work included in this billing cycle.
Start date of the work period covered by this invoice. Used for progress billing and percentage-of-completion revenue recognition.
Client-provided reference number or purchase order number that must appear on the invoice for their accounts payable processing.
Timestamp when this invoice record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this invoice (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, AED).
Number of calendar days the invoice is overdue. Calculated as current date minus due date for unpaid invoices past their due date.
Date the client formally raised a dispute against this invoice. Null if no dispute exists.
Indicates whether the invoice is currently under dispute by the client. True if disputed, False otherwise.
Description of the reason the client has disputed this invoice. Null if no dispute exists.
The contractual date by which payment is expected from the client. Used for aging bucket classification and late payment penalty calculations.
Document number assigned by the ERP system when the invoice was posted to the general ledger. Used for audit trail and reconciliation.
Date the invoice was posted to the general ledger in the ERP system. Used for financial period reconciliation.
Total invoice amount before tax and retention deductions. Represents the certified value of work completed or milestone achieved.
The date the invoice was formally issued to the client. This is the principal business event timestamp for revenue recognition and aging calculations.
Detailed description of the work or milestone being billed. Provides context for the invoice line items and supports client review.
Externally-known unique invoice number issued to the client. This is the business identifier printed on the invoice document and referenced in payment correspondence.

Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the accounts receivable workflow. Tracks progression from draft through payment to closure or dispute resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|partially_paid|paid|overdue|disputed|cancelled|written_off — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Classification of the invoice based on the billing trigger and contract terms. Distinguishes between regular progress claims, milestone payments, variation orders, and retention releases. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: progress_billing|milestone|advance_payment|retention_release|variation_order|final_account|credit_note|debit_note — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Date of the most recent payment received against this invoice. Null if no payments have been received.
Timestamp when this invoice record was last modified. Updated whenever any field changes. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Net amount receivable from the client after deducting retention and adding tax. This is the amount expected to be paid by the due date.
Remaining unpaid balance on the invoice. Calculated as net receivable amount minus amount paid to date.
Date the payment certificate was issued by the engineer or client representative. Typically precedes the invoice date.
Reference number of the payment certificate issued by the engineer or client representative approving the work value. This certificate authorizes the invoice issuance.
The payment instrument or method used by the client to settle this invoice (e.g., bank transfer, wire transfer, check, letter of credit).

Contractual payment terms agreed with the client, defining the number of days from invoice date to due date (e.g., Net 30 means payment due within 30 days). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_7|net_15|net_30|net_45|net_60|net_90|due_on_receipt|custom — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Amount withheld by the client as retention per contract terms. Typically a percentage of the gross amount held until project completion or defects liability period (DLP) expiry.
The percentage of the gross amount withheld as retention. Typically ranges from 5% to 10% per FIDIC standard contract terms.
Accounting method used for revenue recognition on this invoice. Construction typically uses percentage-of-completion per IFRS 15 and GAAP ASC 606.
Amount of revenue recognized in the financial statements for this invoice. May differ from gross amount based on revenue recognition method and contract terms.
Total tax (VAT, GST, or sales tax) applied to the invoice. Calculated based on applicable tax rates and jurisdictional rules.
The tax rate percentage applied to calculate the tax amount. Varies by jurisdiction and project location.
Amount written off as uncollectible bad debt. Null if not written off.
Date the outstanding balance was written off as bad debt. Null if not written off.
Indicates whether the outstanding balance has been written off as uncollectible. True if written off, False otherwise.
Unique identifier for the cash flow forecast record.

Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Required for cash flow forecasting per contract to align financing with project cash needs; contracts define payment terms used in cash flow models.
Foreign key linking to sustainability.climate_risk_assessment. Business justification: Cash‑flow forecasts must incorporate quantified climate‑risk financial impacts for risk‑adjusted planning.
Reference to the construction project for which this cash flow forecast is prepared. Null for corporate-level forecasts.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cash flow forecasts are prepared per cost center to track cash impact of cost allocations.
Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Cash‑flow forecasts are prepared by a finance analyst; linking to employee enables responsibility tracking and variance analysis.
Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_change. Business justification: Forecasts incorporate cash‑flow impact of specific regulatory changes; linking provides the necessary change reference.
Reference to the specific WBS element if the forecast is prepared at a granular work package level. Null for project-level or corporate-level forecasts.
Forecasted drawdown of advance payments or mobilization advances from the client, typically secured by advance payment guarantees.
Date on which the forecast was formally approved for use in financial planning and decision-making.
Name or identifier of the individual who approved the forecast, typically the project manager, finance director, or CFO.
Forecasted payments for performance bonds, advance payment guarantees, and other surety instruments required by the contract.
Percentage of total bonding capacity utilized by this forecast, relevant for corporate-level forecasts to monitor surety capacity constraints.
Projected cash balance at the end of the forecast period, calculated as opening balance plus net cash flow.
Timestamp when this forecast record was first created in the system.
Percentage of available credit facility or working capital line utilized to meet the peak funding requirement, relevant for corporate treasury management.
Cumulative cash position at the end of the forecast period, representing the running total of net cash flows from project inception or corporate fiscal year start.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this forecast (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Forecasted payments for equipment rental and hire charges, including plant, machinery, and temporary facilities.
Key assumptions underlying the forecast, including payment term assumptions, progress rate assumptions, inflation factors, and risk contingencies.
The date on which this forecast was prepared or last updated, representing the as-of date for the forecast assumptions.

The time interval granularity at which cash flows are forecasted: daily for short-term liquidity management, weekly for near-term project cash planning, monthly for standard project forecasting, or quarterly for long-term corporate planning.
Business-readable identifier for the forecast, typically combining project code, forecast period, and version (e.g., PRJ-2024-001-V3).
The end date of the period covered by this cash flow forecast.
The start date of the period covered by this cash flow forecast.
Current lifecycle status of the forecast: draft for work-in-progress, submitted for review, approved for active use, superseded when replaced by a newer version, or archived for historical reference.

Classification of the forecast scope: project-level for individual construction projects, corporate-level for enterprise-wide treasury forecasting, WBS-level for granular work package forecasts, or contract-level for specific contract cash management.
Version number of the forecast, incremented with each revision to track forecast evolution and variance analysis over time.
Total projected cash inflows for the forecast period, including client milestone payments, retention releases, advance drawdowns, and other receipts.
Total projected cash outflows for the forecast period, including subcontractor payments, material procurement, payroll, equipment hire, bond premiums, and other disbursements.
Timestamp when this forecast record was last updated, supporting audit trail and version control.
Forecasted payments for material purchases and procurement, based on Purchase Orders (PO) and supplier payment terms.
Forecasted client milestone payments expected during the period, based on contract payment schedules and progress billing milestones.
Net cash flow for the forecast period, calculated as forecasted inflows minus forecasted outflows, indicating the expected cash position change.
Additional notes, comments, or explanations regarding the forecast, including significant changes from prior versions or special circumstances.
Cash balance at the beginning of the forecast period, serving as the starting point for the period's cash flow projection.
Forecasted payroll disbursements for direct labor and site personnel, including wages, benefits, and statutory contributions.
Maximum negative cash position expected during the forecast period, indicating the highest working capital or credit facility draw required.
Name or identifier of the individual or team who prepared the forecast, typically the project controls team or corporate treasury analyst.
Forecasted release of retention amounts held by the client, typically released upon project completion or after the Defects Liability Period (DLP).

Classification of the cash flow profile shape: front-loaded for projects with high early expenditure, linear for steady cash flow, back-loaded for projects with late-stage heavy spending, or custom for non-standard profiles.
Forecasted payments to subcontractors for work performed, based on subcontract payment terms and progress certificates.
Percentage variance between current and prior forecast, calculated as (current - prior) / prior * 100, indicating forecast volatility.
Difference between the current forecast net cash flow and the previous forecast version for the same period, used for forecast accuracy tracking and trend analysis.
Difference between forecasted outflows and inflows timing, representing the working capital financing need to bridge payment obligations before receipt of client payments.
Primary key for invoice
Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Invoices for permit fees must reference the specific permit to satisfy audit and regulatory reconciliation.
Reference to the construction project to which this invoice is charged. Enables job costing and project-level financial reporting.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Accounts payable invoices must be assigned to a cost center for budget control and reporting.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_code. Business justification: Invoice cost_code column is denormalized; linking to cost_code table enables accurate cost‑code reporting and variance analysis.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Accounts payable invoice must be associated with a GL account for posting; FK replaces code string.
Reference to the purchase order or subcontract against which this invoice is submitted. Used for three-way match validation (PO-GR-Invoice).
Reference to the vendor or subcontractor who issued this invoice. Links to the vendor master data in the procurement domain.
Current status of the invoice in the approval workflow. Tracks whether the invoice has been reviewed and approved by authorized personnel per delegation of authority (DOA) matrix.
The name or identifier of the person who approved the invoice for payment. Used for audit trail and accountability.
The date the invoice was approved for payment. Used for workflow metrics and audit trail.
The timestamp when this invoice record was first created in the accounts payable system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the invoice is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency accounting and foreign exchange management.
Any early payment discount or negotiated discount applied to the invoice. Reduces the net payable amount.
Boolean indicator of whether the invoice is currently under dispute. True if there is a disagreement on invoice accuracy, pricing, or delivery that must be resolved before payment.
Free-text description of the reason for the invoice dispute. Captures details of pricing discrepancies, quantity mismatches, quality issues, or other disagreements.
The date by which payment is due per the payment terms. Used for cash flow forecasting, aging reports, and late payment penalty calculation.
The fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year to which this invoice is allocated. Used for monthly/quarterly financial reporting and variance analysis.
The fiscal year to which this invoice is allocated for financial reporting purposes. Used for annual P&L, EBITDA, and budget vs. actual analysis.
The total invoice amount before tax, retention, and other adjustments. Represents the base value of goods or services invoiced.
Boolean indicator of whether the invoice is on hold pending additional information or resolution of issues. True if payment is temporarily suspended.
Free-text description of the reason the invoice is on hold. May include missing documentation, pending approvals, or compliance issues.
The date the invoice was issued by the vendor or subcontractor. This is the principal business event timestamp for the invoice and is used for aging analysis and payment term calculation.
Free-text description of the goods or services covered by this invoice. Provides context for the invoice line items and supports audit and review processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AP Invoice Header Text

description: The externally-known unique invoice number assigned by the vendor or subcontractor. This is the business identifier used for correspondence, payment reference, and three-way matching.

Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the accounts payable workflow. Tracks progression from receipt through approval to payment or dispute resolution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|pending_approval|approved|disputed|on_hold|paid|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

Classification of the invoice type. Standard invoices are for completed deliveries; progress claims are for work-in-progress per percentage of completion; credit/debit notes are adjustments; prepayments are advance payments; final invoices close out a contract.
The timestamp when this invoice record was last modified. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data quality monitoring.
The final amount payable to the vendor after applying tax, retention, discounts, and other adjustments. This is the amount that will be paid or has been paid.
The actual date payment was made to the vendor or subcontractor. Null if invoice is unpaid. Used for cash flow analysis and Days Payable Outstanding (DPO) calculation.
The payment instrument or method used to pay this invoice (e.g., wire transfer, check, ACH, credit card, letter of credit). Used for cash management and reconciliation.
The unique reference number or transaction ID for the payment made against this invoice. Used for bank reconciliation and audit trail.
The agreed payment terms for this invoice (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30). Defines the payment schedule and any early payment discount conditions.
The date the invoice was received and recorded in the accounts payable system. Used for internal processing metrics and aging analysis.
The amount withheld from payment as retention per contract terms. Typically held until project completion or Defects Liability Period (DLP) expiry. Common in construction contracts per FIDIC terms.
The percentage of the invoice amount withheld as retention per contract terms. Typically 5-10% in construction contracts per FIDIC conditions.
The total tax or Value Added Tax (VAT) amount applied to the invoice. Used for tax reporting and compliance with local tax regulations.
The tax or VAT rate applied to this invoice, expressed as a percentage. Used for tax calculation and compliance reporting.
Status of the three-way match validation between Purchase Order (PO), Goods Receipt (GR), and Invoice. Ensures invoice accuracy and prevents overpayment.
Unique identifier for the payment transaction record. Primary key for the payment_record product.
Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_offset. Business justification: Payments for carbon offsets need to reference the specific offset transaction for reconciliation.
Reference to the construction project for which this payment is made or received, enabling project-level cash flow tracking.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Payments are allocated to cost centers for cash‑flow forecasting and cost‑center performance tracking.

description: Foreign key linking to finance.invoice. Business justification: Payments are applied to a specific AP invoice; FK provides direct linkage for reconciliation.
Identifier for the batch payment run in which this payment was processed, used for grouping and reconciliation of multiple payments.
Reference to the company bank account used for the payment transaction (house bank for outgoing, collection account for incoming).

Reference to the vendor or subcontractor receiving the outgoing payment (AP disbursement). Null for incoming payments.
Indicates whether this payment is an advance payment made before work completion or goods delivery.
Date when the payment was approved by the authorized approver, marking a key lifecycle event.

Bank fees and transaction charges incurred for processing the payment, may be borne by payer or payee.

Date when the payment was cleared and confirmed by the bank, indicating successful fund transfer.
Status of the payment in the bank clearing process, indicating whether funds have been successfully transferred.
Construction cost code for categorizing the payment within the project cost structure and WBS (Work Breakdown Structure).
Timestamp when this payment record was first created in the system, for audit trail and data lineage.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment transaction (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Early payment discount or cash discount amount deducted from the gross payment amount, if applicable.
The contractual due date by which the payment should be made or received, based on invoice date and payment terms.
Foreign exchange rate applied to convert the payment amount to the company's functional currency, if multi-currency transaction.

description: General ledger account code to which the payment transaction is posted for financial accounting and P&L reporting.
Comma-separated list of invoice document numbers that are cleared or partially cleared by this payment transaction.
Timestamp when this payment record was last modified or updated, for audit trail and change tracking.
The final net amount paid or received after all adjustments, discounts, withholding taxes, and bank charges.
Indicates whether this is a partial payment that does not fully settle the referenced invoices or obligations.
The gross monetary value of the payment transaction in the transaction currency, before any adjustments or fees.
Approval workflow status for the payment, indicating whether it has been authorized by the appropriate authority levels.
The interface or system channel through which the payment was initiated or received (e.g., ERP system, banking portal, manual entry).
The actual date when the payment was executed or received. This is the principal business event timestamp for the transaction.
Externally-known unique payment document number assigned by the ERP system (SAP S/4HANA or Viewpoint Vista). Used for reconciliation and audit trail.
The financial instrument or mechanism used to execute the payment (e.g., wire transfer, ACH, cheque, cash, LC drawdown).
Free-text notes or comments related to the payment transaction, capturing special instructions or contextual information.
Priority level assigned to the payment for processing sequence and cash flow management.
Business purpose or reason for the payment (e.g., material supply, subcontractor labor, equipment rental, progress billing).
Current lifecycle status of the payment transaction in the payment processing workflow.
Contractual payment terms applicable to this transaction (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30), governing due dates and discounts.
Classification of payment direction: outgoing payments to vendors/subcontractors (AP disbursements) or incoming receipts from clients (AR collections).
Purchase order number associated with the payment, typically for vendor payments linked to procurement transactions.
Date when the payment was successfully reconciled with the bank statement during the bank reconciliation process.
Status of bank reconciliation for this payment, indicating whether it has been matched with bank statement entries.
Detailed remittance information sent to the payee explaining which invoices or obligations are being settled by this payment.
Amount withheld from the payment as retention per contract terms, to be released upon project completion or defects liability period.
The effective date when funds are available or debited in the bank account, used for cash flow and liquidity management.
Tax amount withheld at source as per local tax regulations, deducted from the gross payment amount.

Primary key for cost_center
Unique identifier for the cost center. Primary key for the cost center master data entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Controlling (CO) Module - Cost Center Accounting

description: Foreign key linking to client.account. Business justification: Required for Cost Center Allocation Report per client, enabling profitability analysis by linking each cost center to the client account it serves.

Identifier of the construction project to which this cost center is dedicated. Nullable for non-project cost centers (corporate overhead, regional offices, shared services). Used to link project-specific cost centers to the project master for WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) integration and job costing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Project Systems (PS); Oracle Primavera P6 Integration

description: Identifier of the manager or executive accountable for the financial performance of this cost center. Used for P&L (Profit and Loss) ownership, budget approval workflows, and variance analysis escalation.
reference: SAP SuccessFactors Organizational Management; Internal Financial Accountability Framework

description: Identifier of the parent cost center in the organizational hierarchy. Enables roll-up reporting for regional, divisional, and corporate-level P&L (Profit and Loss) and EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization) analysis. Nullable for top-level cost centers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Hierarchy; Management Reporting Standards

description: Identifier of the profit center to which this cost center is assigned. Profit centers aggregate revenue and costs for internal P&L (Profit and Loss) reporting; cost centers roll up into profit centers for EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization) calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA (Profit Center Accounting); Management Accounting Standards

description: The methodology used to allocate or distribute costs from this cost center to other cost objects (projects, WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) elements, or profit centers). Direct charge posts costs explicitly; activity-based uses cost drivers; headcount and square footage are common overhead allocation bases; revenue-based allocates proportionally to project revenue; none indicates no allocation (terminal cost center).
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Allocation Cycles; Management Accounting Best Practices

description: The approved annual or period budget allocated to this cost center. Used for budget vs. actual variance reporting, CPI (Cost Performance Index) calculation in EVM (Earned Value Management), and financial forecasting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Budget Management; IFRS/GAAP Budgeting Standards

description: The time horizon for which the budget amount is defined. Annual budgets align with fiscal year planning; quarterly and monthly budgets support rolling forecasts; project lifecycle budgets span the duration of a construction project from NTP (Notice to Proceed) to handover.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Budget Management; Internal Financial Planning Standards

description: The business segment or division to which this cost center is assigned (e.g., Infrastructure, Energy, Commercial, Residential). Used for segment reporting under IFRS 8 and internal divisional P&L (Profit and Loss) analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Business Area; IFRS 8 Operating Segments

description: The legal entity or company code to which this cost center belongs, as defined in SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting (FI). Used for statutory reporting, intercompany eliminations, and IFRS/GAAP compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Company Code; IFRS/GAAP Legal Entity Reporting

description: The SAP S/4HANA Controlling (CO) area to which this cost center belongs. Controlling areas represent organizational units for internal management accounting and can span multiple company codes. Used for cross-company cost center reporting and consolidation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Controlling Area; Management Accounting Framework

description: Accounting classification of the cost center for financial treatment. Direct centers charge costs to specific projects or WBS (Work Breakdown Structure) elements; indirect centers allocate costs via overhead rates; overhead centers represent corporate G&A (General and Administrative); capitalized centers accumulate costs for asset creation; expensed centers flow directly to P&L (Profit and Loss).
reference: IFRS/GAAP Cost Accounting Standards; SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA

description: The externally-known alphanumeric code identifying the cost center in financial systems and reports. Used in SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA (Cost Center Accounting) for posting and reporting. Typically follows organizational coding standards (e.g., project-based, regional, functional).
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA; IFRS/GAAP Chart of Accounts Standards

description: Extended free-text description providing additional context about the cost center's purpose, scope, and responsibilities. Used for documentation, audit trails, and onboarding new finance team members.

A user-defined grouping or classification for aggregating related cost centers in reports (e.g., all regional offices, all project cost centers for a specific client, all MEP (Mechanical Electrical and Plumbing) cost centers). Used for flexible reporting hierarchies beyond the standard organizational structure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Cost Center Groups; Internal Reporting Standards

description: The full descriptive name of the cost center, used for human-readable identification in financial reports, dashboards, and P&L (Profit and Loss) statements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA; Internal Financial Reporting Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the cost center. Active centers accept postings; inactive centers are temporarily disabled; planned centers are approved but not yet operational; closed centers are archived and no longer accept transactions; suspended centers are on hold pending review or restructuring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA; Internal Financial Controls

description: Classification of the cost center by its primary business function. Project cost centers track job-specific costs; regional offices track geographic operations; corporate overhead captures non-project administrative costs; shared services represent centralized functions (IT, HR, Finance); support functions include PMO (Project Management Office), procurement, and QA/QC (Quality Assurance/Quality Control); operational centers track site-level or equipment-based activities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA; Management Accounting Best Practices

description: The primary cost element group or cost category that this cost center typically incurs (e.g., labor, materials, equipment, subcontractor, overhead). Used for cost structure analysis and variance reporting by cost type.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Element Accounting; Internal Cost Classification

description: The username or identifier of the system user who created this cost center record. Used for accountability, audit trails, and access control reviews.
reference: SAP S/4HANA User Audit; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: The date and time when this cost center record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails, data lineage tracking, and compliance with financial record-keeping regulations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; IFRS/GAAP Record Retention Standards

description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which this cost center's budget and actuals are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used for multi-currency consolidation and foreign exchange variance analysis.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; SAP S/4HANA Currency Management

description: The primary functional discipline or department this cost center supports. Used for cross-project functional cost analysis, overhead allocation, and shared-service chargeback models. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: engineering|procurement|construction|quality|safety|finance|hr|it|legal|admin — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA Functional Area; Internal Cost Allocation Framework

description: The depth of this cost center in the organizational hierarchy tree. Level 1 represents corporate or top-level centers; higher numbers represent nested project, site, or functional centers. Used for drill-down reporting and aggregation logic.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Hierarchy; Internal Reporting Framework

description: Indicates whether costs incurred in this cost center are billable to external clients or internal projects. True for project cost centers with reimbursable contracts; false for corporate overhead and non-billable support functions. Used for revenue recognition and progress billing logic.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue Recognition; Internal Billing Policy

description: The username or identifier of the system user who last modified this cost center record. Used for change accountability, audit trails, and data quality investigations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA User Audit; Internal Data Governance Policy

description: The date and time when this cost center record was last updated. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and data quality monitoring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail; IFRS/GAAP Record Retention Standards

description: Indicates whether this cost center is locked for new postings. True when the cost center is under audit, pending closure, or frozen for period-end reporting. Used to enforce financial controls and prevent unauthorized transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Posting Controls; Internal Financial Controls

description: The standard overhead rate (as a percentage) applied to direct costs posted to this cost center. Used for indirect cost allocation, GMP (Guaranteed Maximum Price) contract pricing, and full absorption costing. Nullable for cost centers that do not apply overhead rates.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA Overhead Calculation; IFRS/GAAP Cost Accounting

description: Three-letter code representing the geographic region or country where this cost center operates (e.g., USA, GBR, ARE). Used for regional P&L (Profit and Loss) reporting, tax jurisdiction mapping, and geographic segment analysis.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 Country Codes; Internal Geographic Segmentation

description: The physical site, office, or facility location associated with this cost center. Used for site-level cost tracking, field operations reporting, and HSE (Health Safety and Environment) cost allocation.
reference: Internal Site Master Data; GIS (Geographic Information System) Integration

description: The date from which this cost center becomes effective and can accept financial postings. Used for time-dependent cost center master data and period-based reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA; IFRS/GAAP Period Accounting

description: The date until which this cost center remains effective. Nullable for open-ended cost centers. Used to manage cost center lifecycle, project closeout, and organizational restructuring.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA; IFRS/GAAP Period Accounting

description: Primary key for cost_code
FK to finance.cost_center

Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether cost postings to this code require additional approval workflow beyond standard posting authorization. Used for high-risk or high-value cost categories.
reference: Internal Control Standards; Project Cost Control Procedures

description: Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether this cost code can have budget allocated to it. Typically True for leaf-level codes and False for parent/rollup codes.
reference: Project Budget Management Standards; SAP S/4HANA Budget Allocation Rules

description: Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether costs posted to this code should be capitalized as assets on the balance sheet rather than expensed. Critical for Engineering Procurement and Construction (EPC) projects and asset-intensive developments.
reference: IFRS/GAAP Capitalization Standards; IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: The primary classification of the cost code into major expenditure categories. Used for high-level financial reporting and Profit and Loss (P&L) analysis.
reference: Construction Accounting Standards; IFRS/GAAP Cost Classification

description: The unique alphanumeric cost code identifier used across all project accounting systems. This is the externally-known business identifier used in SAP S/4HANA Project Systems and Viewpoint Vista for budget allocation and actual cost posting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Project Systems Cost Element Master; Viewpoint Vista Cost Code Structure

description: Detailed description of the cost code explaining the scope of work, materials, or services covered by this classification. Provides additional context beyond the name for proper cost allocation.
reference: Construction Accounting Standards; Project Cost Management Guidelines

description: The hierarchical level of this cost code within the Cost Breakdown Structure (CBS). Typically ranges from 1 (top-level category) to 4 (detailed line item). Level 1 might be 'Labor', Level 2 'Structural Labor', Level 3 'Steel Erection Labor'.
reference: Cost Breakdown Structure (CBS) Standards; Project Cost Management Guidelines

description: The full descriptive name of the cost code providing human-readable identification of the cost category (e.g., 'Structural Steel Erection', 'Concrete Formwork Labor', 'Electrical Conduit Installation').
reference: Construction Accounting Standards; Viewpoint Vista Cost Code Master

description: Current lifecycle status of the cost code. Active codes are available for budget allocation and cost posting. Inactive codes are retained for historical reporting but cannot be used for new transactions. Deprecated codes have been superseded by newer classifications.
reference: Master Data Management Standards; SAP S/4HANA Cost Element Status Management

description: Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether actual costs can be posted directly to this cost code. Typically True for detailed codes and False for summary/rollup codes.
reference: Construction Accounting Standards; Viewpoint Vista Cost Posting Rules

description: Classification indicating whether the cost is directly attributable to a specific project deliverable (direct) or allocated across multiple activities (indirect/overhead). Critical for job costing and Earned Value Management (EVM) calculations.
reference: Construction Accounting Standards; EVM Guidelines; Job Costing Best Practices

description: The date and time when this cost code record was first created in the system. Part of the standard audit trail for master data management.
reference: Data Governance Standards; ISO 8601 Timestamp Format

description: The two-digit CSI MasterFormat division number (00-49) that this cost code maps to. Provides standardized alignment with construction specifications and Bill of Quantities (BOQ) structures.
reference: CSI MasterFormat 2020; Construction Specifications Institute Standards

description: The engineering or construction discipline associated with this cost code (e.g., 'CIVIL', 'STRUCT', 'MECH', 'ELEC', 'INST' for Mechanical Electrical Plumbing (MEP) disciplines). Used for discipline-specific cost tracking and resource planning.
reference: Engineering Discipline Classification Standards; Construction Specification Institute (CSI) MasterFormat

description: The date after which this cost code is no longer active for new transactions. Null for currently active codes. Used for cost code retirement and historical reporting.
reference: Master Data Management Standards; Temporal Data Management Guidelines

description: The date from which this cost code becomes active and available for use in budget allocation and cost posting. Supports time-based cost code structures and phased project implementations.
reference: Master Data Management Standards; Temporal Data Management Guidelines

description: Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether costs posted to this code are included in Earned Value Management (EVM) calculations including Cost Performance Index (CPI) and Schedule Performance Index (SPI) metrics.
reference: EVM Standards (ANSI/EIA-748); Project Cost Control Guidelines

description: The unique identifier for this cost code in the source system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA Cost Element number, Viewpoint Vista Cost Code ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation and integration.
reference: Enterprise Integration Standards; System Integration Guidelines

description: The General Ledger (GL) account code in SAP S/4HANA Finance that this cost code maps to for financial statement reporting. Enables integration between project costing and corporate accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Finance GL Master; IFRS/GAAP Chart of Accounts Standards

description: The user ID or name of the person who last modified this cost code record. Used for change tracking and data governance.
reference: Data Governance Standards; Change Management Standards

description: The date and time when this cost code record was last modified. Part of the standard audit trail for master data management and change tracking.
reference: Data Governance Standards; ISO 8601 Timestamp Format

description: Free-text field for additional notes, usage guidelines, or special instructions related to this cost code. Provides context for proper application and interpretation.
The cost code of the parent category in the hierarchical Cost Breakdown Structure (CBS). Null for top-level cost codes. Enables roll-up reporting and hierarchical budget analysis.
reference: Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) Standards; Cost Breakdown Structure (CBS) Guidelines

description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the standard rate (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'GBP'). Required for multi-currency projects and international joint ventures.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS/GAAP Multi-Currency Accounting Standards

description: The accounting method used for revenue recognition associated with this cost code. Percentage of completion is the most common method for construction projects under IFRS 15 and ASC 606.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers; ASC 606 Revenue Recognition; Construction Accounting Standards

description: Identifier of the operational system of record from which this cost code was originally sourced or is mastered. Supports multi-system integration and data lineage tracking.
reference: Enterprise Integration Standards; Master Data Management Guidelines

description: The standard cost rate per unit of measure for this cost code. Used for budget estimation, variance analysis, and Earned Value Management (EVM) calculations. May represent labor rate per hour, material cost per unit, or equipment rate per day.
reference: Construction Cost Estimating Standards; EVM Baseline Guidelines

description: Code indicating the tax treatment for costs posted to this cost code (e.g., 'TAXABLE', 'EXEMPT', 'ZERO_RATED'). Used for Value Added Tax (VAT) and sales tax compliance in multi-jurisdiction projects.
reference: Tax Compliance Standards; VAT/GST Regulations; IFRS/GAAP Tax Accounting

description: The standard unit of measure for quantity tracking associated with this cost code (e.g., 'hours' for labor, 'cubic yards' for concrete, 'linear feet' for piping, 'each' for equipment). Used for productivity analysis and Material Take-Off (MTO) reconciliation.
reference: Construction Industry Standard Units; SAP S/4HANA Materials Management UOM Master

description: Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether this cost code can be directly linked to Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) elements in Oracle Primavera P6 and SAP S/4HANA Project Systems for integrated schedule and cost management.
reference: WBS Integration Standards; Oracle Primavera P6 Cost Integration Guidelines

description: The user ID or name of the person who created this cost code record in the system. Used for audit trail and data governance.
reference: Data Governance Standards; Audit Trail Requirements

description: Unique identifier for the retention ledger entry. Primary key for the finance retention ledger.
Reference to the contract under which this retention is held or owed.
Reference to the billing period during which this retention was withheld or released.
Reference to the construction project to which this retention entry applies.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Retention ledger entries should reference the owning cost center for allocation and reporting.
Reference to the counterparty (client for receivable retention, subcontractor for payable retention).
Reference to the WBS element to which this retention entry is allocated for cost control and reporting.
The cost code associated with this retention entry for job costing and financial tracking.
The total accumulated retention balance held or owed as of this ledger entry date.
reference: IFRS 15 - Revenue from Contracts with Customers

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this retention ledger entry.
Indicates whether this retention entry is under dispute (True if disputed, False otherwise).
Description of the reason for retention dispute if the dispute flag is set.
The end date of the Defects Liability Period, after which final retention is typically released.
The start date of the Defects Liability Period, typically commencing at practical completion.
The fiscal period (month or quarter) within the fiscal year to which this retention transaction is allocated.
The fiscal year to which this retention transaction is allocated for financial reporting and budgeting.
The General Ledger account code to which this retention entry is posted for financial reporting.
The total invoice amount before retention is withheld.
reference: IFRS 15 - Revenue from Contracts with Customers

description: The invoice number or payment application reference associated with this retention withholding or release.
Additional notes or comments regarding this retention ledger entry, including special conditions or release approvals.
The payment certificate number issued by the engineer or client authorizing payment and retention withholding.
The date on which this retention entry was posted to the General Ledger for financial reporting.
The date on which the practical completion certificate was issued, triggering partial retention release and DLP commencement.
The timestamp when this retention ledger record was first created in the data platform.
The timestamp when this retention ledger record was last updated in the data platform.
Indicates whether a retention bond was provided in lieu of cash retention (True if bond provided, False otherwise).
Reference number of the retention bond if provided in lieu of cash retention.
The percentage of the invoice or payment amount withheld as retention (e.g., 5.00 for 5% retention).
The amount of retention released in this transaction (partial or full release).
The date on which retention was released to the contractor or subcontractor.
The type of retention release event: partial release, final release upon practical completion, DLP expiry release, or milestone-based release.
Current status of the retention balance: withheld, partially released, fully released, or disputed.
Indicates whether this is retention receivable (client owes contractor) or retention payable (contractor owes subcontractor).
The amount withheld as retention for this billing period or transaction.
The operational system from which this retention ledger entry originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Viewpoint Vista, Procore).
The unique identifier of this retention ledger entry in the source operational system.
The business date on which this retention ledger transaction occurred (withholding or release event).
Unique identifier for the financial guarantee record. Primary key.
Reference to the construction contract for which this guarantee is issued or received.
Reference to the construction project associated with this financial guarantee.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Financial guarantees are tied to a cost center for budgeting and exposure tracking.
Foreign key linking to hr.employee. Business justification: Guarantee issuance is recorded by a responsible employee; linking supports traceability and regulatory reporting.
Number of amendments or modifications made to the original financial guarantee terms.
Name of the party entitled to call or claim against the financial guarantee (typically the client or contractor).
Classification of the beneficiary party: client, contractor, subcontractor, supplier, or joint venture (JV).
Amount by which this guarantee reduces the organization's available bonding capacity with the surety.
Detailed description of the conditions under which the beneficiary may call or claim against the financial guarantee.
Type of call mechanism: on-demand (unconditional), conditional (requires proof of default), or documentary (requires specific documents).
Monetary amount claimed by the beneficiary against the financial guarantee, if a call was made.
Date on which the beneficiary called or claimed against the financial guarantee, if applicable.
Description of any collateral or security provided to the issuer (e.g., cash deposit, property lien, parent company indemnity).
Indicates whether the issuer required collateral or security to issue the guarantee (True) or not (False).
Indicates whether the financial guarantee meets all contractual and regulatory compliance requirements (True) or not (False).
Timestamp when the financial guarantee record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the face value amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
Date from which the financial guarantee becomes binding and enforceable.
Date on which the financial guarantee expires and is no longer enforceable, unless extended or called prior.
External reference identifier or document number from the source system or issuing institution.
The maximum monetary value of the financial guarantee that can be claimed by the beneficiary.
General ledger account code to which guarantee premium expenses or liabilities are posted.
Externally-known unique reference number assigned by the issuing bank or surety for this financial guarantee instrument.

Current lifecycle status of the financial guarantee: active (in force), expired (past expiry date), called (claim made), released (obligation discharged), cancelled (terminated early), or pending (awaiting issuance).
Classification of the financial guarantee instrument: performance bond, advance payment guarantee, retention bond, bid bond, parent company guarantee, or letter of credit.
Date on which the financial guarantee was issued and became effective.
Name of the bank, surety company, or parent company that issued the financial guarantee.
Type of institution issuing the guarantee: bank, surety company, parent company, or insurance company.
Date of the most recent amendment or modification to the financial guarantee.
User or system identifier of the person who last modified the financial guarantee record.
Timestamp when the financial guarantee record was last modified in the system.
Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the financial guarantee.
Total premium or fee paid to the issuer for providing the financial guarantee.
Frequency at which premium payments are made: one-time, annual, semi-annual, quarterly, or monthly.
Annual premium rate expressed as a percentage of the face value amount.
Date on which the financial guarantee was formally released and the obligation discharged.
Current financial risk exposure represented by this guarantee, used for financial risk reporting and management.
Code identifying the operational system of record from which this financial guarantee data originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Viewpoint Vista).
Work Breakdown Structure element code linking the guarantee to a specific project phase or deliverable.
User or system identifier of the person who created the financial guarantee record.
Primary key for gl_account
Reference to the chart of accounts framework to which this account belongs (e.g., 'Corporate COA', 'IFRS COA', 'Local Statutory COA').
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Chart of Accounts

description: Reference to the company code (legal entity) to which this account belongs. Supports multi-entity chart of accounts.
High-level classification indicating whether the account appears on the balance sheet, Profit and Loss (P&L) statement, or is a statistical (non-financial) account.
Grouping code used to aggregate accounts for reporting and analysis (e.g., 'Direct Construction Costs', 'Overhead', 'Operating Expenses', 'Current Assets').
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Account Group, IFRS/GAAP

description: The full descriptive name of the account (e.g., 'Construction Materials - Direct Costs', 'Accounts Payable - Trade', 'Revenue - EPC Contracts').
The externally-known unique account number in the chart of accounts. Typically a 4-10 digit numeric code used in all financial postings and reports.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Chart of Accounts, IFRS/GAAP

description: Abbreviated name or label for the account used in condensed reports and user interfaces.
Current lifecycle status of the account: active (in use), blocked (no new postings), closed (archived), or pending approval (awaiting activation).
The fundamental financial statement category: asset, liability, equity, revenue, or expense. Determines balance sheet vs. Profit and Loss (P&L) classification.
Alternative or legacy account number used for cross-reference, migration mapping, or external reporting (e.g., statutory chart of accounts).
Detailed balance sheet line item classification (e.g., 'Current Assets - Cash', 'Non-Current Liabilities - Long-Term Debt', 'Equity - Retained Earnings'). Null for P&L accounts.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements, GAAP

description: Classification for cash flow statement reporting: operating activities, investing activities, financing activities, or not applicable for non-cash accounts.
reference: IFRS IAS 7 Statement of Cash Flows, GAAP ASC 230

description: The corresponding account number in the group consolidation chart of accounts. Used for mapping local entity accounts to group reporting structure.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements, SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting

description: Flag indicating whether a cost center must be specified when posting to this account. Enforces cost allocation discipline.
Classification of the cost element: primary (direct posting from FI), secondary (internal allocation), revenue, or not applicable if not a cost element.
Flag indicating whether this account is also a cost element in the controlling (CO) module, enabling integration with project costing and Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) budgets.
Date and time when this account master record was first created in the system.
Indicates the currency perspective for this account: local (entity currency), group (consolidation currency), transaction (original currency), or project (project-specific currency).
reference: IFRS IAS 21 The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates, SAP S/4HANA Currency Management

description: Configuration key that controls which fields are required, optional, or suppressed when posting to this account (e.g., cost center, WBS, profit center).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Field Status Variant

description: Business function or division associated with the account (e.g., 'Project Operations', 'Corporate Overhead', 'Procurement', 'MEP Services'). Used for segment reporting.
reference: IFRS 8 Operating Segments, SAP S/4HANA Functional Area

description: Flag indicating whether individual line items (journal entries) are stored and can be displayed for this account. Required for detailed transaction analysis.
User ID or name of the person who last modified this account master record.
Date and time when this account master record was last modified.
Flag indicating whether open item management is active for this account (e.g., for clearing transactions like AP/AR, advances, or accruals).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Open Item Management

description: Indicates the level at which budgeting and planning are performed for this account (e.g., 'Corporate', 'Division', 'Project', 'Cost Center').
Flag indicating whether direct postings are allowed to this account. False for summary/control accounts that only receive automatic postings.

Detailed Profit and Loss (P&L) line item classification (e.g., 'Revenue - Construction Contracts', 'Cost of Sales - Direct Labor', 'Operating Expenses - General & Administrative'). Null for balance sheet accounts.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements, GAAP

description: Flag indicating whether a profit center must be specified when posting to this account. Supports segment profitability analysis.
Flag indicating whether this is a reconciliation account (subledger control account) for Accounts Payable (AP), Accounts Receivable (AR), or asset accounting.
Flag indicating whether this is the retained earnings account used for year-end profit/loss transfer and equity rollforward.
reference: IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements, GAAP

description: Default sort key used for line item display and reporting (e.g., posting date, document number, reference). Controls how transactions are ordered.
Tax classification code indicating how this account is treated for tax purposes (e.g., 'Input Tax', 'Output Tax', 'Non-Taxable', 'Exempt'). Used for VAT/GST reporting.
reference: Local Tax Regulations, SAP S/4HANA Tax Configuration

description: Tolerance group code that defines permissible payment differences, exchange rate variances, and rounding tolerances for this account.
Flag indicating whether a trading partner (intercompany entity) must be specified when posting to this account. Used for intercompany eliminations.
reference: IFRS 10 Consolidated Financial Statements, SAP S/4HANA Intercompany Accounting

description: The date from which this account is valid and available for posting. Supports time-dependent chart of accounts changes.
The date until which this account is valid. Null for accounts with no planned end date. Used for account retirement and chart of accounts evolution.

business_glossary_term: Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) Element Required Indicator
description: Flag indicating whether a Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) element must be specified when posting to this account. Ensures project-level cost tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS-Project System, PMI PMBOK WBS Standards

description: User ID or name of the person who created this account master record in the system.
Unique identifier for the journal entry document. Primary key for the journal entry record.
Foreign key reference to the construction project to which this journal entry is allocated. Enables project-level P&L and EBITDA reporting.

Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Journal entry posts to a specific GL account; linking to gl_account master eliminates code duplication and enables consistent account attributes.
Free-form assignment field used for additional sorting, grouping, or reconciliation purposes (e.g., invoice number, payment reference).
The business area or division for cross-company code reporting and segment analysis.
The legal entity or company code to which this journal entry belongs. Used for consolidation and statutory reporting.
The cost center to which costs or revenues are allocated. Used for internal management reporting and cost control.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Controlling (CO) Cost Center Accounting

description: Construction-specific cost code for detailed job costing. Categorizes costs by type of work (e.g., labor, materials, equipment, subcontractor).
The date and time when this journal entry record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and compliance.
The credit amount for this journal entry line in the document currency. Zero if this is a debit entry.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the journal entry amounts are denominated.
The debit amount for this journal entry line in the document currency. Zero if this is a credit entry.
The date on the source document or invoice that originated this journal entry. May differ from posting date for period-end adjustments.
The externally-known unique document number assigned to this journal entry in the general ledger system. Used for audit trail and reference in financial reports.
Classification of the journal entry document type. SA=Standard Accrual, AA=Asset Accounting, KR=Vendor Invoice, DR=Customer Invoice, AB=Accounting Document, RV=Reversal Document.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Document Type Configuration

description: Current lifecycle status of the journal entry. Draft=not yet posted, Posted=finalized in GL, Parked=saved but not posted, Reversed=reversed by another entry, Cancelled=voided.
The foreign exchange rate used to convert document currency amounts to local currency. Applicable for multi-currency transactions.
The type of exchange rate used for currency conversion. Spot=transaction date rate, Average=period average, Budget=budgeted rate, Closing=period-end rate.
The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year to which this journal entry is assigned. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods, or 13-16 for special closing periods.
The fiscal year to which this journal entry is assigned for financial reporting purposes.
Free-form descriptive text at the document header level explaining the purpose or context of the journal entry.
Flag indicating whether this journal entry represents an intercompany transaction requiring elimination during consolidation.
Free-form descriptive text at the line item level providing additional context for this specific posting.
The credit amount converted to the local currency of the company code for statutory reporting.
The debit amount converted to the local currency of the company code for statutory reporting.
The user ID or name of the person who last modified this journal entry document.
The date and time when this journal entry record was last modified in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
The percentage of completion used for revenue recognition entries under IFRS 15. Represents the stage of project completion at the time of this journal entry.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers

description: The user ID or name of the person who posted this journal entry to the general ledger, finalizing the transaction.
The date and time when this journal entry was posted to the general ledger. Represents the moment the entry became final and immutable.
The date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger. This is the principal business event timestamp for financial period assignment and reporting.
Two-digit code that controls the type of posting (debit or credit) and the account type (GL, customer, vendor, asset). Standard SAP posting keys include 40=GL Debit, 50=GL Credit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Posting Key Configuration

description: The profit center to which revenues and costs are allocated for segment reporting and profitability analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Controlling (CO) Profit Center Accounting

description: External reference number or identifier linking this journal entry to source documents such as invoices, purchase orders, or contracts.

The method used to calculate percentage of completion for revenue recognition. Cost-to-cost=based on costs incurred, Units-delivered=based on deliverables, Milestones=based on project milestones, Time-elapsed=based on time.
reference: IFRS 15 Revenue from Contracts with Customers

description: Flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal of a previous entry. True if this is a reversal document.
Code indicating the reason for reversal. 01=Error Correction, 02=Period-End Adjustment, 03=Accrual Reversal, 04=Reclassification, 05=Other.
The document number of the original journal entry that this entry reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is true.
The calculated tax amount for this journal entry line in the document currency.
The tax code applied to this journal entry line, determining the tax rate and tax account for automatic tax calculation.
The trading partner company code for intercompany transactions, used for consolidation and elimination entries.
The WBS element code linking this journal entry to a specific project phase or deliverable. Enables project-level financial tracking and EVM integration.
The user ID or name of the person who created this journal entry document in the system.
Unique identifier for the journal entry line item. Primary key for this entity representing one leg of a double-entry accounting transaction.
Reference to the client or customer associated with this line item, if applicable. Used for accounts receivable reconciliation and revenue recognition.
Reference to the construction project to which this financial transaction is allocated. Links financial data to project master data for job costing and project P&L.
reference: Oracle Primavera P6, SAP S/4HANA Project Systems

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Journal entry lines need to be allocated to a cost center; replace the existing cost_center_code string with a proper foreign key.
The system user ID of the person who posted this journal entry line. Provides audit trail for financial transaction accountability.
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Journal entry line items reference GL accounts; FK provides authoritative account details.
Reference to the parent journal entry header that contains this line item. Links this line to the overall transaction batch and posting context.
Reference to the vendor or subcontractor associated with this line item, if applicable. Used for accounts payable reconciliation and vendor spend analysis.
The monetary amount of this journal entry line in the local reporting currency of the entity. Used for financial statement preparation and local statutory reporting.
The monetary amount of this journal entry line in the original transaction currency, before any currency conversion. Enables multi-currency accounting and foreign exchange analysis.
Additional reference field used for payment allocation, clearing, and reconciliation. Often contains check numbers, wire transfer references, or clearing document numbers.
The base amount before tax and adjustments, used for payment terms calculation and cash discount determination.
The business area or division to which this line item is allocated. Supports segment reporting and divisional P&L analysis.
reference: IFRS 8 Operating Segments, SAP S/4HANA Business Area Accounting

description: The date on which this line item was cleared (matched with a payment or offsetting entry). Null if the item remains open.
The document number of the clearing transaction that closed this line item. Used for payment reconciliation and audit trail.
Construction-specific cost code classifying the nature of the expense (e.g., labor, materials, equipment, subcontractor). Used for job costing and BOQ variance analysis.
reference: Viewpoint Vista Job Costing, Construction Industry Cost Coding Standards

description: Indicates whether this line is a debit (D) or credit (C) entry in the double-entry accounting system.
The date on the source document (invoice, receipt, contract) that originated this journal entry line. May differ from posting date due to processing lag.
The payment due date for this line item, calculated based on payment terms and document date. Critical for cash flow management and aging reports.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL Due Date Calculation

description: The system timestamp when this journal entry line was created in the ERP system. Used for audit trail and transaction sequencing.
The foreign exchange rate applied to convert transaction currency to local currency. Critical for multi-currency consolidation and FX gain/loss calculation.

business_glossary_term: Actual Cost of Work Performed (ACWP) / Actual Cost (AC)
description: The actual costs incurred for the work performed during the reporting period. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA financial actuals.
The date on which this EVM record was approved by the project controls manager or PMO. Null if not yet approved.
The name or identifier of the project controls manager or PMO representative who approved this EVM record.
The total authorized budget for the WBS element or cost account. Represents the baseline budget against which performance is measured.

business_glossary_term: Budgeted Cost of Work Performed (BCWP) / Earned Value (EV)
description: The budgeted cost of work that has actually been completed as of the data date. Represents the value of work earned based on physical progress.

business_glossary_term: Budgeted Cost of Work Scheduled (BCWS) / Planned Value (PV)
description: The authorized budget assigned to scheduled work for this WBS element and reporting period. Represents the planned value of work that should have been completed by the data date.
Free-text comments or notes from the cost engineer or project controls team regarding this EVM record. May include explanations of variances, forecast assumptions, or corrective actions.
The name of the cost engineer responsible for providing forecast inputs (ETC, remaining quantities, and rates) for this EVM record.
The ratio of earned value to actual cost (BCWP / ACWP). A CPI less than 1.0 indicates cost overrun; greater than 1.0 indicates cost underrun. Key metric for cost efficiency.
The difference between earned value and actual cost (BCWP - ACWP). A negative value indicates cost overrun; a positive value indicates cost underrun.
The timestamp when this EVM record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this EVM record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).
The as-of date for this EVM snapshot. Represents the point in time at which progress and cost data were captured for analysis.
The method used to calculate the EAC for this record. Options include CPI-based projection, SPI/CPI composite, bottom-up cost engineer forecast, management estimate, or atypical variance adjustment.
The forecasted total cost of the WBS element or cost account at completion. Calculated using CPI-based or cost engineer forecast methods.
The forecasted cost required to complete the remaining work from the data date. Derived from cost engineer inputs for remaining quantities and rates.
The cost engineer's confidence level in the ETC and EAC forecast for this record. Indicates the reliability of the cost-to-complete estimate.
The timestamp when this EVM record was last modified. Audit trail for record updates.
The percentage of work completed for this WBS element or cost account as of the data date. Calculated as (BCWP / BAC) * 100.
The percentage of budget spent as of the data date. Calculated as (ACWP / BAC) * 100.
The current lifecycle status of this EVM record. Indicates whether the record is in draft, submitted for review, approved, revised, or closed.
The quantity of work remaining to be completed for this cost account. Input by cost engineers for ETC calculation. Units vary by work type (e.g., cubic meters, linear meters, hours).
The forecasted unit rate for remaining work. Input by cost engineers to calculate ETC. Represents cost per unit of work (e.g., cost per cubic meter, cost per hour).
The end date of the reporting period for which this EVM snapshot was computed. Defines the close of the measurement window.
The start date of the reporting period for which this EVM snapshot was computed. Defines the beginning of the measurement window.

business_glossary_term: SAP Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) Element Code
description: The SAP S/4HANA WBS element code from which actual cost data (ACWP) was sourced. Enables traceability to the source ERP system.
The ratio of earned value to planned value (BCWP / BCWS). An SPI less than 1.0 indicates the project is behind schedule; greater than 1.0 indicates ahead of schedule.
The difference between earned value and planned value (BCWP - BCWS). A negative value indicates the project is behind schedule; a positive value indicates ahead of schedule.
The cost performance index required to achieve the budget at completion or estimate at completion. Calculated as (BAC - BCWP) / (BAC - ACWP) or (BAC - BCWP) / (EAC - ACWP).
The difference between the budget at completion and the estimate at completion (BAC - EAC). Indicates the expected cost overrun or underrun at project completion.
